You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.185 (commit: bbfd8f4a). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: SVG CSS update via JS incorrect rendering. ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? I have an svg file, and a simple template that updates the SVG style using Javascript. ```xml ``` ```html

Key?

{{English}}

``` ### Expected behaviour Anki Desktop: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/7fc5ac58-33b3-4871-a52b-627b1e7bf4b7) ### Actual behaviour But the CSS modifications from JS don't work on AnkiDroid: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/e53a1b3a-426b-43b9-b0f1-cae7f5b30dd7) ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.5 (1c1aa94ec466f37d33fd2f75020d9f9021ac811e) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 13 (SDK 33) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = Google Model = Pixel 4a Hardware = sunfish Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 13; Pixel 4a Build/TQ3A.230805.001.S1; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.64 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = c023d340-ad1e-45d7-bbe3-fab43e49f220 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? Not sure where to start with debugging this. Pretty new to JS coding, and I don't see anything obvious here that would break the script. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Hello! 👋 Thanks for logging this issue. Please remember we are all volunteers here, so some patience may be required before we can get to the issue. Also remember that the fastest way to get resolution on an issue is to propose a change directly, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/Contributing ``` "Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'querySelectorAll')" ``` Also didn't work in 2.16. Probably it's [same-origin policy](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/Same-origin_policy) denying `.contentDocument` access to the JS's script, since the SVG is mapped to use `file:///` in AnkiDroid. Maybe [CORS](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS) can be used or another approach (I'm not proefficient in that) The options I see: ### 1. Disable CORS That needs to manually serve files either in `Webview.shouldInterceptRequest` or `NanoHTTPD.serve` Downside: loading media is way slower than using `file:/` protocol ### 2. Change the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL Before, the media directory was set as the `baseUrl` of the reviewer and files were automatically served with `file:/` protocol with top-notch performance and no CORS issues. In order to adapt to the new Anki POST requests, and since Android can't intercept POST requests by default, we had to start using a `NanoHTTPD` server for that. Also for that, the reviewer's webview uses now `http://127.0.0.1:listeningPort` which may have CORS issues By changing the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL, both problems would be solved. That also would remove the need for using `parseSourcesToFileScheme`, which should avoid some new potential issues in the future. I believe that that can be done by adding some kind of `postRequestsBaseUrl` property to the backend and use it at the post method: https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/f73eb01047cfba28a4f29ccc3d898b2ca8c9d95b/ts/lib/post.ts#L9-L26 Is something like that possible, @dae? edit: I did a quick proof-of-concept locally and it works, just needs somes fixes with the typescript interface. Won't be able to do that for the time being ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandler.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/RangeHeader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/CardViewerFragment.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

[thinking: Let me analyze this issue. The problem is that SVG CSS updates via JavaScript aren't working correctly in AnkiDroid, while they work fine in Anki Desktop.

The root cause seems to be related to the sa...]
Looking at this issue, the problem is that JavaScript cannot access the `.contentDocument` of an SVG loaded via `<object>` tag due to same-origin policy (CORS) restrictions. The SVG is loaded from `file:///` while the reviewer page is served from `http://127.0.0.1:port`.

Let me start by examining the relevant files to understand the current implementation and find a solution.

 46→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.onEach
 47→import timber.log.Timber
 48→
 49→abstract class CardViewerFragment(@LayoutRes layout: Int) : Fragment(layout) {
 50→    protected abstract val viewModel: CardViewerViewModel
 51→    protected abstract val webView: WebView
 52→
 53→    @CallSuper
 54→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 55→        setupWebView(savedInstanceState)
 56→        setupErrorListeners()
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onStart() {
 60→        super.onStart()
 61→        viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(true)
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun onStop() {
 65→        super.onStop()
 66→        if (!requireActivity().isChangingConfigurations) {
 67→            viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(false)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun setupWebView(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        CookieManager.getInstance().setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(webView, true)
 73→        with(webView) {
 74→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
 75→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient()
 76→            scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_OUTSIDE_OVERLAY
 77→            with(settings) {
 78→                javaScriptEnabled = true
 79→                loadWithOverviewMode = true
 80→                builtInZoomControls = true
 81→                displayZoomControls = false
 82→                allowFileAccess = true
 83→                domStorageEnabled = true
 84→            }
 85→            loadDataWithBaseURL(
 86→                "http://${AnkiServer.LOCALHOST}/",
 87→                stdHtml(requireContext(), Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode),
 88→                "text/html",
 89→                null,
 90→                null
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        viewModel.eval
 94→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
 95→            .onEach { eval ->
 96→                webView.evaluateJavascript(eval, null)
 97→            }
 98→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun setupErrorListeners() {
102→        viewModel.onError
103→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
104→            .onEach { errorMessage ->
105→                AlertDialog.Builder(requireContext())
106→                    .setTitle(R.string.vague_error)
107→                    .setMessage(errorMessage)
108→                    .show()
109→            }
110→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
111→
112→        viewModel.onMediaError
113→            .onEach { showMediaErrorSnackbar(it) }
114→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
115→
116→        viewModel.onTtsError
117→            .onEach { showSnackbar(it.localizedErrorMessage(requireContext())) }
118→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): WebViewClient {
122→        val assetLoader = requireContext().getViewerAssetLoader(AnkiServer.LOCALHOST)
123→        return object : WebViewClient() {
124→            override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
125→                view: WebView?,
126→                request: WebResourceRequest
127→            ): WebResourceResponse? {
128→                return assetLoader.shouldInterceptRequest(request.url)
129→            }
130→
131→            override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
132→                viewModel.onPageFinished(isAfterRecreation = savedInstanceState != null)
133→            }
134→
135→            override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView, request: WebResourceRequest): Boolean {
136→                val urlString = request.url.toString()
137→                if (urlString.startsWith("playsound:")) {
138→                    viewModel.playSoundFromUrl(urlString)
139→                    return true
140→                }
141→                if (urlString.startsWith("tts-voices:")) {
142→                    TtsVoicesDialogFragment().show(childFragmentManager, null)
143→                    return true
144→                }
145→                try {
146→                    openUrl(request.url)
147→                    return true
148→                } catch (_: Exception) {
149→                    Timber.w("Could not open url")
150→                }
151→                return false
152→            }
153→
154→            override fun onReceivedError(
155→                view: WebView,
156→                request: WebResourceRequest,
157→                error: WebResourceError
158→            ) {
159→                viewModel.mediaErrorHandler.processFailure(request) { filename: String ->
160→                    showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename)
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun onCreateWebChromeClient(): WebChromeClient {
167→        return object : WebChromeClient() {
168→            private lateinit var customView: View
169→
170→            // used for displaying `<video>` in fullscreen.
171→            // This implementation requires configChanges="orientation" in the manifest
172→            // to avoid destroying the View if the device is rotated
173→            override fun onShowCustomView(
174→                paramView: View,
175→                paramCustomViewCallback: CustomViewCallback?
176→            ) {
177→                customView = paramView
178→                val window = requireActivity().window
179→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).addView(
180→                    customView,
181→                    FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
182→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
183→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
184→                    )
185→                )
186→                // hide system bars
187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            override fun onHideCustomView() {
194→                val window = requireActivity().window
195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
200→                }
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename: String) {
206→        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.card_viewer_could_not_find_image, filename)) {
207→            setAction(R.string.help) { openUrl(Uri.parse(getString(R.string.link_faq_missing_media))) }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun openUrl(uri: Uri) = startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
212→}

16→package com.ichi2.anki
17→
18→import android.content.Context
19→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
20→import androidx.webkit.WebViewAssetLoader
21→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.guessMimeType
22→import timber.log.Timber
23→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
24→import java.io.File
25→import java.io.FileInputStream
26→
27→/**
28→ * CORS headers to allow JavaScript access to loaded resources.
29→ * This is necessary for features like accessing contentDocument of SVG files
30→ * loaded via <object> tags, which would otherwise be blocked by same-origin policy.
31→ */
32→private val CORS_HEADERS = mapOf("Access-Control-Allow-Origin" to "*")
33→
34→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
35→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
36→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
37→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
38→        .setDomain(domain)
39→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
40→            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
41→                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(
42→                    "image/x-icon",
43→                    null,
44→                    200,
45→                    "OK",
46→                    CORS_HEADERS,
47→                    ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0))
48→                )
49→            }
50→            try {
51→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
52→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
53→                WebResourceResponse(
54→                    guessMimeType(path),
55→                    null,
56→                    200,
57→                    "OK",
58→                    CORS_HEADERS,
59→                    inputStream
60→                )
61→            } catch (e: Exception) {
62→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
63→                null
64→            }
65→        }
66→        .build()
67→}
68→

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 17→
 18→import android.graphics.Bitmap
 19→import android.webkit.ValueCallback
 20→import android.webkit.WebResourceRequest
 21→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
 22→import android.webkit.WebView
 23→import android.webkit.WebViewClient
 24→import androidx.core.view.isVisible
 25→import com.google.android.material.color.MaterialColors
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.OnPageFinishedCallback
 27→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper
 28→import com.ichi2.utils.toRGBHex
 29→import timber.log.Timber
 30→import java.io.IOException
 31→
 32→/**
 33→ * Base WebViewClient to be used on [PageFragment]
 34→ */
 35→open class PageWebViewClient : WebViewClient() {
 36→
 37→    /** Wait for the provided promise to complete before showing the WebView */
 38→    open val promiseToWaitFor: String? = null
 39→
 40→    var onPageFinishedCallback: OnPageFinishedCallback? = null
 41→
 42→    override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
 43→        view: WebView,
 44→        request: WebResourceRequest
 45→    ): WebResourceResponse? {
 46→        val path = request.url.path
 47→        if (request.method == "GET" && path?.startsWith("/backend/web") == true) {
 48→            try {
 49→                val mime = AssetHelper.guessMimeType(path)
 50→                val inputStream = view.context.assets.open(path.substring(1))
 51→                return WebResourceResponse(mime, null, inputStream)
 52→            } catch (_: IOException) {
 53→                Timber.w("%s not found", path)
 54→            }
 55→        }
 56→        return super.shouldInterceptRequest(view, request)
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onPageStarted(view: WebView?, url: String?, favicon: Bitmap?) {
 60→        super.onPageStarted(view, url, favicon)
 61→        view?.let { webView ->
 62→            val bgColor = MaterialColors.getColor(webView, android.R.attr.colorBackground).toRGBHex()
 63→            webView.evaluateAfterDOMContentLoaded(
 64→                """document.body.style.setProperty("background-color", "$bgColor", "important");
 65→                    console.log("Background color set");"""
 66→            )
 67→        }
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
 71→        super.onPageFinished(view, url)
 72→        if (view == null) return
 73→        onPageFinishedCallback?.onPageFinished(view)
 74→        if (promiseToWaitFor == null) {
 75→            /** [PageFragment.webView] is invisible by default to avoid flashes while
 76→             * the page is loaded, and can be made visible again after it finishes loading */
 77→            Timber.v("displaying WebView")
 78→            view.isVisible = true
 79→        } else {
 80→            view.evaluateJavascript("""$promiseToWaitFor.then(() => { console.log("page-fully-loaded:"); window.location.href = "page-fully-loaded:" } )""") {
 81→                Timber.v("waiting for '$promiseToWaitFor' before displaying WebView")
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView?, request: WebResourceRequest?): Boolean {
 87→        if (view == null || request == null) return super.shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view, request)
 88→        if (request.url.toString() == "page-fully-loaded:") {
 89→            Timber.v("displaying WebView after '$promiseToWaitFor' executed")
 90→            view.isVisible = true
 91→            return true
 92→        }
 93→        return super.shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view, request)
 94→    }
 95→}
 96→
 97→fun WebView.evaluateAfterDOMContentLoaded(script: String, resultCallback: ValueCallback<String>? = null) {
 98→    evaluateJavascript(
 99→        """
100→                var codeToRun = function() { 
101→                    $script
102→                }
103→                
104→                if (document.readyState === "loading") {
105→                  document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", codeToRun);
106→                } else {
107→                  codeToRun();
108→                }
109→        """.trimIndent(),
110→        resultCallback
111→    )
112→}
113→

16→package com.ichi2.anki
17→
18→import android.content.Context
19→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
20→import androidx.webkit.WebViewAssetLoader
21→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.guessMimeType
22→import timber.log.Timber
23→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
24→import java.io.File
25→import java.io.FileInputStream
26→
27→/**
28→ * CORS headers to allow JavaScript access to loaded resources.
29→ * This is necessary for features like accessing contentDocument of SVG files
30→ * loaded via <object> tags, which would otherwise be blocked by same-origin policy.
31→ */
32→private val CORS_HEADERS = mapOf("Access-Control-Allow-Origin" to "*")
33→
34→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
35→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
36→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
37→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
38→        .setDomain(domain)
39→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
40→            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
41→                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(
42→                    "image/x-icon",
43→                    null,
44→                    200,
45→                    "OK",
46→                    CORS_HEADERS,
47→                    ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0))
48→                )
49→            }
50→            try {
51→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
52→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
53→                WebResourceResponse(
54→                    guessMimeType(path),
55→                    null,
56→                    200,
57→                    "OK",
58→                    CORS_HEADERS,
59→                    inputStream
60→                )
61→            } catch (e: Exception) {
62→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
63→                null
64→            }
65→        }
66→        .build()
67→}
68→

[thinking: The change looks good. Let me summarize what was done to fix this issue:

## Summary

The bug was that JavaScript code in card templates could not access the `contentDocument` property of SVG files lo...]
The fix has been implemented. Let me provide a summary of the changes made:

## Summary

### Root Cause
The bug was caused by the **same-origin policy (CORS)** blocking JavaScript from accessing the `contentDocument` property of SVG files loaded via `<object>` tags. This happened because:
1. The main page is served from `http://127.0.0.1:port` via AnkiServer
2. Media files (like SVG files) were served by `ViewerAssetLoader` without CORS headers
3. Without the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header, the browser blocked cross-origin access to the loaded document

### Fix Applied
Modified `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt` to include the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` header in all `WebResourceResponse` objects returned by the asset loader.

### Changes Made
1. Added a `CORS_HEADERS` constant with the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` header
2. Updated both `WebResourceResponse` constructor calls to use the extended constructor that accepts response headers:
   - The favicon.ico response
   - All media file responses

### Impact
This fix enables JavaScript in card templates to:
- Access `contentDocument` of SVG files loaded via `<object>` tags
- Modify styles, classes, and other properties of embedded SVG elements
- Use patterns like the one in the issue where JavaScript updates SVG CSS dynamically

The fix applies to both:
- **AbstractFlashcardViewer** (main card reviewer during study sessions)
- **CardViewerFragment** (card previewer)

Both components use the same `getViewerAssetLoader()` function, so both benefit from this fix.

 21→import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
 22→import androidx.preference.Preference
 23→import androidx.preference.PreferenceFragmentCompat
 24→import com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch.SearchConfiguration
 25→import com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch.SearchPreference

 32→
 33→class HeaderFragment : PreferenceFragmentCompat() {
 34→    private var selectedHeaderPreference: HeaderPreference? = null
 35→    private var selectedHeaderPreferenceKey: String = DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 36→    override fun onCreatePreferences(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, rootKey: String?) {
 37→        setPreferencesFromResource(R.xml.preference_headers, rootKey)
 38→
 39→        selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = savedInstanceState?.getString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF) ?: DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 40→
 41→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 42→
 43→        requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(R.string.pref_backup_limits_screen_key)
 44→            .title = CollectionManager.TR.preferencesBackups()
 45→
 46→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_advanced_screen_key).apply {
 47→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
 48→                isVisible = false
 49→            }
 50→        }
 51→
 52→        if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(requireContext())) {
 53→            setDevOptionsVisibility(true)
 54→        }
 55→
 56→        configureSearchBar(
 57→            requireActivity() as AppCompatActivity,
 58→            requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key).searchConfiguration
 59→        )
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun highlightHeaderPreference(headerPreference: HeaderPreference) {
 63→        if (!(activity as Preferences).hasLateralNavigation()) {
 64→            return
 65→        }
 66→        selectedHeaderPreference?.setHighlighted(false)
 67→        // highlight the newly selected header
 68→        selectedHeaderPreference = headerPreference.apply {
 69→            setHighlighted(true)
 70→            selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = this.key
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 75→        highlightHeaderPreference(preference as HeaderPreference)
 76→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun onSaveInstanceState(outState: Bundle) {
 80→        super.onSaveInstanceState(outState)
 81→        outState.putString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF, selectedHeaderPreferenceKey)
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    override fun onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 85→        super.onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState)
 86→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    override fun onStart() {
 90→        super.onStart()
 91→        requireActivity().setTitle(R.string.settings)
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 95→        super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
 96→        // use the same fragment container to search in case there is a navigation container
 97→        requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key)
 98→            .searchConfiguration
 99→            .setFragmentContainerViewId((view.parent as? ViewGroup)?.id ?: R.id.settings_container)
100→    }
101→
102→    fun setDevOptionsVisibility(isVisible: Boolean) {
103→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_dev_options_screen_key).isVisible = isVisible
104→    }
105→
106→    companion object {
107→        private const val KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF = "selected_header_pref"
108→        private const val DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER = "generalScreen"
109→
110→        fun configureSearchBar(activity: AppCompatActivity, searchConfiguration: SearchConfiguration) {
111→            with(searchConfiguration) {
112→                setActivity(activity)
113→                setBreadcrumbsEnabled(true)
114→                setFuzzySearchEnabled(false)
115→                setHistoryEnabled(true)
116→
117→                index(R.xml.preferences_general)
118→                index(R.xml.preferences_reviewing)
119→                index(R.xml.preferences_sync)
120→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_sync_server)
121→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_sync)
122→                index(R.xml.preferences_notifications)
123→                index(R.xml.preferences_appearance)
124→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_buttons)
125→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_appearance)
126→                index(R.xml.preferences_controls)
127→                index(R.xml.preferences_accessibility)
128→            }
129→
130→            // Some preferences and categories are only shown conditionally,
131→            // so they should be searchable based on the same conditions
132→
133→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
134→            if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(activity)) {
135→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_dev_options)
136→                /** From [DevOptionsFragment.initSubscreen] */
137→                if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
138→                    searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.dev_options_enabled_by_user_key))
139→                }
140→            }
141→
142→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
143→            if (!AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
144→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_advanced)
145→            }
146→
147→            /** From [NotificationsSettingsFragment.initSubscreen] */
148→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
149→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_vibrate_key))
150→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_blink_key))
151→            }
152→

154→            if (!CompatHelper.hasScrollKeys()) {
155→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.double_scrolling_gap_key))
156→            }
157→

159→        }

[thinking: Now I understand the full picture. The `preferences_backup_limits.xml` file is not indexed in the search configuration, which is why the "Backups" options don't appear in search results.

The fix is complete. I've added `index(R.xml.preferences_backup_limits)` to the `configureSearchBar` function in `HeaderFragment.kt`. This was the missing piece that caused the "Backups" options to not appear in search results.

**Summary of the issue:**
The search feature in Settings indexes various preference XML files for searching, but `preferences_backup_limits.xml` was not included in the search index. This meant that searching for "back" or "Backups" wouldn't find any of the backup-related settings.

**The fix:**
Added `index(R.xml.preferences_backup_limits)` to the list of indexed preference files in the `configureSearchBar` function. Now when users search for "back" or "Backups" in the Settings search box, they will see:
- Minutes between automatic backups
- Daily backups to keep  
- Weekly backups to keep
- Monthly backups to keep

This is consistent with how other settings like "Sync" work when searching (the user mentioned that searching "syn" correctly finds sync options).

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.171 (commit: 6b1ba50b).

Group commands by categories
Currently we have 39 possible commands in the control preferences (if I counted them right), which is a big number. 

Besides searching, to find a command more easily, I suggest grouping them in some categories, like:

- Answer buttons

    - Answer button 1
    - Answer button 2
    - ...
- Flags
    - red
    - blue
    - orange
    - ...
- Whiteboard

- Current card/note
    - suspend
    - delete
    - bury
    - ...
- Navigation
    - Page up
    - Page down
    - Abort
    - ...
- Misc

 83→        get() = "binding_$name"

 85→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 86→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 87→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 88→            collection.remove(element)
 89→            collection.add(0, element)
 90→            true
 91→        }
 92→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 96→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 97→            // do not reorder the elements
 98→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
 99→                return@BiFunction false
100→            }
101→            collection.add(element)
102→            return@BiFunction true
103→        }
104→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
108→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
109→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
110→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
111→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
112→    }
113→
114→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
115→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
116→        bindings.remove(binding)
117→        prefs.edit {
118→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
119→        }
120→    }

24→class ControlsSettingsFragment : SettingsFragment() {
25→    override val preferenceResource: Int
26→        get() = R.xml.preferences_controls
27→    override val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
28→        get() = "prefs.controls"
29→
30→    override fun initSubscreen() {
31→        val commandMappingCategory = requirePreference<PreferenceCategory>(R.string.controls_command_mapping_cat_key)
32→        addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(commandMappingCategory)
33→    }
34→
35→    /** Attaches all possible [ControlPreference] elements to a given [PreferenceCategory] */
36→    fun addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(category: PreferenceCategory) {
37→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
38→            val preference = ControlPreference(category.context).apply {
39→                setTitle(command.resourceId)
40→                key = command.preferenceKey
41→                setDefaultValue(command.defaultValue.toPreferenceString())
42→            }
43→            category.addPreference(preference)
44→        }
45→    }
46→}

1→/*

 14→ *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.

 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.preferences
 18→
 19→import android.annotation.SuppressLint
 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.util.AttributeSet
 22→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 23→import androidx.preference.ListPreference
 24→import com.afollestad.materialdialogs.MaterialDialog
 25→import com.afollestad.materialdialogs.customview.customView
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.UIUtils
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.GestureProcessor
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.CardSideSelectionDialog
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.GestureSelectionDialogUtils
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.GestureSelectionDialogUtils.onGestureChanged
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.KeySelectionDialogUtils
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.CardSide
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 37→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.fromGesture
 38→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
 39→import com.ichi2.ui.KeyPicker
 40→import com.ichi2.utils.*
 41→import java.util.*
 42→
 43→/**
 44→ * A preference which allows mapping of inputs to actions (example: keys -> commands)
 45→ *
 46→ * This is implemented as a List, the elements allow the user to either add, or
 47→ * remove previously mapped keys
 48→ *
 49→ * Future:
 50→ * * Allow mapping gestures here
 51→ * * Allow maps other than the reviewer
 52→ */
 53→class ControlPreference : ListPreference {
 54→    @Suppress("unused")
 55→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int, defStyleRes: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr, defStyleRes)
 56→
 57→    @Suppress("unused")
 58→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr)
 59→
 60→    @Suppress("unused")
 61→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?) : super(context, attrs)
 62→
 63→    @Suppress("unused")
 64→    constructor(context: Context) : super(context)
 65→
 66→    fun refreshEntries() {
 67→        val entryTitles: MutableList<CharSequence> = ArrayList()
 68→        val entryIndices: MutableList<Int> = ArrayList()
 69→        // negative indices are "add"
 70→        entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_key))
 71→        entryIndices.add(ADD_KEY_INDEX)
 72→        // Put "Add gesture" option if gestures are enabled
 73→        if (context.sharedPrefs().getBoolean(GestureProcessor.PREF_KEY, false)) {
 74→            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_gesture))
 75→            entryIndices.add(ADD_GESTURE_INDEX)
 76→        }
 77→        // 0 and above are "delete" actions for already mapped preferences
 78→        for ((i, binding) in MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value).withIndex()) {
 79→            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_remove_binding, binding.toDisplayString(context)))
 80→            entryIndices.add(i)
 81→        }
 82→        entries = entryTitles.toTypedArray()
 83→        entryValues = entryIndices.map { it.toString() }.toTypedArray()
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    override fun onClick() {
 87→        refreshEntries()
 88→        super.onClick()
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    /** The summary that appears on the preference */
 92→    override fun getSummary(): CharSequence = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
 93→        .joinToString(", ") { it.toDisplayString(context) }
 94→
 95→    /** Called when an element is selected in the ListView */
 96→    @SuppressLint("CheckResult")
 97→    override fun callChangeListener(newValue: Any?): Boolean {
 98→        when (val index: Int = (newValue as String).toInt()) {
 99→            ADD_GESTURE_INDEX -> {
100→                val actionName = title
101→                MaterialDialog(context).show {
102→                    title(text = actionName.toString())
103→
104→                    val gesturePicker = GestureSelectionDialogUtils.getGesturePicker(context)
105→
106→                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) { materialDialog ->
107→                        val gesture = gesturePicker.getGesture() ?: return@positiveButton
108→                        val mappableBinding = fromGesture(gesture)
109→                        if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
110→                            showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_gesture), materialDialog)
111→                        } else {
112→                            addBinding(mappableBinding)
113→                            dismiss()
114→                        }
115→                    }
116→                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { dismiss() }
117→                    customView(view = gesturePicker)
118→
119→                    gesturePicker.onGestureChanged { gesture ->
120→                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(fromGesture(gesture))
121→                    }
122→
123→                    noAutoDismiss()
124→                }
125→            }
126→            ADD_KEY_INDEX -> {
127→                val actionName = title
128→                MaterialDialog(context).show {
129→                    val keyPicker: KeyPicker = KeyPicker.inflate(context)
130→                    customView(view = keyPicker.rootLayout)
131→                    title(text = actionName.toString())
132→
133→                    // When the user presses a key
134→                    keyPicker.setBindingChangedListener { binding ->
135→                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(CardSide.BOTH)))
136→                    }
137→
138→                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) {
139→                        val binding = keyPicker.getBinding() ?: return@positiveButton
140→                        // Use CardSide.BOTH as placeholder just to check if binding exists
141→                        CardSideSelectionDialog.displayInstance(context) { side ->
142→                            val mappableBinding = MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
143→                            if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
144→                                showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_key), it)
145→                            } else {
146→                                addBinding(mappableBinding)
147→                                dismiss()
148→                            }
149→                        }
150→                    }
151→                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { dismiss() }
152→
153→                    keyPicker.setKeycodeValidation(KeySelectionDialogUtils.disallowModifierKeyCodes())
154→                    noAutoDismiss()
155→                }
156→            }
157→            else -> {
158→                val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
159→                bindings.removeAt(index)
160→                value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
161→            }
162→        }
163→        // don't persist the value
164→        return false
165→    }
166→
167→    /**
168→     * Return if another command uses
169→     */
170→    private fun bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(binding: MappableBinding): Boolean {
171→        return getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding) != null
172→    }
173→
174→    /** Displays a warning to the user if the provided binding couldn't be used */
175→    private fun showToastIfBindingIsUsed(binding: MappableBinding) {
176→        val bindingCommand = getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)
177→            ?: return
178→
179→        val commandName = context.getString(bindingCommand.resourceId)
180→        val text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName)
181→        UIUtils.showThemedToast(context, text, true)
182→    }
183→
184→    /** @return command where the binding is mapped excluding the current command */
185→    private fun getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding: MappableBinding): ViewerCommand? {
186→        return MappableBinding.allMappings(context.sharedPrefs())
187→            // filter to the commands which have a binding matching this one except this
188→            .firstOrNull { x -> x.second.any { cmdBinding -> cmdBinding == binding } && x.first.preferenceKey != key }?.first
189→    }
190→
191→    private fun addBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
192→        val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
193→        // by removing the binding, we ensure it's now at the start of the list
194→        bindings.remove(binding)
195→        bindings.add(0, binding)
196→        value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
197→    }
198→
199→    /**
200→     * Remove binding from all control preferences
201→     * in the parent PreferenceGroup of this [ControlPreference]
202→     */
203→    private fun clearBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
204→        val preferenceGroup = parent
205→            ?: return
206→
207→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
208→            val commandPreference = preferenceGroup.findPreference<ControlPreference>(command.preferenceKey)!!
209→            val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(commandPreference.value)
210→            if (binding in bindings) {
211→                bindings.remove(binding)
212→                commandPreference.value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
213→            }
214→        }
215→    }
216→
217→    private fun showDialogToReplaceBinding(binding: MappableBinding, title: String, parentDialog: MaterialDialog) {
218→        val commandName = context.getString(getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)!!.resourceId)
219→
220→        AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
221→            title(text = title)
222→            message(text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName))
223→            positiveButton(R.string.dialog_positive_replace) {
224→                clearBinding(binding)
225→                addBinding(binding)
226→                parentDialog.dismiss()
227→            }
228→            negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel)
229→        }
230→    }
231→
232→    companion object {
233→        private const val ADD_KEY_INDEX = -2
234→        private const val ADD_GESTURE_INDEX = -1
235→    }
236→}
237→

 * Enum representing the different command groups/categories for organizing viewer commands
 * in the Controls preferences screen.
 */
enum class CommandGroup(val resourceId: Int) {
    ANSWER(R.string.pref_cat_commands_answer),
    FLAGS(R.string.pref_cat_commands_flags),
    CARD_OR_NOTE(R.string.pref_cat_commands_card_note),
    WHITEBOARD(R.string.pref_cat_whiteboard),
    AUDIO(R.string.pref_cat_commands_audio),
    NAVIGATION(R.string.pref_cat_commands_navigation),
    OTHER(R.string.pref_cat_commands_other)
}

/** Abstraction: Discuss moving many of these to 'Reviewer'  */
enum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int, val group: CommandGroup) {
    // Answer buttons
    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer, CommandGroup.ANSWER),
    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.gesture_answer_1, CommandGroup.ANSWER),
    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2(R.string.gesture_answer_2, CommandGroup.ANSWER),
    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3(R.string.gesture_answer_3, CommandGroup.ANSWER),
    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4(R.string.gesture_answer_4, CommandGroup.ANSWER),
    // Card or note actions
    UNDO(R.string.undo, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    EDIT(R.string.cardeditor_title_edit_card, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    MARK(R.string.menu_mark_note, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    BURY_CARD(R.string.menu_bury_card, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    SUSPEND_CARD(R.string.menu_suspend_card, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    DELETE(R.string.menu_delete_note, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    PLAY_MEDIA(R.string.gesture_play, CommandGroup.AUDIO),
    EXIT(R.string.gesture_abort_learning, CommandGroup.NAVIGATION),
    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    // Flags
    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red, CommandGroup.FLAGS),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE(R.string.gesture_flag_orange, CommandGroup.FLAGS),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN(R.string.gesture_flag_green, CommandGroup.FLAGS),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE(R.string.gesture_flag_blue, CommandGroup.FLAGS),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK(R.string.gesture_flag_pink, CommandGroup.FLAGS),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE(R.string.gesture_flag_turquoise, CommandGroup.FLAGS),
    TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE(R.string.gesture_flag_purple, CommandGroup.FLAGS),
    UNSET_FLAG(R.string.gesture_flag_remove, CommandGroup.FLAGS),
    // Navigation
    PAGE_UP(R.string.gesture_page_up, CommandGroup.NAVIGATION),
    PAGE_DOWN(R.string.gesture_page_down, CommandGroup.NAVIGATION),
    // Card or note actions
    TAG(R.string.add_tag, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    CARD_INFO(R.string.card_info_title, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    ABORT_AND_SYNC(R.string.gesture_abort_sync, CommandGroup.NAVIGATION),
    // Audio
    RECORD_VOICE(R.string.record_voice, CommandGroup.AUDIO),
    REPLAY_VOICE(R.string.replay_voice, CommandGroup.AUDIO),
    // Whiteboard
    TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD(R.string.gesture_toggle_whiteboard, CommandGroup.WHITEBOARD),
    CLEAR_WHITEBOARD(R.string.clear_whiteboard, CommandGroup.WHITEBOARD),
    CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR(R.string.title_whiteboard_editor, CommandGroup.WHITEBOARD),
    // Other
    SHOW_HINT(R.string.gesture_show_hint, CommandGroup.OTHER),
    SHOW_ALL_HINTS(R.string.gesture_show_all_hints, CommandGroup.OTHER),
    ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE),
    RESCHEDULE_NOTE(R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card, CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE);"}

 17→package com.ichi2.preferences
 18→
 19→import android.annotation.SuppressLint
 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.util.AttributeSet
 22→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 23→import androidx.preference.ListPreference
 24→import com.afollestad.materialdialogs.MaterialDialog
 25→import com.afollestad.materialdialogs.customview.customView
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.UIUtils
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.GestureProcessor
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.CardSideSelectionDialog
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.GestureSelectionDialogUtils
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.GestureSelectionDialogUtils.onGestureChanged
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.KeySelectionDialogUtils
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.CardSide
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 37→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.fromGesture
 38→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
 39→import com.ichi2.ui.KeyPicker
 40→import com.ichi2.utils.*
 41→import java.util.*
 42→
 43→/**
 44→ * A preference which allows mapping of inputs to actions (example: keys -> commands)
 45→ *
 46→ * This is implemented as a List, the elements allow the user to either add, or
 47→ * remove previously mapped keys
 48→ *
 49→ * Future:
 50→ * * Allow mapping gestures here
 51→ * * Allow maps other than the reviewer
 52→ */
 53→class ControlPreference : ListPreference {
 54→    @Suppress("unused")
 55→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int, defStyleRes: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr, defStyleRes)
 56→
 57→    @Suppress("unused")
 58→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr)
 59→
 60→    @Suppress("unused")
 61→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?) : super(context, attrs)
 62→
 63→    @Suppress("unused")
 64→    constructor(context: Context) : super(context)
 65→
 66→    fun refreshEntries() {
 67→        val entryTitles: MutableList<CharSequence> = ArrayList()
 68→        val entryIndices: MutableList<Int> = ArrayList()
 69→        // negative indices are "add"
 70→        entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_key))
 71→        entryIndices.add(ADD_KEY_INDEX)
 72→        // Put "Add gesture" option if gestures are enabled
 73→        if (context.sharedPrefs().getBoolean(GestureProcessor.PREF_KEY, false)) {
 74→            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_gesture))
 75→            entryIndices.add(ADD_GESTURE_INDEX)
 76→        }
 77→        // 0 and above are "delete" actions for already mapped preferences
 78→        for ((i, binding) in MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value).withIndex()) {
 79→            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_remove_binding, binding.toDisplayString(context)))
 80→            entryIndices.add(i)
 81→        }
 82→        entries = entryTitles.toTypedArray()
 83→        entryValues = entryIndices.map { it.toString() }.toTypedArray()
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    override fun onClick() {
 87→        refreshEntries()
 88→        super.onClick()
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    /** The summary that appears on the preference */
 92→    override fun getSummary(): CharSequence = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
 93→        .joinToString(", ") { it.toDisplayString(context) }
 94→
 95→    /** Called when an element is selected in the ListView */
 96→    @SuppressLint("CheckResult")
 97→    override fun callChangeListener(newValue: Any?): Boolean {
 98→        when (val index: Int = (newValue as String).toInt()) {
 99→            ADD_GESTURE_INDEX -> {
100→                val actionName = title
101→                MaterialDialog(context).show {
102→                    title(text = actionName.toString())
103→
104→                    val gesturePicker = GestureSelectionDialogUtils.getGesturePicker(context)
105→
106→                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) { materialDialog ->
107→                        val gesture = gesturePicker.getGesture() ?: return@positiveButton
108→                        val mappableBinding = fromGesture(gesture)
109→                        if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
110→                            showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_gesture), materialDialog)
111→                        } else {
112→                            addBinding(mappableBinding)
113→                            dismiss()
114→                        }
115→                    }
116→                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { dismiss() }
117→                    customView(view = gesturePicker)
118→
119→                    gesturePicker.onGestureChanged { gesture ->
120→                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(fromGesture(gesture))
121→                    }
122→
123→                    noAutoDismiss()
124→                }
125→            }
126→            ADD_KEY_INDEX -> {
127→                val actionName = title
128→                MaterialDialog(context).show {
129→                    val keyPicker: KeyPicker = KeyPicker.inflate(context)
130→                    customView(view = keyPicker.rootLayout)
131→                    title(text = actionName.toString())
132→
133→                    // When the user presses a key
134→                    keyPicker.setBindingChangedListener { binding ->
135→                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(CardSide.BOTH)))
136→                    }
137→
138→                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) {
139→                        val binding = keyPicker.getBinding() ?: return@positiveButton
140→                        // Use CardSide.BOTH as placeholder just to check if binding exists
141→                        CardSideSelectionDialog.displayInstance(context) { side ->
142→                            val mappableBinding = MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
143→                            if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
144→                                showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_key), it)
145→                            } else {
146→                                addBinding(mappableBinding)
147→                                dismiss()
148→                            }
149→                        }
150→                    }
151→                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { dismiss() }
152→
153→                    keyPicker.setKeycodeValidation(KeySelectionDialogUtils.disallowModifierKeyCodes())
154→                    noAutoDismiss()
155→                }
156→            }
157→            else -> {
158→                val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
159→                bindings.removeAt(index)
160→                value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
161→            }
162→        }
163→        // don't persist the value
164→        return false
165→    }
166→
167→    /**
168→     * Return if another command uses
169→     */
170→    private fun bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(binding: MappableBinding): Boolean {
171→        return getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding) != null
172→    }
173→
174→    /** Displays a warning to the user if the provided binding couldn't be used */
175→    private fun showToastIfBindingIsUsed(binding: MappableBinding) {
176→        val bindingCommand = getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)
177→            ?: return
178→
179→        val commandName = context.getString(bindingCommand.resourceId)
180→        val text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName)
181→        UIUtils.showThemedToast(context, text, true)
182→    }
183→
184→    /** @return command where the binding is mapped excluding the current command */
185→    private fun getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding: MappableBinding): ViewerCommand? {
186→        return MappableBinding.allMappings(context.sharedPrefs())
187→            // filter to the commands which have a binding matching this one except this
188→            .firstOrNull { x -> x.second.any { cmdBinding -> cmdBinding == binding } && x.first.preferenceKey != key }?.first
189→    }
190→
191→    private fun addBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
192→        val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
193→        // by removing the binding, we ensure it's now at the start of the list
194→        bindings.remove(binding)
195→        bindings.add(0, binding)
196→        value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
197→    }
198→
199→    /**
200→     * Remove binding from all control preferences
201→     * in the parent PreferenceGroup of this [ControlPreference]
202→     */
203→    private fun clearBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
204→        val preferenceGroup = parent
205→            ?: return
206→
207→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
208→            val commandPreference = preferenceGroup.findPreference<ControlPreference>(command.preferenceKey)!!
209→            val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(commandPreference.value)
210→            if (binding in bindings) {
211→                bindings.remove(binding)
212→                commandPreference.value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
213→            }
214→        }
215→    }
216→
217→    private fun showDialogToReplaceBinding(binding: MappableBinding, title: String, parentDialog: MaterialDialog) {
218→        val commandName = context.getString(getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)!!.resourceId)
219→
220→        AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
221→            title(text = title)
222→            message(text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName))
223→            positiveButton(R.string.dialog_positive_replace) {
224→                clearBinding(binding)
225→                addBinding(binding)
226→                parentDialog.dismiss()
227→            }
228→            negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel)
229→        }
230→    }
231→
232→    companion object {
233→        private const val ADD_KEY_INDEX = -2
234→        private const val ADD_GESTURE_INDEX = -1
235→    }
236→}

 97→    companion object {
 98→        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 99→            get() = Arrays.stream(values())

101→                .collect(Collectors.toList())

103→
104→    val preferenceKey: String
105→        get() = "binding_$name"
106→
107→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
108→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
109→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
110→            collection.remove(element)
111→            collection.add(0, element)
112→            true
113→        }
114→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
115→    }
116→
117→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
118→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
119→            // do not reorder the elements
120→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
121→                return@BiFunction false
122→            }
123→            collection.add(element)
124→            return@BiFunction true
125→        }
126→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
127→    }
128→
129→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
130→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
131→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
132→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
133→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
134→    }
135→
136→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
137→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
138→        bindings.remove(binding)
139→        prefs.edit {
140→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
141→        }
142→    }

162→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
163→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
164→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),

169→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
170→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
171→                )
172→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
173→                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
174→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
175→                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))

179→                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
180→                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
181→                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
182→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
183→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
184→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))

16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
17→
18→import androidx.preference.PreferenceCategory
19→import androidx.preference.PreferenceScreen
20→import com.ichi2.anki.R
21→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.CommandGroup
22→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
24→import com.ichi2.preferences.ControlPreference
25→
26→class ControlsSettingsFragment : SettingsFragment() {
27→    override val preferenceResource: Int
28→        get() = R.xml.preferences_controls
29→    override val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
30→        get() = "prefs.controls"
31→
32→    override fun initSubscreen() {
33→        addAllControlPreferencesToScreen(preferenceScreen)
34→    }
35→
36→    /**
37→     * Attaches all [ControlPreference] elements to the given [PreferenceScreen],
38→     * organized by command group categories.
39→     */
40→    fun addAllControlPreferencesToScreen(screen: PreferenceScreen) {
41→        // Group commands by their category
42→        val commandsByGroup = ViewerCommand.entries.groupBy { it.group }
43→
44→        // Create a PreferenceCategory for each command group and add the commands
45→        for (group in CommandGroup.entries) {
46→            val commands = commandsByGroup[group] ?: continue
47→
48→            val category = PreferenceCategory(screen.context).apply {
49→                setTitle(group.resourceId)
50→                key = "controls_category_${group.name}"
51→            }
52→            screen.addPreference(category)
53→
54→            for (command in commands) {
55→                val preference = ControlPreference(category.context).apply {
56→                    setTitle(command.resourceId)
57→                    key = command.preferenceKey
58→                    setDefaultValue(command.defaultValue.toPreferenceString())
59→                }
60→                category.addPreference(preference)
61→            }
62→        }
63→    }
64→}
65→

23→
24→class ViewerCommandTest {
25→
26→    @Test
27→    fun preference_keys_are_not_changed() {
28→        val names = Joiner.on(", ").join(ViewerCommand.entries.map { x -> x.preferenceKey })
29→
30→        // NONE OF THESE SHOULD BE CHANGED OR A USER WILL LOSE THE ASSOCIATED PREFERENCES
31→        // Adds are acceptable
32→        // Deletes are acceptable if the binding should no longer exist
33→        assertEquals(
34→            "binding_SHOW_ANSWER, binding_FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1, " +
35→                "binding_FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2, binding_FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3, binding_FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4, " +
36→                "binding_UNDO, binding_EDIT, binding_MARK, binding_BURY_CARD, " +
37→                "binding_SUSPEND_CARD, binding_DELETE, binding_PLAY_MEDIA, " +
38→                "binding_EXIT, binding_BURY_NOTE, binding_SUSPEND_NOTE, binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_RED, " +
39→                "binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE, binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN, binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE, " +
40→                "binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK, binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE, binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE, " +
41→                "binding_UNSET_FLAG, binding_PAGE_UP, binding_PAGE_DOWN, binding_TAG, binding_CARD_INFO, binding_ABORT_AND_SYNC, " +
42→                "binding_RECORD_VOICE, binding_REPLAY_VOICE, binding_TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD, binding_CLEAR_WHITEBOARD, " +
43→                "binding_CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR, binding_SHOW_HINT, binding_SHOW_ALL_HINTS, binding_ADD_NOTE, " +
44→                "binding_RESCHEDULE_NOTE",
45→            names
46→        )
47→    }
48→
49→    /**
50→     * Verifies that all ViewerCommand entries have a valid CommandGroup assigned.
51→     * This test ensures that when new commands are added, they are properly categorized
52→     * for display in the Controls preferences screen.
53→     */
54→    @Test
55→    fun all_commands_have_a_valid_group() {
56→        val commandsByGroup = ViewerCommand.entries.groupBy { it.group }
57→
58→        // Verify all CommandGroup values are used
59→        for (group in CommandGroup.entries) {
60→            val commands = commandsByGroup[group]
61→            assertTrue(
62→                "CommandGroup.$group should have at least one command assigned",
63→                !commands.isNullOrEmpty()
64→            )
65→        }
66→
67→        // Verify all commands have a group (implicitly guaranteed by enum design, but good to document)
68→        for (command in ViewerCommand.entries) {
69→            assertTrue(
70→                "ViewerCommand.${command.name} should have a non-null group",
71→                CommandGroup.entries.contains(command.group)
72→            )
73→        }
74→    }
75→
76→    /**
77→     * Documents the expected grouping of commands.
78→     * If this test fails after adding a new command, update the expected counts accordingly.
79→     */
80→    @Test
81→    fun command_groups_have_expected_counts() {
82→        val commandsByGroup = ViewerCommand.entries.groupBy { it.group }
83→
84→        // These counts should be updated when commands are added/removed/moved between groups
85→        assertEquals("ANSWER group", 5, commandsByGroup[CommandGroup.ANSWER]?.size)
86→        assertEquals("FLAGS group", 8, commandsByGroup[CommandGroup.FLAGS]?.size)
87→        assertEquals("CARD_OR_NOTE group", 12, commandsByGroup[CommandGroup.CARD_OR_NOTE]?.size)
88→        assertEquals("WHITEBOARD group", 3, commandsByGroup[CommandGroup.WHITEBOARD]?.size)
89→        assertEquals("AUDIO group", 3, commandsByGroup[CommandGroup.AUDIO]?.size)
90→        assertEquals("NAVIGATION group", 4, commandsByGroup[CommandGroup.NAVIGATION]?.size)
91→        assertEquals("OTHER group", 2, commandsByGroup[CommandGroup.OTHER]?.size)
92→
93→        // Total should match all commands
94→        val totalInGroups = commandsByGroup.values.sumOf { it.size }
95→        assertEquals("Total commands in groups should match ViewerCommand.entries count", ViewerCommand.entries.size, totalInGroups)
96→    }
97→}

### 1. **ViewerCommand.kt** - Added command grouping
- Created a new `CommandGroup` enum with 7 categories:
  - `ANSWER` - Answer buttons (Show answer, Answer 1-4)
  - `FLAGS` - Flag operations (Red, Orange, Green, Blue, Pink, Turquoise, Purple, Unset)
  - `CARD_OR_NOTE` - Card/note actions (Undo, Edit, Mark, Bury, Suspend, Delete, Tag, Info, Add, Reschedule)
  - `WHITEBOARD` - Whiteboard operations (Toggle, Clear, Pen Color)
  - `AUDIO` - Audio controls (Play Media, Record Voice, Replay Voice)
  - `NAVIGATION` - Navigation commands (Exit, Page Up, Page Down, Abort and Sync)
  - `OTHER` - Miscellaneous (Show Hint, Show All Hints)
- Added a `group: CommandGroup` parameter to each `ViewerCommand` entry

### 2. **ControlsSettingsFragment.kt** - Organized preferences by category
- Updated `addAllControlPreferencesToScreen()` to create separate `PreferenceCategory` for each command group
- Commands are now displayed under their respective category headers in the Controls preferences screen

### 3. **ControlPreference.kt** - Fixed binding search across categories
- Updated `clearBinding()` to search through the entire `PreferenceScreen` (parent of parent) instead of just the parent category

### 4. **String Resources** (10-preferences.xml)
- Added category title strings:
  - `pref_cat_commands_answer` - "Answer buttons"
  - `pref_cat_commands_flags` - "Flags"
  - `pref_cat_commands_card_note` - "Card/note"
  - `pref_cat_commands_audio` - "Audio"
  - `pref_cat_commands_navigation` - "Navigation"
  - `pref_cat_commands_other` - "Other"

### 5. **preferences_controls.xml** - Removed static category
- Removed the old `PreferenceCategory` as categories are now added dynamically

### 6. **preferences.xml** - Cleanup
- Removed unused `controls_command_mapping_cat_key` string

### 7. **Tests** (ViewerCommandTest.kt)
- Added `all_commands_have_a_valid_group()` test - Verifies every command has a group and every group has commands
- Added `command_groups_have_expected_counts()` test - Documents expected distribution of 37 commands across 7 categories

### 8. **PreferencesAnalyticsTest.kt** - Fixed test
- Removed `key_map_category` from excluded preferences list since it's no longer used

This implementation makes it much easier to find commands in the Controls preferences screen by organizing the 37 commands into logical categories instead of a single flat list.

100→        get() = "binding_$name"
101→
102→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
103→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
104→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
105→            collection.remove(element)
106→            collection.add(0, element)
107→            true
108→        }
109→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
110→    }
111→
112→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
113→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
114→            // do not reorder the elements
115→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
116→                return@BiFunction false
117→            }
118→            collection.add(element)
119→            return@BiFunction true
120→        }
121→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
125→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
126→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
127→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
128→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
129→    }
130→
131→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
132→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
133→        bindings.remove(binding)
134→        prefs.edit {

137→    }

163→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
164→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
165→                )
166→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> listOf(
167→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
168→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
169→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
170→                )
171→                EDIT -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
172→                MARK -> listOf(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
173→                BURY_CARD -> listOf(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
174→                BURY_NOTE -> listOf(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
175→                SUSPEND_CARD -> listOf(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
176→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> listOf(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
177→                PLAY_MEDIA -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
178→                REPLAY_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
