[thinking: Looking at the code, I see the issue. In the `ImportFileSelectionFragment.kt` file, when importing CSV files, the mime types are defined as:
```kotlin
mimeType = "*/*",
extraMimes =
    arrayOf(
     ...]
Looking at this issue, I can see the problem. The user is unable to see or open CSV files because the MIME type `text/csv` (which is the standard MIME type for CSV files) is not properly handled in the codebase. 

Let me trace through the code:

1. In `AndroidManifest.xml`, the intent-filter for MIME types includes `text/comma-separated-values` but NOT `text/csv`:
   ```xml
   <data android:mimeType="text/comma-separated-values"/>
   ```

2. In `IntentHandler.kt`, the `getLaunchType` function only checks for:
   ```kotlin
   mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
       mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
   ```

3. In `ImportFileSelectionFragment.kt`, the `extraMimes` array correctly includes `text/csv`:
   ```kotlin
   extraMimes = arrayOf(
       TEXT_PLAIN,
       "text/comma-separated-values",
       "text/csv",
       "text/tab-separated-values",
   ),
   ```

So the problem is that **`text/csv`** (the standard MIME type for CSV files) is missing from both:
1. The `AndroidManifest.xml` intent-filter 
2. The `IntentHandler.getLaunchType()` function

Let me check if there are any related tests:

 31→@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)

1. **AndroidManifest.xml**: Added `<data android:mimeType="text/csv"/>` to the intent-filter for MIME types. This allows the app to regist...]
All changes have been applied successfully. Here's a summary of the fix:

## Summary of Changes

### Root Cause
The `text/csv` MIME type (which is the **standard MIME type for CSV files**) was missing from the app's intent handling. While the app supported `text/comma-separated-values` and `text/tab-separated-values`, it didn't support `text/csv` - which is what most file managers and apps use when sharing/opening CSV files.

### Changes Made

1. **`AndroidManifest.xml`** (line 241)
   - Added `<data android:mimeType="text/csv"/>` to the intent-filter
   - This allows the app to register as a handler for files with the `text/csv` MIME type

2. **`IntentHandler.kt`** (lines 336-338)
   - Added `mimeType == "text/csv"` to the `getLaunchType()` function
   - This ensures files with `text/csv` MIME type are properly routed to the text import handler

3. **`IntentHandlerTest.kt`** (lines 99, 104)
   - Added test cases for `text/csv` MIME type for both `ACTION_VIEW` and `ACTION_SEND` intents
   - Ensures the new functionality is covered by tests

### Why This Fixes the Issue
The user couldn't see or open CSV files because:
- Their device's file manager was reporting CSV files with the `text/csv` MIME type
- AnkiDroid wasn't registered as a handler for `text/csv`, so:
  1. The system wouldn't show AnkiDroid as an option to open CSV files
  2. Even if a CSV file was shared with AnkiDroid through another mechanism, the `getLaunchType()` function wouldn't recognize it as a text import

The fix adds support for the standard `text/csv` MIME type alongside the existing alternative MIME types.

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.171 (commit: 7a65160e).

## Issue to Resolve

Regression: can't select deck in add screen
Also, I can't change decks in the note editor


https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/69634269/91e7b6db-ef3e-4048-b9dd-30545b4405ce

_Originally posted by @BrayanDSO in https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/14171#issuecomment-1697548993_

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/DeckSpinnerSelection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

 39→ * Populates the spinner with data, and handles display of the selected deck within the spinner control
 40→ *
 41→ * @param spinner
 42→ * The spinner displayed in the activity.
 43→ * Empty at construction. After initialization, it contains in this order:
 44→ * 1. "All decks" if [showAllDecks] is true
 45→ * 2. All decks from [mAllDeckIds].
 46→ * @param showAllDecks Whether the deck selection should allow "All Decks" as an option
 47→ * @param alwaysShowDefault If true, never hide the default deck. If false, match [DeckPicker]'s logic
 48→ * @param showFilteredDecks whether to show filtered decks
 49→ */
 50→class DeckSpinnerSelection(
 51→    private val context: AnkiActivity,
 52→    private val collection: Collection,
 53→    private val spinner: Spinner,
 54→    private val showAllDecks: Boolean,
 55→    private val alwaysShowDefault: Boolean,
 56→    private val showFilteredDecks: Boolean
 57→) {
 58→    /**
 59→     * All of the decks shown to the user.
 60→     */
 61→    private lateinit var mAllDeckIds: ArrayList<Long>
 62→
 63→    private val mFragmentManagerSupplier: FragmentManagerSupplier = context.asFragmentManagerSupplier()
 64→
 65→    lateinit var dropDownDecks: List<DeckNameId>
 66→        private set
 67→    private var mDeckDropDownAdapter: DeckDropDownAdapter? = null
 68→
 69→    fun initializeActionBarDeckSpinner(actionBar: ActionBar) {
 70→        actionBar.setDisplayShowTitleEnabled(false)
 71→
 72→        // Add drop-down menu to select deck to action bar.
 73→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = showFilteredDecks)
 74→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 75→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 76→            val thisDid = d.id
 77→            mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)
 78→        }
 79→        mDeckDropDownAdapter = DeckDropDownAdapter(context, dropDownDecks)
 80→        spinner.adapter = mDeckDropDownAdapter
 81→        setSpinnerListener()
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    fun initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner() {
 85→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = false)
 86→        val deckNames = ArrayList<String>(dropDownDecks.size)
 87→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 88→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 89→            val currentName = d.name
 90→            deckNames.add(currentName)
 91→        }
 92→        val noteDeckAdapter: ArrayAdapter<String?> = object : ArrayAdapter<String?>(context, R.layout.multiline_spinner_item, deckNames as List<String?>) {
 93→            override fun getDropDownView(position: Int, convertView: View?, parent: ViewGroup): View {
 94→                // Cast the drop down items (popup items) as text view
 95→                val tv = super.getDropDownView(position, convertView, parent) as TextView
 96→
 97→                // If this item is selected
 98→                if (position == spinner.selectedItemPosition) {
 99→                    tv.setBackgroundColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_background))
100→                    tv.setTextColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_text))
101→                }
102→
103→                // Return the modified view
104→                return tv
105→            }
106→        }
107→        spinner.adapter = noteDeckAdapter
108→        setSpinnerListener()
109→    }
110→
111→    /**
112→     * @return All decks, except maybe default if it should be hidden.
113→     */
114→    fun computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered: Boolean): List<DeckNameId> {
115→        return collection.decks.allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = includeFiltered)
116→    }
117→
118→    fun setSpinnerListener() {
119→        spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
120→            if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
121→                displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
122→            }
123→            true
124→        }
125→        setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
126→    }
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Move the selected deck in the spinner to [deckId].
130→     * Timber if [deckId] is not an id of a known deck.
131→     * @param deckId The ID of the deck to select
132→     */
133→    fun updateDeckPosition(deckId: DeckId) {
134→        val position = mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)
135→        if (position != -1) {
136→            spinner.setSelection(position)
137→        } else {
138→            Timber.w("updateDeckPosition() error :: deckId=%d, position=%d", deckId, position)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    fun notifyDataSetChanged() {
143→        mDeckDropDownAdapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
144→    }
145→
146→    fun setEnabledActionBarSpinner(enabled: Boolean) {
147→        spinner.isEnabled = enabled
148→    }
149→
150→    fun setSpinnerVisibility(view: Int) {
151→        spinner.visibility = view
152→    }
153→
154→    /**
155→     * Iterates the drop down decks, and selects the one matching the given id.
156→     * @param deckId The deck id to be selected.
157→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck If true, deckId will be set as the current deck id of Collection
158→     * (this means the deck selected here will continue to appear in any future Activity whose
159→     * display data is loaded from Collection's current deck). If false, deckId will not be set as
160→     * the current deck id of Collection.
161→     * @return True if selection succeeded.
162→     */
163→    fun selectDeckById(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
164→        return if (deckId == ALL_DECKS_ID) {
165→            selectAllDecks()
166→        } else {
167→            selectDeck(deckId, setAsCurrentDeck)
168→        }
169→    }
170→
171→    /**
172→     * select in the spinner deck with id
173→     * @param deckId The deck id to search (not ALL_DECKS_ID)
174→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck whether this deck should be selected in the collection (if it exists)
175→     * @return whether it was found
176→     */
177→    private fun selectDeck(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
178→        for (dropDownDeckIdx in mAllDeckIds.indices) {
179→            if (mAllDeckIds[dropDownDeckIdx] == deckId) {
180→                val position = if (showAllDecks) dropDownDeckIdx + 1 else dropDownDeckIdx
181→                spinner.setSelection(position)
182→                if (setAsCurrentDeck) {
183→                    collection.decks.select(deckId)
184→                }
185→                return true
186→            }
187→        }
188→        return false
189→    }
190→
191→    /**
192→     * Select all decks. Must be called only if [showAllDecks].
193→     * @return whether selection was a success.
194→     */
195→    fun selectAllDecks(): Boolean {
196→        if (!showAllDecks) {
197→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport("selectAllDecks was called while `showAllDecks is false`", "DeckSpinnerSelection:selectAllDecks")
198→            return false
199→        }
200→        spinner.setSelection(0)
201→        return true
202→    }
203→
204→    /**
205→     * Displays a [DeckSelectionDialog]
206→     */
207→    fun displayDeckSelectionDialog(col: Collection?) {
208→        val decks = fromCollection(col!!, includeFiltered = false).toMutableList()
209→        if (showAllDecks) {
210→            decks.add(SelectableDeck(ALL_DECKS_ID, context.resources.getString(R.string.card_browser_all_decks)))
211→        }
212→        val dialog = DeckSelectionDialog.newInstance(context.getString(R.string.search_deck), null, false, decks)
213→        AnkiActivity.showDialogFragment(mFragmentManagerSupplier.getFragmentManager(), dialog)
214→    }
215→
216→    companion object {
217→        const val ALL_DECKS_ID = 0L
218→    }
219→}

[thinking: I found the bug! Looking at `initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner()`:```kotlin
fun initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner() {    dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = false)    val deckNames = Ar...]
I found the bug! In `initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner()`, the `mAllDeckIds` ArrayList is created with a capacity but never actually populated with the deck IDs. The loop only adds deck names to `deckNames`, but doesn't add the IDs to `mAllDeckIds`.

Compare this to `initializeActionBarDeckSpinner()` which properly adds the IDs:
```kotlin
for (d in dropDownDecks) {
    val thisDid = d.id
    mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)  // IDs are added here
}
```

But in `initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner()`:
```kotlin
for (d in dropDownDecks) {
    val currentName = d.name
    deckNames.add(currentName)  // Only names are added, IDs are missing!
}
```

This causes `updateDeckPosition()` to fail because `mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)` will always return -1 since the list is empty.

Let me fix this:

 74→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)

 95→            override fun getDropDownView(position: Int, convertView: View?, parent: ViewGroup): View {
 96→                // Cast the drop down items (popup items) as text view
 97→                val tv = super.getDropDownView(position, convertView, parent) as TextView

100→                if (position == spinner.selectedItemPosition) {
101→                    tv.setBackgroundColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_background))
102→                    tv.setTextColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_text))
103→                }

108→        }

113→    /**
114→     * @return All decks, except maybe default if it should be hidden.
115→     */
116→    fun computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered: Boolean): List<DeckNameId> {
117→        return collection.decks.allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = includeFiltered)
118→    }

120→    fun setSpinnerListener() {
121→        spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
122→            if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
123→                displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
124→            }
125→            true
126→        }
127→        setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
128→    }
129→
130→    /**
131→     * Move the selected deck in the spinner to [deckId].
132→     * Timber if [deckId] is not an id of a known deck.
133→     * @param deckId The ID of the deck to select
134→     */
135→    fun updateDeckPosition(deckId: DeckId) {
136→        val position = mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)
137→        if (position != -1) {
138→            spinner.setSelection(position)
139→        } else {
140→            Timber.w("updateDeckPosition() error :: deckId=%d, position=%d", deckId, position)
141→        }
142→    }

144→    fun notifyDataSetChanged() {
145→        mDeckDropDownAdapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
146→    }

149→        spinner.isEnabled = enabled
150→    }

152→    fun setSpinnerVisibility(view: Int) {
153→        spinner.visibility = view
154→    }
155→
156→    /**
157→     * Iterates the drop down decks, and selects the one matching the given id.
158→     * @param deckId The deck id to be selected.
159→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck If true, deckId will be set as the current deck id of Collection
160→     * (this means the deck selected here will continue to appear in any future Activity whose
161→     * display data is loaded from Collection's current deck). If false, deckId will not be set as
162→     * the current deck id of Collection.
163→     * @return True if selection succeeded.
164→     */
165→    fun selectDeckById(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
166→        return if (deckId == ALL_DECKS_ID) {
167→            selectAllDecks()
168→        } else {
169→            selectDeck(deckId, setAsCurrentDeck)
170→        }
171→    }
172→
173→    /**
174→     * select in the spinner deck with id
175→     * @param deckId The deck id to search (not ALL_DECKS_ID)
176→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck whether this deck should be selected in the collection (if it exists)
177→     * @return whether it was found
178→     */
179→    private fun selectDeck(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
180→        for (dropDownDeckIdx in mAllDeckIds.indices) {
181→            if (mAllDeckIds[dropDownDeckIdx] == deckId) {
182→                val position = if (showAllDecks) dropDownDeckIdx + 1 else dropDownDeckIdx
183→                spinner.setSelection(position)
184→                if (setAsCurrentDeck) {
185→                    collection.decks.select(deckId)
186→                }
187→                return true
188→            }
189→        }
190→        return false
191→    }
192→
193→    /**
194→     * Select all decks. Must be called only if [showAllDecks].
195→     * @return whether selection was a success.
196→     */
197→    fun selectAllDecks(): Boolean {
198→        if (!showAllDecks) {
199→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport("selectAllDecks was called while `showAllDecks is false`", "DeckSpinnerSelection:selectAllDecks")
200→            return false
201→        }
202→        spinner.setSelection(0)
203→        return true
204→    }
205→
206→    /**
207→     * Displays a [DeckSelectionDialog]
208→     */
209→    fun displayDeckSelectionDialog(col: Collection?) {
210→        val decks = fromCollection(col!!, includeFiltered = false).toMutableList()
211→        if (showAllDecks) {
212→            decks.add(SelectableDeck(ALL_DECKS_ID, context.resources.getString(R.string.card_browser_all_decks)))
213→        }
214→        val dialog = DeckSelectionDialog.newInstance(context.getString(R.string.search_deck), null, false, decks)
215→        AnkiActivity.showDialogFragment(mFragmentManagerSupplier.getFragmentManager(), dialog)
216→    }
217→
218→    companion object {
219→        const val ALL_DECKS_ID = 0L
220→    }
221→}

The fix is complete. Let me provide a summary of the issue and the fix:

## Summary

**Root Cause:** In `DeckSpinnerSelection.initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner()`, the `mAllDeckIds` ArrayList was created with the correct capacity but never populated with actual deck IDs. The loop only added deck names to `deckNames` but forgot to add the IDs to `mAllDeckIds`:

```kotlin
// Before (buggy code)
for (d in dropDownDecks) {
    val currentName = d.name
    deckNames.add(currentName)
    // mAllDeckIds was never populated here!
}
```

This caused deck selection to fail because:
1. `updateDeckPosition()` (line 135-142) uses `mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)` to find the position - which always returned `-1` since the list was empty
2. `selectDeckById()` uses `mAllDeckIds` to iterate and find the deck to select

**Fix:** Added the missing line to populate `mAllDeckIds` in the loop, consistent with how `initializeActionBarDeckSpinner()` handles this:

```kotlin
// After (fixed code)
for (d in dropDownDecks) {
    val currentName = d.name
    val thisDid = d.id
    deckNames.add(currentName)
    mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)  // Now IDs are properly collected
}
```

This ensures that when a user selects a deck in the DeckSelectionDialog (in the add card screen or note editor), the selected deck can be properly identified and displayed in the spinner.

 48→
 49→abstract class CardViewerFragment(@LayoutRes layout: Int) : Fragment(layout) {
 50→    protected abstract val viewModel: CardViewerViewModel
 51→    protected abstract val webView: WebView
 52→
 53→    @CallSuper
 54→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 55→        setupWebView(savedInstanceState)
 56→        setupErrorListeners()
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onStart() {
 60→        super.onStart()
 61→        viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(true)
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun onStop() {
 65→        super.onStop()
 66→        if (!requireActivity().isChangingConfigurations) {
 67→            viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(false)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun setupWebView(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        CookieManager.getInstance().setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(webView, true)
 73→        with(webView) {
 74→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
 75→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient()
 76→            scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_OUTSIDE_OVERLAY
 77→            with(settings) {
 78→                javaScriptEnabled = true
 79→                loadWithOverviewMode = true
 80→                builtInZoomControls = true
 81→                displayZoomControls = false
 82→                allowFileAccess = true
 83→                domStorageEnabled = true
 84→            }
 85→            loadDataWithBaseURL(
 86→                "http://${AnkiServer.LOCALHOST}/",
 87→                stdHtml(requireContext(), Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode),
 88→                "text/html",
 89→                null,
 90→                null
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        viewModel.eval
 94→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
 95→            .onEach { eval ->
 96→                webView.evaluateJavascript(eval, null)
 97→            }
 98→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun setupErrorListeners() {
102→        viewModel.onError
103→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
104→            .onEach { errorMessage ->
105→                AlertDialog.Builder(requireContext())
106→                    .setTitle(R.string.vague_error)
107→                    .setMessage(errorMessage)
108→                    .show()
109→            }
110→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
111→
112→        viewModel.onMediaError
113→            .onEach { showMediaErrorSnackbar(it) }
114→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
115→
116→        viewModel.onTtsError
117→            .onEach { showSnackbar(it.localizedErrorMessage(requireContext())) }
118→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): WebViewClient {
122→        val resourceHandler = ViewerResourceHandler(requireContext(), AnkiServer.LOCALHOST)
123→        return object : WebViewClient() {
124→            override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
125→                view: WebView?,
126→                request: WebResourceRequest
127→            ): WebResourceResponse? {
128→                return resourceHandler.shouldInterceptRequest(request)
129→            }
130→
131→            override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
132→                viewModel.onPageFinished(isAfterRecreation = savedInstanceState != null)
133→            }
134→
135→            override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView, request: WebResourceRequest): Boolean {
136→                val urlString = request.url.toString()
137→                if (urlString.startsWith("playsound:")) {
138→                    viewModel.playSoundFromUrl(urlString)
139→                    return true
140→                }
141→                if (urlString.startsWith("tts-voices:")) {
142→                    TtsVoicesDialogFragment().show(childFragmentManager, null)
143→                    return true
144→                }
145→                try {
146→                    openUrl(request.url)
147→                    return true
148→                } catch (_: Exception) {
149→                    Timber.w("Could not open url")
150→                }
151→                return false
152→            }
153→
154→            override fun onReceivedError(
155→                view: WebView,
156→                request: WebResourceRequest,
157→                error: WebResourceError
158→            ) {
159→                viewModel.mediaErrorHandler.processFailure(request) { filename: String ->
160→                    showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename)
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun onCreateWebChromeClient(): WebChromeClient {
167→        return object : WebChromeClient() {
168→            private lateinit var customView: View
169→
170→            // used for displaying `<video>` in fullscreen.
171→            // This implementation requires configChanges="orientation" in the manifest
172→            // to avoid destroying the View if the device is rotated
173→            override fun onShowCustomView(
174→                paramView: View,
175→                paramCustomViewCallback: CustomViewCallback?
176→            ) {
177→                customView = paramView
178→                val window = requireActivity().window
179→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).addView(
180→                    customView,
181→                    FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
182→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
183→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
184→                    )
185→                )
186→                // hide system bars
187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            override fun onHideCustomView() {
194→                val window = requireActivity().window
195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
200→                }
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename: String) {
206→        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.card_viewer_could_not_find_image, filename)) {
207→            setAction(R.string.help) { openUrl(Uri.parse(getString(R.string.link_faq_missing_media))) }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun openUrl(uri: Uri) = startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
212→}

368→    /**
369→     * Global method to show dialog fragment including adding it to back stack Note: DO NOT call this from an async

374→    open fun showDialogFragment(newFragment: DialogFragment) {

386→    open fun showAsyncDialogFragment(newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment) {

390→    /**
391→     * Global method to show a dialog fragment including adding it to back stack and handling the case where the dialog
392→     * is shown from an async task, by showing the message in the notification bar if the activity was stopped before the
393→     * AsyncTask completed
394→     *
395→     * @param newFragment  the AsyncDialogFragment you want to show
396→     * @param channel the Channel to use for the notification
397→     */
398→    fun showAsyncDialogFragment(
399→        newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment,
400→        channel: Channel
401→    ) {
402→        try {
403→            showDialogFragment(newFragment)
404→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
405→            Timber.w("failed to show fragment, activity is likely paused. Sending notification")
406→            // Store a persistent message to SharedPreferences instructing AnkiDroid to show dialog
407→            DialogHandler.storeMessage(newFragment.dialogHandlerMessage?.toMessage())
408→            // Show a basic notification to the user in the notification bar in the meantime
409→            val title = newFragment.notificationTitle
410→            val message = newFragment.notificationMessage
411→            showSimpleNotification(title, message, channel)
412→        }
413→    }
414→
415→    /**
416→     * Show a simple message dialog, dismissing the message without taking any further action when OK button is pressed.
417→     * If a DialogFragment cannot be shown due to the Activity being stopped then the message is shown in the
418→     * notification bar instead.
419→     *
420→     * @param message
421→     * @param reload flag which forces app to be restarted when true
422→     */
423→    open fun showSimpleMessageDialog(
424→        message: String,
425→        title: String = "",
426→        reload: Boolean = false
427→    ) {
428→        val newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment =
429→            SimpleMessageDialog.newInstance(title, message, reload)
430→        showAsyncDialogFragment(newFragment)
431→    }
432→
433→    fun showSimpleNotification(
434→        title: String,
435→        message: String?,
436→        channel: Channel
437→    ) {
438→        val prefs = this.sharedPrefs()
439→        // Show a notification unless all notifications have been totally disabled
440→        if (prefs.getString(MINIMUM_CARDS_DUE_FOR_NOTIFICATION, "0")!!
441→            .toInt() <= Preferences.PENDING_NOTIFICATIONS_ONLY
442→        ) {
443→            // Use the title as the ticker unless the title is simply "AnkiDroid"
444→            val ticker: String? = if (title == resources.getString(R.string.app_name)) {
445→                message
446→            } else {
447→                title
448→            }
449→            // Build basic notification
450→            val builder = NotificationCompat.Builder(
451→                this,
452→                channel.id
453→            )
454→                .setSmallIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_notify)
455→                .setContentTitle(title)
456→                .setContentText(message)
457→                .setColor(this.getColor(R.color.material_light_blue_500))
458→                .setStyle(NotificationCompat.BigTextStyle().bigText(message))
459→                .setVisibility(NotificationCompat.VISIBILITY_PUBLIC)
460→                .setTicker(ticker)
461→            // Enable vibrate and blink if set in preferences
462→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetVibrate", false)) {
463→                builder.setVibrate(longArrayOf(1000, 1000, 1000))
464→            }
465→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetBlink", false)) {
466→                builder.setLights(Color.BLUE, 1000, 1000)
467→            }
468→            // Creates an explicit intent for an Activity in your app
469→            val resultIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
470→            resultIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
471→            val resultPendingIntent = PendingIntentCompat.getActivity(
472→                this,
473→                0,
474→                resultIntent,
475→                PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT,
476→                false
477→            )
478→            builder.setContentIntent(resultPendingIntent)
479→            val notificationManager = getSystemService(NOTIFICATION_SERVICE) as NotificationManager
480→            // mId allows you to update the notification later on.
481→            notificationManager.notify(SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID, builder.build())
482→        }
483→    }

548→     * If storage permissions are not granted, shows a toast message and finishes the activity.

550→     * This should be called AFTER a call to `super.`[onCreate]

573→
574→        fun showDialogFragment(manager: FragmentManager, newFragment: DialogFragment) {
575→            // DialogFragment.show() will take care of adding the fragment
576→            // in a transaction. We also want to remove any currently showing
577→            // dialog, so make our own transaction and take care of that here.
578→            val ft = manager.beginTransaction()
579→            val prev = manager.findFragmentByTag(DIALOG_FRAGMENT_TAG)
580→            if (prev != null) {
581→                ft.remove(prev)
582→            }
583→            // save transaction to the back stack
584→            ft.addToBackStack(DIALOG_FRAGMENT_TAG)
585→            newFragment.show(ft, DIALOG_FRAGMENT_TAG)
586→            manager.executePendingTransactions()
587→        }
588→
589→        private const val SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID = 0
590→    }
591→}
592→

16→package com.ichi2.anki

18→import android.content.Context

20→import android.os.Bundle
21→import android.view.KeyEvent
22→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
23→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentContainerView
24→import androidx.fragment.app.commit
25→import com.ichi2.themes.setTransparentStatusBar
26→import com.ichi2.utils.getInstanceFromClassName
27→import kotlin.reflect.KClass
28→import kotlin.reflect.jvm.jvmName
29→
30→/**
31→ * Activity aimed to host a fragment on the entire screen.
32→ * For that, it uses [R.layout.single_fragment_activity], which has only a [FragmentContainerView]
33→ *
34→ * Useful to avoid creating a Activity for every new screen
35→ * while being able to reuse the fragment on other places.
36→ *
37→ * [getIntent] can be used as an easy way to build a [SingleFragmentActivity]
38→ */
39→open class SingleFragmentActivity : AnkiActivity() {
40→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
41→        if (showedActivityFailedScreen(savedInstanceState)) {
42→            return
43→        }
44→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
45→        setContentView(R.layout.single_fragment_activity)
46→        setTransparentStatusBar()
47→
48→        // avoid recreating the fragment on configuration changes
49→        if (savedInstanceState != null) {
50→            return
51→        }
52→
53→        val fragmentClassName = requireNotNull(intent.getStringExtra(FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA)) {
54→            "'$FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA' extra should be provided"
55→        }
56→        val fragment = getInstanceFromClassName<Fragment>(fragmentClassName).apply {
57→            arguments = intent.getBundleExtra(FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA)
58→        }
59→        supportFragmentManager.commit {
60→            replace(R.id.fragment_container, fragment)
61→        }
62→    }
63→
64→    override fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
65→        val fragment = supportFragmentManager.findFragmentById(R.id.fragment_container)!!
66→        if (fragment is DispatchKeyEventListener && fragment.dispatchKeyEvent(event)) {
67→            return true
68→        }
69→        return super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
70→    }
71→
72→    companion object {
73→        const val FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA = "fragmentName"
74→        const val FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA = "fragmentArgs"
75→
76→        fun getIntent(context: Context, fragmentClass: KClass<out Fragment>, arguments: Bundle? = null): Intent {
77→            return Intent(context, SingleFragmentActivity::class.java).apply {
78→                putExtra(FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA, fragmentClass.jvmName)
79→                putExtra(FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA, arguments)
80→            }
81→        }
82→    }
83→}
84→
85→interface DispatchKeyEventListener {
86→    fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean
87→}
88→

[thinking: No tests for `PreviewerFragment` specifically. The fix looks good. Let me summarize what I've done:

The bug was in `SingleFragmentActivity.dispatchKeyEvent()`. The original code was:

```kotlin
overr...]
The fix is complete. Let me summarize what was causing the bug and how it was fixed:

## Root Cause Analysis

The bug was in `SingleFragmentActivity.dispatchKeyEvent()`. The original code was:

```kotlin
override fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
    val fragment = supportFragmentManager.findFragmentById(R.id.fragment_container)!!
    return if (fragment is DispatchKeyEventListener) {
        fragment.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
    } else {
        super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
    }
}
```

**The Problem:**
- When the fragment implements `DispatchKeyEventListener`, the code directly returned whatever the fragment returned
- `PreviewerFragment.dispatchKeyEvent()` returns `false` for the back button (KEYCODE_BACK is not handled in its `when` block)
- Since the fragment returned `false`, the activity also returned `false` **without ever calling** `super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)`
- This meant the back button key event was never passed to the Android framework's default handling, which is what closes the activity

## The Fix

Changed the logic to:
```kotlin
override fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
    val fragment = supportFragmentManager.findFragmentById(R.id.fragment_container)!!
    if (fragment is DispatchKeyEventListener && fragment.dispatchKeyEvent(event)) {
        return true
    }
    return super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
}
```

Now:
1. If the fragment is a `DispatchKeyEventListener` AND it handles the event (returns `true`), we return `true` (event consumed)
2. Otherwise, we **always** call `super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)` to let the Android framework handle the event, including the back button which will properly close the activity

This fix is minimal and only affects `SingleFragmentActivity.kt`. It ensures that when fragments don't handle a key event, the event is still properly passed to the system for default handling.

Now I understand the issue. The `parseSourcesToFileScheme` function uses Jsoup to parse HTML, and when changes are made, it returns `doc.body().html()` which can strip out or modify `<script>` tags.

The fix should avoid using Jsoup's HTML serialization while still using it to find the elements that need modification. Instead, we should perform string replacements on the original content to preserve scripts and other elements.

 53→     * A closed collection can be detected via [withOpenColOrNull] or by checking [Collection.dbClosed].
 54→     */
 55→    private var collection: Collection? = null
 56→
 57→    private var queue: CoroutineDispatcher = Dispatchers.IO.limitedParallelism(1)
 58→
 59→    @VisibleForTesting
 60→    var emulateOpenFailure = false
 61→
 62→    /** A speed optimisation to remove the logging function of the collection */
 63→    @VisibleForTesting
 64→    var disableLogFile: Boolean = false
 65→
 66→    /**
 67→     * Execute the provided block on a serial background queue, to ensure
 68→     * concurrent access does not happen.
 69→     *
 70→     * The background queue is run in a [Dispatchers.IO] context.
 71→     * It's important that the block is not suspendable - if it were, it would allow
 72→     * multiple requests to be interleaved when a suspend point was hit.
 73→     *
 74→     * TODO Disallow running functions that are supposed to be run inside the queue outside of it.
 75→     *   For instance, this can be done by marking a [block] with a context
 76→     *   that cannot be instantiated outside of this class:
 77→     *
 78→     *       suspend fun<T> withQueue(block: context(Queue) () -> T): T {

 81→     *          }

 83→     *
 84→     *   Then, only functions that are also marked can be run inside the block:
 85→     *
 86→     *       context(Queue) suspend fun canOnlyBeRunInWithQueue()
 87→     */
 88→    private suspend fun<T> withQueue(@WorkerThread block: CollectionManager.() -> T): T {
 89→        return withContext(queue) {
 90→            this@CollectionManager.block()
 91→        }
 92→    }

All changes have been successfully implemented. Let me provide a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

I've implemented the fix for improving the "discard changes" dialog options. Here's what was changed:

### 1. `DiscardChangesDialog.kt`
- Changed from a `class` with a `companion object` to an `object`
- Modified `showDialog` function to accept custom parameters:
  - `positiveButtonText: String` - For the "Discard" button
  - `negativeButtonText: String` - For the "Keep Editing" button  
  - `message: String` - For the dialog message
- Removed unused `R` import since strings are now passed as parameters

### 2. All callers updated to use new signature with backend translations (TR):
- **`NoteEditor.kt`** - Updated to use `TR.addingKeepEditing()` and `TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()`
- **`CardTemplateEditor.kt`** - Added `TR` import and updated the call
- **`CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt`** - Added `TR` import and updated the call
- **`DeckOptions.kt`** - Updated to use `CollectionManager.TR` translations
- **`EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt`** - Added `CollectionManager` import and updated the call

### 3. `CardTemplateEditorTest.kt`
- Updated test assertions to expect `"Discard current input?"` message instead of the old resource string

### Result
The dialog will now show:
- **Message**: "Discard current input?" (from `TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()`)
- **Positive button**: "Discard" (from `R.string.discard`)
- **Negative button**: "Keep editing" (from `TR.addingKeepEditing()`)

This matches the Anki Desktop behavior and provides clearer button options than the generic "OK" and "Cancel" buttons.

 30→import java.io.BufferedReader
 31→import java.io.IOException
 32→import java.io.InputStream
 33→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 34→
 35→class HtmlGenerator(
 36→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 37→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 38→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 39→    private val showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean,
 40→    val resources: Resources
 41→) {
 42→
 43→    @CheckResult
 44→    fun generateHtml(col: Collection, card: Card, side: SingleCardSide): CardHtml {
 45→        return CardHtml.createInstance(col, card, side, this)
 46→    }
 47→
 48→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: SingleCardSide): String {
 49→        return when (side) {
 50→            SingleCardSide.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 51→            SingleCardSide.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 52→        }
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 56→        return if (showAudioPlayButtons) {
 57→            Sound.expandSounds(content)
 58→        } else {
 59→            stripAvRefs(content)
 60→        }
 61→    }
 62→
 63→    companion object {
 64→        fun createInstance(
 65→            context: Context,
 66→            col: Collection,
 67→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 68→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 69→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 70→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 71→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 72→            val showAudioPlayButtons = !col.config.getBool(ConfigKey.Bool.HIDE_AUDIO_PLAY_BUTTONS)
 73→            return HtmlGenerator(
 74→                typeAnswer,
 75→                cardAppearance,
 76→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 77→                showAudioPlayButtons,
 78→                context.resources
 79→            )
 80→        }
 81→
 82→        /**
 83→         * Load the template for the card
 84→         */
 85→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 86→            try {
 87→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 88→                return CardTemplate(data)
 89→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 90→                Timber.w(e)
 91→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 92→            }
 93→        }
 94→
 95→        /**
 96→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 97→         *
 98→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 99→         * @return String version of the InputStream
100→         */
101→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
102→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
103→            try {
104→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
105→                var line: String?
106→                val sb = StringBuilder()
107→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
108→                    sb.append(line)
109→                }
110→                rd.close()
111→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
112→            } catch (e: Exception) {
113→                Timber.w(e)
114→            }
115→            return contentOfMyInputStream
116→        }
117→    }
118→}

2. The `HtmlGenerator.filterT...]
Now I have a clear understanding of the bug. The issue is:

1. When `useInputTag` is `true`, the typed answer comes from the WebView's `<input>` field
2. The value is supposed to be sent via JavaScript signals (`taBlur` or `taEnter`)
3. But when clicking "Show Answer" directly, the `taBlur` event may not fire or may not be processed in time before `displayCardAnswer()` generates the answer HTML

The fix needs to explicitly retrieve the input value from the WebView when `useInputTag` is true, before generating the answer HTML.

Let me implement the fix:

[thinking: The implementation looks good. Let me summarize the changes:1. **Root Cause**: When the "Type answer into the card" advanced option (`useInputTag`) is enabled, the typed answer needs to come from the ...]
The fix has been implemented. Let me provide a summary of the changes:

## Summary

**Root Cause:** When the "Type answer into the card" advanced option (`useInputTag`) is enabled, the typed answer comes from a WebView `<input>` field. The JavaScript signals (`taBlur`/`typeblurtext`) that transmit this value may not have been processed yet when the "Show Answer" button is clicked, resulting in an empty typed answer and no comparison being shown.

**Fix:** Modified `displayCardAnswer()` in `AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` to explicitly retrieve the typed answer from the WebView input field using `evaluateJavascript` when `useInputTag` is enabled.

### Changes Made:

1. **Added explicit input retrieval when `useInputTag` is true:**
   - When `useInputTag` is enabled AND there's a type answer field (`correct != null`) AND the webView exists, we use `evaluateJavascript` to get the current value from the input element with id `typeans`
   - The JavaScript safely handles the case where the input element doesn't exist

2. **Refactored the answer display flow:**
   - Created a new private method `displayCardAnswerAfterInput()` that contains the code to generate and display the answer HTML
   - This method is called either:
     - After the `evaluateJavascript` callback returns the typed input (when `useInputTag` is true)
     - Directly (when `useInputTag` is false or the conditions aren't met)

3. **Proper handling of the returned JavaScript value:**
   - The value from `evaluateJavascript` is returned as a JSON-encoded string (with quotes)
   - The code properly parses this by removing surrounding quotes and unescaping special characters

This fix ensures that the typed answer comparison is shown correctly when the "Type answer into the card" option is enabled, matching the expected behavior shown in the issue.

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.175 (commit: 3f67d1d8).

## Issue to Resolve

Add option to Redo Answer Card
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Sometimes, I accidentally click on the Undo button in the reviewer, which undoes the rating of the last card I reviewed.
This happens especially when I am editing a particular card during review because the location of the ✓ button of the edit note window coincides with the location of the undo button.

Because AnkiDroid doesn't have the Redo button, I have to rethink which answer button should be used for rating that card.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
This problem can be solved by adding a Redo option, just like Desktop Anki has.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Really liked the feature request. I'd try to implement it  
I've been working a bit on implementing this feature and had some questions for other contributers. Would a redo button like this also stay active while the whiteboard is up to redo strokes? I believe that would make the most sense, but I want to see if anyone has any objections. 
So, as of #14171, general redo suport should be practical to implement. The whiteboard is another matter, and may need its own undo/redo buttons.
Im gonna do it

17→package com.ichi2.anki

19→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentActivity

21→import com.google.android.material.snackbar.Snackbar
22→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.TR

27→/** If there's an action pending in the review queue, undo it and show a snackbar */
28→suspend fun FragmentActivity.undoAndShowSnackbar(duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT) {
29→    withProgress {
30→        val changes = undoableOp {
31→            if (!undoAvailable()) {
32→                OpChangesAfterUndo.getDefaultInstance()
33→            } else {
34→                undo()
35→            }
36→        }
37→        val message = if (changes.operation.isEmpty()) {
38→            TR.actionsNothingToUndo()
39→        } else {
40→            TR.undoActionUndone(changes.operation)
41→        }
42→        showSnackbar(message, duration)
43→    }
44→}

 36→enum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 37→    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),
 38→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.answer_again),
 39→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2(R.string.answer_hard),

 45→    BURY_CARD(R.string.menu_bury_card),
 46→    SUSPEND_CARD(R.string.menu_suspend_card),
 47→    DELETE(R.string.menu_delete_note),
 48→    PLAY_MEDIA(R.string.gesture_play),
 49→    EXIT(R.string.gesture_abort_learning),
 50→    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note),
 51→    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note),
 52→    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red),
 53→    TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE(R.string.gesture_flag_orange),
 54→    TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN(R.string.gesture_flag_green),
 55→    TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE(R.string.gesture_flag_blue),
 56→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK(R.string.gesture_flag_pink),
 57→    TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE(R.string.gesture_flag_turquoise),
 58→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE(R.string.gesture_flag_purple),
 59→    UNSET_FLAG(R.string.gesture_flag_remove),
 60→    PAGE_UP(R.string.gesture_page_up),
 61→    PAGE_DOWN(R.string.gesture_page_down),
 62→    TAG(R.string.add_tag),
 63→    CARD_INFO(R.string.card_info_title),
 64→    ABORT_AND_SYNC(R.string.gesture_abort_sync),
 65→    RECORD_VOICE(R.string.record_voice),
 66→    REPLAY_VOICE(R.string.replay_voice),
 67→    TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD(R.string.gesture_toggle_whiteboard),
 68→    CLEAR_WHITEBOARD(R.string.clear_whiteboard),
 69→    CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR(R.string.title_whiteboard_editor),
 70→    SHOW_HINT(R.string.gesture_show_hint),
 71→    SHOW_ALL_HINTS(R.string.gesture_show_all_hints),
 72→    ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note),
 73→    RESCHEDULE_NOTE(R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card);
 74→
 75→    companion object {
 76→        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 77→            get() = Arrays.stream(values())
 78→                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 79→                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    val preferenceKey: String
 83→        get() = "binding_$name"
 84→
 85→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 86→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 87→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 88→            collection.remove(element)
 89→            collection.add(0, element)
 90→            true
 91→        }
 92→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 96→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 97→            // do not reorder the elements
 98→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
 99→                return@BiFunction false
100→            }
101→            collection.add(element)
102→            return@BiFunction true
103→        }
104→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
108→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
109→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
110→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
111→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
112→    }
113→
114→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
115→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
116→        bindings.remove(binding)
117→        prefs.edit {
118→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
123→    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
124→        get() = // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
125→            when (this) {
126→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> from(
127→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
128→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
129→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
130→                )
131→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> from(
132→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
133→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
134→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
135→                )
136→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> from(
137→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
138→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
139→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
140→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
141→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
142→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
143→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
144→                )
145→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> from(
146→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
147→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
148→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
149→                )
150→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
151→                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
152→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
153→                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
154→                SUSPEND_CARD -> from(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
155→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> from(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
156→                PLAY_MEDIA -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
157→                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
158→                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
159→                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
160→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
161→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
162→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
163→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
164→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
165→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
166→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
167→                SHOW_HINT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
168→                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
169→                ADD_NOTE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
170→                else -> ArrayList()
171→            }

173→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
174→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
175→    }
176→
177→    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
178→        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {
182→        return ArrayList(listOf(*bindings))
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun keyCode(keyCode: Int, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
186→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keyCode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))

  96→open class Reviewer :
  97→    AbstractFlashcardViewer(),
  98→    ReviewerUi {
  99→    var queueState: CurrentQueueState? = null
 100→    val customSchedulingKey = TimeManager.time.intTimeMS().toString()
 101→    private var mHasDrawerSwipeConflicts = false
 102→    private var mShowWhiteboard = true
 103→    private var mPrefFullscreenReview = false
 104→    private lateinit var mColorPalette: LinearLayout
 105→    private var toggleStylus = false
 106→
 107→    // TODO: Consider extracting to ViewModel
 108→    // Card counts
 109→    private var mNewCount: SpannableString? = null
 110→    private var mLrnCount: SpannableString? = null
 111→    private var mRevCount: SpannableString? = null
 112→    private lateinit var mTextBarNew: TextView
 113→    private lateinit var mTextBarLearn: TextView
 114→    private lateinit var mTextBarReview: TextView
 115→    private lateinit var answerTimer: AnswerTimer
 116→    private var mPrefHideDueCount = false
 117→
 118→    // Whiteboard
 119→    var prefWhiteboard = false
 120→
 121→    @get:CheckResult
 122→    @get:VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
 123→    var whiteboard: Whiteboard? = null
 124→        protected set
 125→
 126→    // Record Audio
 127→    /** File of the temporary mic record  */
 128→    @get:VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
 129→    var audioView: AudioView? = null
 130→        protected set
 131→    private var tempAudioPath: String? = null
 132→
 133→    // ETA
 134→    private var mEta = 0
 135→    private var mPrefShowETA = false
 136→
 137→    /** Handle Mark/Flag state of cards  */
 138→    private var mCardMarker: CardMarker? = null
 139→
 140→    // Preferences from the collection
 141→    private var mShowRemainingCardCount = false
 142→    private val mActionButtons = ActionButtons(this)
 143→    private lateinit var mToolbar: Toolbar
 144→
 145→    @VisibleForTesting
 146→    protected val mProcessor = PeripheralKeymap(this, this)
 147→    private val mOnboarding = Onboarding.Reviewer(this)
 148→
 149→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 150→        if (showedActivityFailedScreen(savedInstanceState)) {
 151→            return
 152→        }
 153→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 154→        if (handledLaunchFromWebBrowser(intent, this)) {
 155→            this.setResult(RESULT_CANCELED)
 156→            finishWithAnimation(ActivityTransitionAnimation.Direction.END)
 157→            return
 158→        }
 159→        mColorPalette = findViewById(R.id.whiteboard_editor)
 160→        answerTimer = AnswerTimer(findViewById(R.id.card_time))
 161→        mTextBarNew = findViewById(R.id.new_number)
 162→        mTextBarLearn = findViewById(R.id.learn_number)
 163→        mTextBarReview = findViewById(R.id.review_number)
 164→        mToolbar = findViewById(R.id.toolbar)
 165→
 166→        startLoadingCollection()
 167→    }
 168→
 169→    override fun onPause() {
 170→        answerTimer.pause()
 171→        super.onPause()
 172→    }
 173→
 174→    override fun onResume() {
 175→        answerTimer.resume()
 176→        super.onResume()
 177→        if (answerField != null) {
 178→            answerField!!.focusWithKeyboard()
 179→        }
 180→    }
 181→
 182→    protected val flagToDisplay: Int
 183→        get() {
 184→            return FlagToDisplay(
 185→                currentCard!!.userFlag(),
 186→                mActionButtons.findMenuItem(ActionButtons.RES_FLAG)?.isActionButton ?: true,
 187→                mPrefFullscreenReview
 188→            ).get()
 189→        }
 190→
 191→    override fun recreateWebView() {
 192→        super.recreateWebView()
 193→        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 194→    }
 195→
 196→    @NeedsTest("is hidden if marked is on app bar")
 197→    @NeedsTest("is not hidden if marked is not on app bar")
 198→    @NeedsTest("is not hidden if marked is on app bar and fullscreen is enabled")
 199→    override fun shouldDisplayMark(): Boolean {
 200→        val markValue = super.shouldDisplayMark()
 201→        if (!markValue) {
 202→            return false
 203→        }
 204→
 205→        // If we don't know: assume it's not shown
 206→        val shownAsToolbarButton = mActionButtons.findMenuItem(ActionButtons.RES_MARK)?.isActionButton == true
 207→        return !shownAsToolbarButton || mPrefFullscreenReview
 208→    }
 209→
 210→    protected open fun onMark(card: Card?) {
 211→        if (card == null) {
 212→            return
 213→        }
 214→        launchCatchingTask {
 215→            toggleMark(card.note())
 216→            refreshActionBar()
 217→            onMarkChanged()
 218→        }
 219→    }
 220→
 221→    private fun onMarkChanged() {
 222→        if (currentCard == null) {
 223→            return
 224→        }
 225→        mCardMarker!!.displayMark(shouldDisplayMark())
 226→    }
 227→
 228→    protected open fun onFlag(card: Card?, flag: Int) {
 229→        if (card == null) {
 230→            return
 231→        }
 232→        launchCatchingTask {
 233→            card.setUserFlag(flag)
 234→            withCol {
 235→                setUserFlagForCards(listOf(card.id), flag)
 236→            }
 237→            refreshActionBar()
 238→            onFlagChanged()
 239→        }
 240→    }
 241→
 242→    private fun onFlagChanged() {
 243→        if (currentCard == null) {
 244→            return
 245→        }
 246→        mCardMarker!!.displayFlag(flagToDisplay)
 247→    }
 248→
 249→    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
 250→        val extras = intent.extras
 251→        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
 252→            // deckId is not set, load default
 253→            return
 254→        }
 255→        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
 256→        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
 257→
 258→        // deckId does not exist, load default
 259→        if (getColUnsafe.decks.get(did) == null) {
 260→            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
 261→            return
 262→        }
 263→        // Select the deck
 264→        getColUnsafe.decks.select(did)
 265→    }
 266→
 267→    override fun getContentViewAttr(fullscreenMode: FullScreenMode): Int {
 268→        return when (fullscreenMode) {
 269→            FullScreenMode.BUTTONS_ONLY -> R.layout.reviewer_fullscreen
 270→            FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE -> R.layout.reviewer_fullscreen_noanswers
 271→            else -> R.layout.reviewer
 272→        }
 273→    }
 274→
 275→    public override fun fitsSystemWindows(): Boolean {
 276→        return !fullscreenMode.isFullScreenReview()
 277→    }
 278→
 279→    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
 280→        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
 281→        if (Intent.ACTION_VIEW == intent.action) {
 282→            Timber.d("onCreate() :: received Intent with action = %s", intent.action)
 283→            selectDeckFromExtra()
 284→        }
 285→        // Load the first card and start reviewing. Uses the answer card
 286→        // task to load a card, but since we send null
 287→        // as the card to answer, no card will be answered.
 288→        prefWhiteboard = MetaDB.getWhiteboardState(this, parentDid)
 289→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 290→            // DEFECT: Slight inefficiency here, as we set the database using these methods
 291→            val whiteboardVisibility = MetaDB.getWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid)
 292→            setWhiteboardEnabledState(true)
 293→            setWhiteboardVisibility(whiteboardVisibility)
 294→            toggleStylus = MetaDB.getWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid)
 295→            whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus
 296→        }
 297→        launchCatchingTask { updateCardAndRedraw() }
 298→        disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts()
 299→
 300→        // Set full screen/immersive mode if needed
 301→        if (mPrefFullscreenReview) {
 302→            setFullScreen(this)
 303→        }
 304→        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 305→    }
 306→
 307→    override fun onOptionsItemSelected(item: MenuItem): Boolean {
 308→        // 100ms was not enough on my device (Honor 9 Lite -  Android Pie)
 309→        delayedHide(1000)
 310→        if (drawerToggle.onOptionsItemSelected(item)) {
 311→            return true
 312→        }
 313→        when (item.itemId) {
 314→            android.R.id.home -> {
 315→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Home button pressed")
 316→                closeReviewer(RESULT_OK)
 317→            }
 318→            R.id.action_undo -> {
 319→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Undo button pressed")
 320→                if (mShowWhiteboard && whiteboard != null && !whiteboard!!.undoEmpty()) {
 321→                    whiteboard!!.undo()
 322→                } else {

 324→                }
 325→            }
 326→            R.id.action_reset_card_progress -> {
 327→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Reset progress button pressed")

 329→            }
 330→            R.id.action_mark_card -> {
 331→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Mark button pressed")
 332→                onMark(currentCard)
 333→            }
 334→            R.id.action_replay -> {
 335→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Replay audio button pressed (from menu)")
 336→                playSounds(true)
 337→            }
 338→            R.id.action_toggle_mic_tool_bar -> {

 342→            }
 343→            R.id.action_tag -> {
 344→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Tag button pressed")

 346→            }
 347→            R.id.action_edit -> {
 348→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Edit note button pressed")
 349→                editCard()
 350→            }
 351→            R.id.action_bury -> {
 352→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Bury button pressed")

 354→                    if (!isAvailable) {

 445→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Flag six")
 446→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_TURQUOISE)
 447→            }
 448→            R.id.action_flag_seven -> {

 452→            R.id.action_card_info -> {

 554→    private fun openOrToggleMicToolbar() {
 555→        if (!canRecordAudio(this)) {
 556→            ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(
 557→                this,
 558→                arrayOf(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO),
 559→                REQUEST_AUDIO_PERMISSION
 560→            )

 566→    private fun toggleMicToolBar() {
 567→        audioView?.let {
 568→            it.visibility = if (it.visibility != View.VISIBLE) View.VISIBLE else View.GONE
 569→            return
 570→        }
 571→        // Record mic tool bar does not exist yet
 572→        tempAudioPath = generateTempAudioFile(this)
 573→        if (tempAudioPath == null) {
 574→            return
 575→        }
 576→        audioView = createRecorderInstance(
 577→            this,
 578→            R.drawable.ic_play_arrow_white_24dp,
 579→            R.drawable.ic_pause_white_24dp,
 580→            R.drawable.ic_stop_white_24dp,
 581→            R.drawable.ic_rec,
 582→            R.drawable.ic_rec_stop,
 583→            tempAudioPath!!
 584→        )
 585→        if (audioView == null) {
 586→            tempAudioPath = null
 587→            return
 588→        }
 589→        val lp2 = FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
 590→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
 591→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
 592→        )
 593→        audioView!!.layoutParams = lp2
 594→        val micToolBarLayer = findViewById<LinearLayout>(R.id.mic_tool_bar_layer)
 595→        micToolBarLayer.addView(audioView)
 596→    }

 659→    // Related to https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/11061#issuecomment-1107868455
 660→    @NeedsTest("Order of operations needs Testing around Menu (Overflow) Icons and their colors.")
 661→    override fun onCreateOptionsMenu(menu: Menu): Boolean {
 662→        Timber.d("onCreateOptionsMenu()")
 663→        // NOTE: This is called every time a new question is shown via invalidate options menu
 664→        menuInflater.inflate(R.menu.reviewer, menu)
 665→        displayIcons(menu)
 666→        mActionButtons.setCustomButtonsStatus(menu)
 667→        var alpha = if (super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 668→        val markCardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_mark_card)
 669→        if (currentCard != null && isMarked(currentCard!!.note())) {
 670→            markCardIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_unmark_note).setIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_white)
 671→        } else {
 672→            markCardIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_mark_note).setIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_border_white)
 673→        }
 674→        markCardIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 675→
 676→        val flagIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_flag)
 677→        if (flagIcon != null) {
 678→            if (currentCard != null) {
 679→                when (currentCard!!.userFlag()) {
 680→                    1 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_red)
 681→                    2 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_orange)
 682→                    3 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_green)
 683→                    4 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_blue)
 684→                    5 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_pink)
 685→                    6 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_turquoise)
 686→                    7 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_purple)
 687→                    else -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_transparent)
 688→                }
 689→            }
 690→            flagIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 691→        }
 692→
 693→        // Undo button
 694→        @DrawableRes val undoIconId: Int
 695→        val undoEnabled: Boolean
 696→        val whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes = mShowWhiteboard && whiteboard?.undoEmpty() == false
 697→        if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {
 698→            undoIconId = R.drawable.eraser
 699→            undoEnabled = true
 700→        } else {
 701→            undoIconId = R.drawable.ic_undo_white
 702→            undoEnabled = colIsOpenUnsafe() && getColUnsafe.undoAvailable()
 703→        }
 704→        val alphaUndo = if (undoEnabled && super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 705→        val undoIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_undo)
 706→        undoIcon.setIcon(undoIconId)
 707→        undoIcon.setEnabled(undoEnabled).iconAlpha = alphaUndo
 708→        undoIcon.actionView!!.isEnabled = undoEnabled
 709→        if (colIsOpenUnsafe()) { // Required mostly because there are tests where `col` is null
 710→            if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {
 711→                undoIcon.title = resources.getString(R.string.undo_action_whiteboard_last_stroke)
 712→            } else if (getColUnsafe.undoAvailable()) {
 713→                undoIcon.title = getColUnsafe.undoLabel()
 714→                //  e.g. Undo Bury, Undo Change Deck, Undo Update Note
 715→            } else {
 716→                // In this case, there is no object word for the verb, "Undo",
 717→                // so in some languages such as Japanese, which have pre/post-positional particle with the object,
 718→                // we need to use the string for just "Undo" instead of the string for "Undo %s".
 719→                undoIcon.title = resources.getString(R.string.undo)
 720→            }
 721→        }
 722→        if (undoEnabled) {
 723→            mOnboarding.onUndoButtonEnabled()
 724→        }
 725→        val toggleWhiteboardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_toggle_whiteboard)
 726→        val toggleStylusIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_toggle_stylus)
 727→        val hideWhiteboardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_hide_whiteboard)
 728→        val changePenColorIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color)
 729→        // White board button
 730→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 731→            // Configure the whiteboard related items in the action bar
 732→            toggleWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.disable_whiteboard)
 733→            // Always allow "Disable Whiteboard", even if "Enable Whiteboard" is disabled
 734→            toggleWhiteboardIcon.isVisible = true
 735→            if (!mActionButtons.status.toggleStylusIsDisabled()) {
 736→                toggleStylusIcon.isVisible = true
 737→            }
 738→            if (!mActionButtons.status.hideWhiteboardIsDisabled()) {
 739→                hideWhiteboardIcon.isVisible = true
 740→            }
 741→            if (!mActionButtons.status.clearWhiteboardIsDisabled()) {
 742→                menu.findItem(R.id.action_clear_whiteboard).isVisible = true
 743→            }
 744→            if (!mActionButtons.status.saveWhiteboardIsDisabled()) {
 745→                menu.findItem(R.id.action_save_whiteboard).isVisible = true
 746→            }
 747→            if (!mActionButtons.status.whiteboardPenColorIsDisabled()) {
 748→                changePenColorIcon.isVisible = true
 749→            }
 750→            val whiteboardIcon = ContextCompat.getDrawable(this, R.drawable.ic_gesture_white)!!.mutate()
 751→            val stylusIcon = ContextCompat.getDrawable(this, R.drawable.ic_gesture_stylus)!!.mutate()
 752→            val whiteboardColorPaletteIcon = VectorDrawableCompat.create(resources, R.drawable.ic_color_lens_white_24dp, this.theme)!!.mutate()
 753→            if (mShowWhiteboard) {
 754→                whiteboardIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 755→                hideWhiteboardIcon.icon = whiteboardIcon
 756→                hideWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.hide_whiteboard)
 757→                whiteboardColorPaletteIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 758→                changePenColorIcon.icon = whiteboardColorPaletteIcon
 759→                if (toggleStylus) {
 760→                    toggleStylusIcon.setTitle(R.string.disable_stylus)
 761→                    stylusIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 762→                } else {
 763→                    toggleStylusIcon.setTitle(R.string.enable_stylus)
 764→                    stylusIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 765→                }
 766→                toggleStylusIcon.icon = stylusIcon
 767→            } else {
 768→                whiteboardIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 769→                hideWhiteboardIcon.icon = whiteboardIcon
 770→                hideWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.show_whiteboard)
 771→                whiteboardColorPaletteIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 772→                stylusIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 773→                toggleStylusIcon.isEnabled = false
 774→                toggleStylusIcon.icon = stylusIcon
 775→                changePenColorIcon.isEnabled = false
 776→                changePenColorIcon.icon = whiteboardColorPaletteIcon
 777→                mColorPalette.visibility = View.GONE
 778→            }
 779→        } else {
 780→            toggleWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.enable_whiteboard)
 781→        }
 782→        if (colIsOpenUnsafe() && getColUnsafe.decks.isDyn(parentDid)) {
 783→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_open_deck_options).isVisible = false
 784→        }
 785→        if (mTTS.enabled && !mActionButtons.status.selectTtsIsDisabled()) {
 786→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_select_tts).isVisible = true
 787→        }
 788→        // Setup bury / suspend providers
 789→        val suspendIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_suspend)
 790→        val buryIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_bury)
 791→        MenuItemCompat.setActionProvider(suspendIcon, SuspendProvider(this))
 792→        MenuItemCompat.setActionProvider(buryIcon, BuryProvider(this))
 793→        if (suspendNoteAvailable()) {
 794→            suspendIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_action_suspend_dropdown)
 795→            suspendIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_suspend)
 796→        } else {
 797→            suspendIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_pause_circle_outline)
 798→            suspendIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_suspend_card)
 799→        }
 800→        if (buryNoteAvailable()) {
 801→            buryIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flip_to_back_dropdown)
 802→            buryIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_bury)
 803→        } else {
 804→            buryIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flip_to_back_white)
 805→            buryIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_bury_card)
 806→        }
 807→        alpha = if (super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 808→        buryIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 809→        suspendIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 810→        MenuItemCompat.setActionProvider(menu.findItem(R.id.action_schedule), ScheduleProvider(this))
 811→        mOnboarding.onCreate()
 812→
 813→        increaseHorizontalPaddingOfOverflowMenuIcons(menu)
 814→        tintOverflowMenuIcons(menu, skipIf = { isFlagResource(it.itemId) })
 815→
 816→        return super.onCreateOptionsMenu(menu)
 817→    }
