 65→
 66→open class AnkiDroidJsAPI(
 67→    private val activity: AbstractFlashcardViewer,
 68→) {
 69→    private val currentCard: Card
 70→        get() = activity.currentCard!!
 71→
 72→    private val getColUnsafe: Collection
 73→        get() = activity.getColUnsafe
 74→
 75→    /**
 76→     Javascript Interface class for calling Java function from AnkiDroid WebView
 77→     see js-api.js for available functions
 78→     */
 79→
 80→    private val context: Context = activity
 81→
 82→    // Text to speech
 83→    private val talker = JavaScriptTTS()
 84→
 85→    // Speech to Text
 86→    private val speechRecognizer = JavaScriptSTT(context)
 87→
 88→    open fun convertToByteArray(
 89→        apiContract: ApiContract,
 90→        boolean: Boolean,
 91→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.Boolean(apiContract.isValid, boolean).toString().toByteArray()
 92→
 93→    open fun convertToByteArray(
 94→        apiContract: ApiContract,
 95→        int: Int,
 96→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.Integer(apiContract.isValid, int).toString().toByteArray()
 97→
 98→    open fun convertToByteArray(
 99→        apiContract: ApiContract,
100→        long: Long,
101→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.Long(apiContract.isValid, long).toString().toByteArray()
102→
103→    open fun convertToByteArray(
104→        apiContract: ApiContract,
105→        string: String,
106→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.String(apiContract.isValid, string).toString().toByteArray()
107→
108→    /**
109→     * The method parse json data and return api contract object
110→     * @param byteArray
111→     * @return apiContract or null
112→     */
113→    private fun parseJsApiContract(byteArray: ByteArray): ApiContract? {
114→        try {
115→            val data = JSONObject(byteArray.decodeToString())
116→            val cardSuppliedApiVersion = data.optString("version", "")
117→            val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact = data.optString("developer", "")
118→            val cardSuppliedData = data.optString("data", "")
119→            val isValid = requireApiVersion(cardSuppliedApiVersion, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
120→            return ApiContract(isValid, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact, cardSuppliedData)
121→        } catch (j: JSONException) {
122→            Timber.w(j)
123→            activity.runOnUiThread {
124→                activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, j.localizedMessage))
125→            }
126→        }
127→        return null
128→    }
129→
130→    /*
131→     * see 02-strings.xml
132→     * Show Error code when mark card or flag card unsupported
133→     * 1 - mark card
134→     * 2 - flag card
135→     *
136→     * show developer contact if js api used in card is deprecated
137→     */
138→    private fun showDeveloperContact(
139→        errorCode: Int,
140→        apiDevContact: String,
141→    ) {
142→        val errorMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.anki_js_error_code, errorCode)
143→        val snackbarMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.api_version_developer_contact, apiDevContact, errorMsg)
144→
145→        activity.showSnackbar(snackbarMsg, Snackbar.LENGTH_INDEFINITE) {
146→            setMaxLines(3)
147→            setAction(R.string.reviewer_invalid_api_version_visit_documentation) {
148→                activity.openUrl(Uri.parse("https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki"))
149→            }
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    /**
154→     * Supplied api version must be equal to current api version to call mark card, toggle flag functions etc.
155→     */
156→    private fun requireApiVersion(
157→        apiVer: String,
158→        apiDevContact: String,
159→    ): Boolean {
160→        try {
161→            if (apiDevContact.isEmpty() || apiVer.isEmpty()) {
162→                activity.runOnUiThread {
163→                    activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, ""))
164→                }
165→                return false
166→            }
167→            val versionCurrent = Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.CURRENT_JS_API_VERSION)
168→            val versionSupplied = Version.parse(apiVer)
169→
170→            /*
171→             * if api major version equals to supplied major version then return true and also check for minor version and patch version
172→             * show toast for update and contact developer if need updates
173→             * otherwise return false
174→             */
175→            return when {
176→                versionSupplied == versionCurrent -> {
177→                    true
178→                }
179→                versionSupplied.isLowerThan(versionCurrent) -> {
180→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
181→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.update_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
182→                    }
183→                    versionSupplied.isHigherThanOrEquivalentTo(Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.MINIMUM_JS_API_VERSION))
184→                }
185→                else -> {
186→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
187→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.valid_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
188→                    }
189→                    false
190→                }
191→            }
192→        } catch (e: Exception) {
193→            Timber.w(e, "requireApiVersion::exception")
194→        }
195→        return false
196→    }
197→
198→    /**
199→     * Handle js api request,
200→     * some of the methods are overridden in Reviewer.kt and default values are returned.
201→     * @param methodName
202→     * @param bytes
203→     * @param returnDefaultValues `true` if default values should be returned (if non-[Reviewer])
204→     * @return
205→     */
206→    open suspend fun handleJsApiRequest(
207→        methodName: String,
208→        bytes: ByteArray,
209→        returnDefaultValues: Boolean = true,
210→    ) = withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
211→        // the method will call to set the card supplied data and is valid version for each api request
212→        val apiContract = parseJsApiContract(bytes)!!
213→        // if api not init or is api not called from reviewer then return default -1
214→        // also other action will not be modified
215→        if (!apiContract.isValid or returnDefaultValues) {
216→            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, -1)
217→        }
218→
219→        val cardDataForJsAPI = activity.getCardDataForJsApi()
220→        val apiParams = apiContract.cardSuppliedData
221→
222→        return@withContext when (methodName) {
223→            "init" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
224→            "newCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.newCardCount)
225→            "lrnCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.lrnCardCount)
226→            "revCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.revCardCount)
227→            "eta" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.eta)
228→            "nextTime1" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime1)
229→            "nextTime2" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime2)
230→            "nextTime3" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime3)
231→            "nextTime4" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime4)
232→            "toggleFlag" -> {
233→                if (apiParams !in flagCommands) {
234→                    showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_FLAG_CARD, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
235→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
236→                }
237→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.executeCommand(flagCommands[apiParams]!!))
238→            }
239→            "markCard" ->
240→                processAction({
241→                    activity.executeCommand(ViewerCommand.MARK)
242→                }, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_MARK_CARD, ::convertToByteArray)
243→            "buryCard" -> processAction(activity::buryCard, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_BURY_CARD, ::convertToByteArray)
244→            "buryNote" -> processAction(activity::buryNote, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_BURT_NOTE, ::convertToByteArray)
245→            "suspendCard" -> processAction(activity::suspendCard, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SUSPEND_CARD, ::convertToByteArray)
246→            "suspendNote" -> processAction(activity::suspendNote, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SUSPEND_NOTE, ::convertToByteArray)
247→            "setCardDue" -> {
248→                try {
249→                    val days = apiParams.toInt()
250→                    if (days < 0 || days > 9999) {
251→                        showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SET_DUE, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
252→                        return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
253→                    }
254→                    activity.launchCatchingTask {
255→                        activity.rescheduleCards(listOf(currentCard.id), days)
256→                    }
257→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
258→                } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
259→                    showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SET_DUE, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
260→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
261→                }
262→            }
263→            "resetProgress" -> {
264→                val cardIds = listOf(currentCard.id)
265→                activity.launchCatchingTask { activity.resetCards(cardIds) }
266→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
267→            }
268→            "cardMark" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.note(getColUnsafe).hasTag(getColUnsafe, "marked"))
269→            "cardFlag" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.userFlag().code)
270→            "cardReps" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.reps)
271→            "cardInterval" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.ivl)
272→            "cardFactor" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.factor)
273→            "cardMod" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.mod)
274→            "cardId" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.id)
275→            "cardNid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.nid)
276→            "cardType" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.type.code)
277→            "cardDid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.did)
278→            "cardLeft" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.left)
279→            "cardODid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.oDid)
280→            "cardODue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.oDue)
281→            "cardQueue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.queue.code)
282→            "cardLapses" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.lapses)
283→            "cardDue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.due)
284→            "deckName" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, Decks.basename(activity.getColUnsafe.decks.name(currentCard.did)))
285→            "isActiveNetworkMetered" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, NetworkUtils.isActiveNetworkMetered())
286→            "ttsSetLanguage" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setLanguage(apiParams))
287→            "ttsSpeak" -> {
288→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
289→                val text = jsonObject.getString("text")
290→                val queueMode = jsonObject.getInt("queueMode")
291→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.speak(text, queueMode))
292→            }
293→            "ttsIsSpeaking" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.isSpeaking)
294→            "ttsSetPitch" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setPitch(apiParams.toFloat()))
295→            "ttsSetSpeechRate" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setSpeechRate(apiParams.toFloat()))
296→            "ttsFieldModifierIsAvailable" -> {
297→                // Know if {{tts}} is supported - issue #10443
298→                // Return false for now
299→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
300→            }
301→            "ttsStop" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.stop())
302→            "searchCard" -> {
303→                val intent =
304→                    Intent(context, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
305→                        putExtra("currentCard", currentCard.id)
306→                        putExtra("search_query", apiParams)
307→                    }
308→                activity.startActivity(intent)
309→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
310→            }
311→            "searchCardWithCallback" -> ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract)
312→            "isDisplayingAnswer" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isDisplayingAnswer)
313→            "addTagToCard" -> {
314→                activity.runOnUiThread { activity.showTagsDialog() }
315→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
316→            }
317→            "isInFullscreen" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isFullscreen)
318→            "isTopbarShown" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.prefShowTopbar)
319→            "isInNightMode" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isInNightMode)
320→            "enableHorizontalScrollbar" -> {
321→                activity.webView!!.isHorizontalScrollBarEnabled = apiParams.toBoolean()
322→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
323→            }
324→            "enableVerticalScrollbar" -> {
325→                activity.webView!!.isVerticalScrollBarEnabled = apiParams.toBoolean()
326→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
327→            }
328→            "showNavigationDrawer" -> {
329→                activity.onNavigationPressed()
330→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
331→            }
332→            "showOptionsMenu" -> {
333→                activity.openOptionsMenu()
334→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
335→            }
336→            "showToast" -> {
337→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
338→                val text = jsonObject.getString("text")
339→                val shortLength = jsonObject.optBoolean("shortLength", true)
340→                val msgDecode = activity.decodeUrl(text)
341→                showThemedToast(context, msgDecode, shortLength)
342→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
343→            }
344→            "showAnswer" -> {
345→                activity.displayCardAnswer()
346→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
347→            }
348→            "answerEase1" -> {
349→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.AGAIN)
350→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
351→            }
352→            "answerEase2" -> {
353→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.HARD)
354→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
355→            }
356→            "answerEase3" -> {
357→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.GOOD)
358→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
359→            }
360→            "answerEase4" -> {
361→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.EASY)
362→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
363→            }
364→
365→            "addTagToNote" -> {
366→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
367→                val noteId = jsonObject.getLong("noteId")
368→                val tag = jsonObject.getString("tag")
369→                val note =
370→                    getColUnsafe.getNote(noteId).apply {
371→                        addTag(tag)
372→                    }
373→                getColUnsafe.updateNote(note)
374→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
375→            }
376→            "sttSetLanguage" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.setLanguage(apiParams))
377→            "sttStart" -> {
378→                val callback =
379→                    object : JavaScriptSTT.SpeechRecognitionCallback {
380→                        override fun onResult(results: List<String>) {
381→                            activity.lifecycleScope.launch {
382→                                val apiResult = ApiResult.success(Json.encodeToString(ListSerializer(String.serializer()), results))
383→                                val jsonEncodedString = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) { JSONObject.quote(apiResult.toString()) }
384→                                activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSttResult($jsonEncodedString)", null)
385→                            }
386→                        }
387→
388→                        override fun onError(errorMessage: String) {
389→                            activity.lifecycleScope.launch {
390→                                val apiResult = ApiResult.failure(errorMessage)
391→                                val jsonEncodedString = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) { JSONObject.quote(apiResult.toString()) }
392→                                activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSttResult($jsonEncodedString)", null)
393→                            }
394→                        }
395→                    }
396→                speechRecognizer.setRecognitionCallback(callback)
397→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.start())
398→            }
399→            "sttStop" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.stop())
400→            else -> {
401→                showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_ERROR, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
402→                throw Exception("unhandled request: $methodName")
403→            }
404→        }
405→    }
406→
407→    private fun processAction(
408→        action: () -> Boolean,
409→        apiContract: ApiContract,
410→        errorCode: Int,
411→        conversion: (ApiContract, Boolean) -> ByteArray,
412→    ): ByteArray {
413→        val status = action()
414→        if (!status) {
415→            showDeveloperContact(errorCode, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
416→        }
417→        return conversion(apiContract, status)
418→    }

420→    @NeedsTest("needs coverage")
421→    private suspend fun ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract: ApiContract): ByteArray =
422→        withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
423→            val cards =
424→                try {
425→                    searchForRows(apiContract.cardSuppliedData, SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering(), CardsOrNotes.CARDS)
426→                        .map { withCol { getCard(it.cardOrNoteId) } }
427→                } catch (exc: Exception) {
428→                    activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript(
429→                        "console.log('${context.getString(R.string.search_card_js_api_no_results)}')",
430→                        null,
431→                    )
432→                    showDeveloperContact(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SEARCH_CARD, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
433→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
434→                }
435→            val searchResult: MutableList<String> = ArrayList()
436→            for (card in cards) {
437→                val jsonObject = JSONObject()
438→                val fieldsData = card.note(getColUnsafe).fields
439→                val fieldsName = card.noteType(getColUnsafe).fieldsNames
440→
441→                val noteId = card.nid
442→                val cardId = card.id
443→                jsonObject.put("cardId", cardId)
444→                jsonObject.put("noteId", noteId)
445→
446→                val jsonFieldObject = JSONObject()
447→                fieldsName.zip(fieldsData).forEach { pair ->
448→                    jsonFieldObject.put(pair.component1(), pair.component2())
449→                }
450→                jsonObject.put("fieldsData", jsonFieldObject)
451→
452→                searchResult.add(jsonObject.toString())
453→            }
454→
455→            // quote result to prevent JSON injection attack
456→            val jsonEncodedString = JSONObject.quote(searchResult.toString())
457→            activity.runOnUiThread {
458→                activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSearchCard($jsonEncodedString)", null)
459→            }
460→            convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
461→        }

467→        var eta = -1
468→        var nextTime1 = ""
469→        var nextTime2 = ""
470→        var nextTime3 = ""
471→        var nextTime4 = ""

474→    sealed class ApiResult protected constructor(
475→        private val status: JvmBoolean,
476→    ) {
477→        class Boolean(
478→            status: JvmBoolean,
479→            val value: JvmBoolean,
480→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
481→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
482→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
483→            }
484→        }
485→
486→        class Integer(
487→            status: JvmBoolean,
488→            val value: JvmInt,
489→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
490→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
491→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
492→            }
493→        }
494→
495→        class Float(
496→            status: JvmBoolean,
497→            val value: JvmFloat,
498→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
499→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
500→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
501→            }
502→        }
503→
504→        class Long(
505→            status: JvmBoolean,
506→            val value: JvmLong,
507→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
508→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
509→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
510→            }
511→        }
512→
513→        class String(
514→            status: JvmBoolean,
515→            val value: JvmString,
516→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
517→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
518→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
519→            }
520→        }
521→
522→        abstract fun putValue(o: JSONObject)
523→
524→        override fun toString() =
525→            JSONObject()
526→                .apply {
527→                    put(SUCCESS_KEY, status)
528→                    putValue(this)
529→                }.toString()
530→
531→        @Suppress("RemoveRedundantQualifierName") // we don't want `String(true, value)`
532→        companion object {
533→            fun success(value: JvmString) = ApiResult.String(true, value)
534→
535→            fun failure(value: JvmString) = ApiResult.String(false, value)
536→        }
537→    }
538→
539→    class ApiContract(
540→        val isValid: Boolean,
541→        val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact: String,
542→        val cardSuppliedData: String,
543→    )
544→
545→    companion object {
546→        /**
547→         * Key for a success value.
548→         */
549→        const val VALUE_KEY = "value"
550→        const val SUCCESS_KEY = "success"
551→    }
552→}

42→package com.ichi2.utils

45→import org.json.JSONObject
46→
47→fun JSONArray.deepClone(): JSONArray {
48→    val clone = JSONArray()
49→    for (i in 0 until length()) {
50→        clone.put(
51→            when (get(i)) {
52→                is JSONObject -> getJSONObject(i).deepClone()
53→                is JSONArray -> getJSONArray(i).deepClone()
54→                else -> get(i)
55→            },
56→        )
57→    }
58→    return clone
59→}
60→
61→fun JSONArray.jsonObjectIterable(): Iterable<JSONObject> = Iterable { jsonObjectIterator() }
62→
63→@KotlinCleanup("see if jsonObject/string/longIterator() methods can be combined into one")
64→fun JSONArray.jsonObjectIterator(): Iterator<JSONObject> =
65→    object : Iterator<JSONObject> {
66→        private var index = 0
67→
68→        override fun hasNext(): Boolean = index < length()
69→
70→        override fun next() =
71→            getJSONObject(index).also {
72→                index++
73→            }
74→    }
75→
76→fun JSONArray.stringIterable(): Iterable<String> = Iterable { stringIterator() }
77→
78→fun JSONArray.stringIterator(): Iterator<String> {
79→    return object : Iterator<String> {
80→        private var index = 0
81→
82→        override fun hasNext(): Boolean = index < length()
83→
84→        override fun next(): String {
85→            val string = getString(index)
86→            index++
87→            return string
88→        }
89→    }
90→}
91→
92→/**
93→ * @return Given an array of objects, return the array of the value with `key`, assuming that they are String.
94→ * E.g. templates, fields are a JSONArray whose objects have name
95→ */
96→fun JSONArray.toStringList(key: String): List<String> = jsonObjectIterable().map { it.getString(key) }

 18→package com.ichi2.libanki
 19→
 20→import androidx.annotation.WorkerThread
 21→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithCount
 22→import com.ichi2.libanki.utils.join
 23→import java.util.AbstractSet
 24→
 25→/**
 26→ * Anki maintains a cache of used tags so it can quickly present a list of tags
 27→ * for autocomplete and in the browser. For efficiency, deletions are not
 28→ * tracked, so unused tags can only be removed from the list with a DB check.
 29→ *
 30→ * This module manages the tag cache and tags for notes.
 31→ *
 32→ */
 33→@WorkerThread
 34→class Tags(
 35→    private val col: Collection,
 36→) {
 37→    /** all tags */
 38→    fun all(): List<String> = col.backend.allTags()
 39→
 40→    fun byDeck(did: DeckId): List<String> =
 41→        col.backend
 42→            .customStudyDefaults(did)
 43→            .tagsList
 44→            .map { it.name }
 45→
 46→    // Legacy signature, used by unit tests.
 47→    fun bulkAdd(
 48→        ids: List<Long>,
 49→        tags: String,
 50→        add: Boolean,
 51→    ) {
 52→        if (add) {
 53→            bulkAdd(ids, tags)
 54→        } else {
 55→            bulkRemove(ids, tags)
 56→        }
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    /** Add space-separate tags to provided notes. */
 60→    fun bulkAdd(
 61→        noteIds: List<NoteId>,
 62→        tags: String,
 63→    ): OpChangesWithCount = col.backend.addNoteTags(noteIds = noteIds, tags = tags)
 64→
 65→    // Remove space-separated tags from provided notes.
 66→    fun bulkRemove(
 67→        noteIds: List<Long>,
 68→        tags: String,
 69→    ): OpChangesWithCount =
 70→        col.backend.removeNoteTags(
 71→            noteIds = noteIds,
 72→            tags = tags,
 73→        )
 74→
 75→    /*
 76→     * String-based utilities
 77→     * ***********************************************************
 78→     */
 79→
 80→    /** Parse a string and return a list of tags. */
 81→    fun split(tags: String): MutableList<String> =
 82→        tags
 83→            .replace('\u3000', ' ')
 84→            .split("\\s".toRegex())
 85→            .filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
 86→            .toMutableList()
 87→
 88→    /** Join tags into a single string, with leading and trailing spaces. */
 89→    fun join(tags: kotlin.collections.Collection<String>): String {
 90→        if (tags.isEmpty()) {
 91→            return ""
 92→        }
 93→        return " ${" ".join(tags)} "
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    /*
 97→     * List-based utilities
 98→     * ***********************************************************
 99→     */
100→
101→    /** {@inheritDoc}  */
102→    fun canonify(tagList: List<String>): AbstractSet<String> {
103→        // this is now a no-op - the tags are canonified when the note is saved
104→
105→        // libAnki difference: tagList was returned directly
106→        return HashSet(tagList)
107→    }
108→
109→    /** True if TAG is in TAGS. Ignore case.*/
110→    fun inList(
111→        tag: String,
112→        tags: Iterable<String>,
113→    ): Boolean = tags.map { it.lowercase() }.contains(tag.lowercase())
114→}
115→
116→fun Collection.completeTagRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray = backend.completeTagRaw(input)
117→

389→            val c = n.firstCard()
390→

392→            c.type = CardType.Rev
393→            c.due = 28
394→            c.factor = 2800
395→            c.ivl = 8
396→
397→            // before reset
398→            assertEquals(28, c.due, "Card due before reset")
399→            assertEquals(8, c.ivl, "Card interval before reset")
400→            assertEquals(2800, c.factor, "Card ease before reset")
401→            assertEquals(CardType.Rev, c.type, "Card type before reset")
402→
403→            val reviewer: Reviewer = startReviewer()
404→            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()
405→
406→            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi
407→
408→            // test that card reset
409→            assertThat(getDataFromRequest("resetProgress", jsapi), equalTo(formatApiResult(true)))
410→            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()
411→
412→            // verify that card progress reset
413→            // --------------------------------
414→            val cardAfterReset = col.getCard(reviewer.currentCard!!.id)
415→            assertEquals(2, cardAfterReset.due, "Card due after reset")
416→            assertEquals(0, cardAfterReset.ivl, "Card interval after reset")
417→            assertEquals(CardType.New, cardAfterReset.type, "Card type after reset")
418→        }
419→
420→    companion object {
421→        fun jsApiContract(data: String = ""): ByteArray =
422→            JSONObject()
423→                .apply {
424→                    put("version", "0.0.3")
425→                    put("developer", "test@example.com")
426→                    put("data", data)
427→                }.toString()
428→                .toByteArray()
429→
430→        private inline fun formatSuccessfulApiResult(block: JSONObject.() -> Unit) =
431→            JSONObject()
432→                .apply {
433→                    put(SUCCESS_KEY, true)
434→                    block(this)
435→                }.toString()
436→
437→        fun formatApiResult(res: Boolean) = formatSuccessfulApiResult { put(VALUE_KEY, res) }
438→
439→        fun formatApiResult(res: Int) = formatSuccessfulApiResult { put(VALUE_KEY, res) }
440→
441→        fun formatApiResult(res: Long) = formatSuccessfulApiResult { put(VALUE_KEY, res) }
442→
443→        fun formatApiResult(res: String) = formatSuccessfulApiResult { put(VALUE_KEY, res) }
444→
445→        suspend fun getDataFromRequest(
446→            methodName: String,
447→            jsAPI: AnkiDroidJsAPI,
448→            apiData: String = "",
449→        ): String =
450→            jsAPI
451→                .handleJsApiRequest(methodName, jsApiContract(apiData), false)
452→                .decodeToString()
453→    }
454→}

    fun ankiResetProgressTest() =
        runTest {
            val n = addBasicNote("Front", "Back")
            val c = n.firstCard()

            // Make card review with 28L due and 280% ease
            c.type = CardType.Rev
            c.due = 28
            c.factor = 2800
            c.ivl = 8

            // before reset
            assertEquals(28, c.due, "Card due before reset")
            assertEquals(8, c.ivl, "Card interval before reset")
            assertEquals(2800, c.factor, "Card ease before reset")
            assertEquals(CardType.Rev, c.type, "Card type before reset")

            val reviewer: Reviewer = startReviewer()
            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi

            // test that card reset
            assertThat(getDataFromRequest("resetProgress", jsapi), equalTo(formatApiResult(true)))
            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            // verify that card progress reset
            // --------------------------------
            val cardAfterReset = col.getCard(reviewer.currentCard!!.id)
            assertEquals(2, cardAfterReset.due, "Card due after reset")
            assertEquals(0, cardAfterReset.ivl, "Card interval after reset")
            assertEquals(CardType.New, cardAfterReset.type, "Card type after reset")
        }

    @Test
    fun ankiGetNoteTagsTest() =
        runTest {
            val note = addBasicNote("foo", "bar")
            note.addTag("testTag1")
            note.addTag("testTag2")
            col.updateNote(note)

            val reviewer: Reviewer = startReviewer()
            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi

            // Get tags from note using JS API
            val tagsJson = """{"noteId":${note.id}}"""
            val result = getDataFromRequest("getNoteTags", jsapi, tagsJson)
            val jsonObject = JSONObject(result)

            assertThat(jsonObject.getBoolean(SUCCESS_KEY), equalTo(true))
            // The value should be a JSON array string
            val tagsArray = org.json.JSONArray(jsonObject.getString(VALUE_KEY))
            assertThat(tagsArray.length(), equalTo(2))
            val tagsList = (0 until tagsArray.length()).map { tagsArray.getString(it) }
            assertThat(tagsList.contains("testTag1"), equalTo(true))
            assertThat(tagsList.contains("testTag2"), equalTo(true))
        }

    @Test
    fun ankiSetNoteTagsTest() =
        runTest {
            val note = addBasicNote("foo", "bar")
            note.addTag("oldTag")
            col.updateNote(note)

            val reviewer: Reviewer = startReviewer()
            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi

            // Set new tags using JS API (replacing old tags)
            val setTagsJson = """{"noteId":${note.id},"tags":["newTag1","newTag2"]}"""
            val result = getDataFromRequest("setNoteTags", jsapi, setTagsJson)
            assertThat(result, equalTo(formatApiResult(true)))
            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            // Verify that tags were replaced
            val updatedNote = col.getNote(note.id)
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.size, equalTo(2))
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.contains("newTag1"), equalTo(true))
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.contains("newTag2"), equalTo(true))
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.contains("oldTag"), equalTo(false))
        }

    @Test
    fun ankiSetNoteTagsRemoveAllTest() =
        runTest {
            val note = addBasicNote("foo", "bar")
            note.addTag("tag1")
            note.addTag("tag2")
            col.updateNote(note)

            val reviewer: Reviewer = startReviewer()
            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            val jsapi = reviewer.jsApi

            // Remove all tags by setting empty array
            val setTagsJson = """{"noteId":${note.id},"tags":[]}"""
            val result = getDataFromRequest("setNoteTags", jsapi, setTagsJson)
            assertThat(result, equalTo(formatApiResult(true)))
            waitForAsyncTasksToComplete()

            // Verify that all tags were removed
            val updatedNote = col.getNote(note.id)
            assertThat(updatedNote.tags.isEmpty(), equalTo(true))
        }"}

conversationHistory.content:
You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.1714 (commit: 7c30e125).

## Issue to Resolve

Update to Anki 23.12 backend
The Anki update includes some significant changes to the FSRS algorithm. So, for best results, all reviews should be done on apps with the same codebase.

(There are no "breaking" changes, but still ...)

Now that the 23.12 release is public, the backend code of AnkiDroid should be updated as soon as possible.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I/we get notifications for anki releases, we are aware of them for what it's worth
I was almost certain that you guys get notifications for the releases, but I still created this issue because the 23.12 update changes the scheduler code, which means that doing reviews on apps with different backend versions can lead to some unwanted behaviour. So, quickly updating to this version is somewhat more important.

After this release, for several months, there are no expected changes to the FSRS scheduler code. So, I would not urge for a very rapid update to the new backend version for those releases.
Also, 23.12 introduced some important fixes. From release notes:

- When cards are rescheduled multiple times without reviews, extra entries will not be added to the review log.
- Fix some bugs in sorting by FSRS retrievability.
- Recompute FSRS memory state when cards moved into different deck.
- Added a new v3 option to limit child decks by their parent, when clicking on the child.
- Fix filtered deck ordering of intraday learning cards and reviews
- Fix relative overdueness order error in filtered deck.
The new backend can't be simply dropped in. Some changes will need to be made to AnkiDroid:

- The filtered deck screen will need to support customizing the separate preview delays for again/hard & good buttons
- The auto advance feature will need to switch to using the settings in the current card's deck options
> The new backend can't be simply dropped in.

Yes, I agree. All that I have to say is this update should take a slightly higher priority than normal.
Another issue I didn't mention above: Anki displays various options when importing an .apkg file, which AnkiDroid is currently not doing. As a temporary workaround to get things to compile when switching to the 23.12 backend, you can hard-code the argument change:

```diff
diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
index 07ec516536..bb272bd17b 100644
--- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
+++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 package com.ichi2.libanki

 import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
+import anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions
 import anki.search.SearchNode
 import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend

@@ -107,7 +108,13 @@ fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
     limit: ExportLimit,
     legacy: Boolean = true
 ) {
-    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
+    val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
+        this.withScheduling = withScheduling
+        this.withMedia = withMedia
+        this.legacy = legacy
+        this.withDeckConfigs = true
+    }
+    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
 }

 fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
diff --git a/build.gradle b/build.gradle
index aeafc762f8..2057d8a32f 100644
--- a/build.gradle
+++ b/build.gradle
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ buildscript {
     ext.kotlin_version = '1.9.21'
     ext.lint_version = '31.1.1'
     ext.acra_version = '5.11.3'
-    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.32-anki23.10.1'
+    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.34-anki23.12'
     ext.hamcrest_version = '2.2'
     ext.junit_version = '5.10.1'
     ext.coroutines_version = '1.7.3'
```

A proper fix will mean using the import-anki-package.html page. Currently, for some unknown reason, it is not being included in the backend builds, and it could use the help of someone knowledgeable in Gradle to debug.

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android-Backend/pull/339

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/card_template.html`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/BackendImporting.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiPackageImporterFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiServer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/PostRequestHandler.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerViewModel.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt`
- `build.gradle`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

 17→package com.ichi2.libanki

 19→import anki.import_export.ExportLimit

 22→
 23→/**
 24→ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 25→ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 26→ * and this routine will return false.
 27→ *
 28→ * There must not be an active transaction.
 29→ *
 30→ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 31→ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 32→ * to check for success.
 33→ *
 34→ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 35→ *
 36→ */
 37→fun Collection.createBackup(
 38→    backupFolder: String,
 39→    force: Boolean,
 40→    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 41→): Boolean {
 42→    return backend.createBackup(
 43→        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 44→        force = force,
 45→        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
 46→    )
 47→}
 48→
 49→/**
 50→ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 51→ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 52→ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 53→ */
 54→fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
 55→    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()
 56→}
 57→
 58→/**
 59→ * Replace the collection file with the one in the provided .colpkg file.
 60→ * The collection must be already closed, and must be opened afterwards.
 61→ * */
 62→fun importCollectionPackage(
 63→    backend: Backend,
 64→    colPath: String,
 65→    colpkgPath: String
 66→) {
 67→    backend.importCollectionPackage(
 68→        colPath = colPath,
 69→        backupPath = colpkgPath,
 70→        mediaFolder = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media"),
 71→        mediaDb = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media.db")
 72→    )
 73→}
 74→
 75→/**
 76→ * Export the collection into a .colpkg file.
 77→ * If legacy=false, a file targeting Anki 2.1.50+ is created. It compresses better and is faster to
 78→ * create, but older clients can not read it.
 79→ */
 80→fun Collection.exportCollectionPackage(
 81→    outPath: String,
 82→    includeMedia: Boolean,
 83→    legacy: Boolean = true
 84→) {
 85→    close(forFullSync = true)
 86→    backend.exportCollectionPackage(
 87→        outPath = outPath,
 88→        includeMedia = includeMedia,
 89→        legacy = legacy
 90→    )
 91→    reopen()
 92→}
 93→
 94→fun Collection.importAnkiPackageRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
 95→    return backend.importAnkiPackageRaw(input)
 96→}
 97→
 98→/**
 99→ * Export the specified deck to an .apkg file.
100→ * * If legacy is false, an apkg will be created that can only
101→ * be opened with recent Anki versions.
102→ */
103→fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
104→    outPath: String,
105→    withScheduling: Boolean,
106→    withMedia: Boolean,
107→    limit: ExportLimit,
108→    legacy: Boolean = true
109→) {
110→    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
111→}
112→
113→fun Collection.exportNotesCsv(
114→    outPath: String,
115→    withHtml: Boolean,
116→    withTags: Boolean,
117→    withDeck: Boolean,
118→    withNotetype: Boolean,
119→    withGuid: Boolean,
120→    limit: ExportLimit
121→) {
122→    backend.exportNoteCsv(outPath, withHtml, withTags, withDeck, withNotetype, withGuid, limit)
123→}
124→
125→fun Collection.exportCardsCsv(outPath: String, withHtml: Boolean, limit: ExportLimit) {
126→    backend.exportCardCsv(outPath, withHtml, limit)
127→}
128→
129→fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
130→    return backend.getCsvMetadataRaw(input)
131→}
132→
133→fun Collection.importCsvRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
134→    return backend.importCsvRaw(input)
135→}
136→
137→fun Collection.buildSearchString(input: ByteArray): String {
138→    return backend.buildSearchString(SearchNode.parseFrom(input))
139→}

54→ * NOTE: this should be used only with [android.webkit.WebView.evaluateJavascript].
55→ */
56→val hideShowButtonCss = """
57→    javascript:(
58→        function() {
59→            var hideShowButtonStyle = '.desktop-only { display: none !important; }';
60→            var newStyle = document.createElement('style');                    
61→            newStyle.appendChild(document.createTextNode(hideShowButtonStyle));
62→            document.head.appendChild(newStyle);       
63→        }
64→    )()
65→""".trimIndent()

67→/**
68→ * Calls the native [CardBrowser] to display the results of the search query constructed from the
69→ * input. This method will always return the received input.
70→ */
71→suspend fun FragmentActivity.searchInBrowser(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
72→    val searchString = withCol { buildSearchString(input) }
73→    val starterIntent = Intent(this, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
74→        putExtra("search_query", searchString)
75→        putExtra("all_decks", true)
76→    }
77→    startActivity(starterIntent)
78→    return input
79→}

14→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
15→
16→import android.content.Context
17→import android.content.Intent
18→import android.os.Bundle
19→import android.webkit.WebView
20→import androidx.activity.OnBackPressedCallback
21→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
22→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager
23→import com.ichi2.anki.R
24→import com.ichi2.anki.SingleFragmentActivity
25→import com.ichi2.anki.hideShowButtonCss
26→
27→class AnkiPackageImporterFragment : PageFragment() {
28→    override val title: String
29→        get() = resources.getString(R.string.menu_import)
30→    override val pageName: String
31→        get() = "import-page"
32→    override lateinit var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
33→    override var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient = PageChromeClient()
34→    private lateinit var backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
35→
36→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
37→        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
38→        backCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
39→            override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
40→                CollectionManager.getBackend().setWantsAbort()
41→                // once triggered the callback is not needed as the import process can't be resumed
42→                remove()
43→            }
44→        }
45→        val path = arguments?.getString(ARG_FILE_PATH)
46→            ?: throw IllegalStateException("No path provided for apkg package to import")
47→        webViewClient = AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(path, backCallback)
48→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
49→        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backCallback)
50→    }
51→
52→    class AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(
53→        private val path: String,
54→        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
55→    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
56→        /**
57→         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
58→         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
59→         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
60→         */
61→        private var isDone = false
62→
63→        override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
64→            val params = """{ type: "json_file", path: "$path"}"""
65→            // https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/main/ts/import-page/index.ts
66→            view!!.evaluateJavascript("anki.setupImportPage($params);$hideShowButtonCss;") {
67→                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
68→            }
69→        }
70→
71→        override fun onLoadResource(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
72→            super.onLoadResource(view, url)
73→            backCallback.isEnabled = when {
74→                url == null -> false
75→                url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
76→                url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
77→                    isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
78→                    false
79→                }
80→                else -> false
81→            }
82→        }
83→    }
84→
85→    companion object {
86→        private const val ARG_FILE_PATH = "arg_file_path"
87→
88→        fun getIntent(context: Context, filePath: String): Intent {
89→            val args = bundleOf(ARG_FILE_PATH to filePath)
90→            return SingleFragmentActivity.getIntent(context, AnkiPackageImporterFragment::class, args)
91→        }
92→    }
93→}
94→

 24→/**

 26→ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 27→ * and this routine will return false.
 28→ *
 29→ * There must not be an active transaction.
 30→ *
 31→ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 32→ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 33→ * to check for success.
 34→ *
 35→ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 36→ *
 37→ */

 41→    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 42→): Boolean {

 47→    )
 48→}

 51→ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 52→ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 53→ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.

 18→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 19→
 20→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD
 21→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 22→import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25→
 26→const val PORT = 0
 27→// const val PORT = 40001
 28→
 29→// local debugging:
 30→// ~/Local/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb forward tcp:40001 tcp:40001
 31→
 32→open class AnkiServer(
 33→    private val postHandler: PostRequestHandler
 34→) : NanoHTTPD(LOCALHOST, PORT) {
 35→
 36→    fun baseUrl(): String {
 37→        return "http://$LOCALHOST:$listeningPort/"
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    // it's faster to serve local files without GZip. see 'page render' in logs
 41→    // This also removes 'W/System: A resource failed to call end.'
 42→    override fun useGzipWhenAccepted(r: Response?) = false
 43→
 44→    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45→        val uri = session.uri
 46→        val mime = getMimeFromUri(uri)
 47→
 48→        if (session.method == Method.GET) {
 49→            val resourcePath = "web$uri"
 50→            val stream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream(resourcePath)
 51→            Timber.d("GET: Requested %s (%s), found? %b", uri, resourcePath, stream != null)
 52→            return newChunkedResponse(Response.Status.OK, mime, stream)
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        if (session.method == Method.POST) {
 56→            Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 57→            val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 58→            return buildResponse {
 59→                postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→        return newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun buildResponse(
 66→        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 67→    ): Response {
 68→        return try {
 69→            val data = runBlocking {
 70→                block()
 71→            }
 72→            newChunkedResponse(data)
 73→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 74→            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 75→        }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    companion object {
 79→        const val LOCALHOST = "127.0.0.1"
 80→
 81→        /** Common prefix used on Anki requests */
 82→        const val ANKI_PREFIX = "/_anki/"
 83→        const val ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX = "/jsapi/"
 84→
 85→        fun getMimeFromUri(uri: String): String {
 86→            return when (uri.substringAfterLast(".")) {
 87→                "ico" -> "image/x-icon"
 88→                "css" -> "text/css"
 89→                "js" -> "text/javascript"
 90→                "html" -> "text/html"
 91→                else -> "application/binary"
 92→            }
 93→        }
 94→
 95→        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 96→            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 97→            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 98→            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 99→            return bytes
100→        }
101→
102→        fun newChunkedResponse(
103→            data: ByteArray?,
104→            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
105→            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
106→        ): Response {
107→            return if (data == null) {
108→                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
109→            } else {
110→                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
111→            }
112→        }
113→    }
114→}
115→

25→
26→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
27→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
28→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
29→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
30→        .setDomain(domain)
31→        .addPathHandler("/web/") { path: String ->
32→            val inputStream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream("web/$path")
33→            WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
34→        }
35→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
36→            try {
37→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
38→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40→            } catch (e: Exception) {
41→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42→                null
43→            }
44→        }
45→        .build()
46→}

The changes have been successfully implemented. Let me provide a summary of what was done:

## Summary of Changes

The issue requested updating AnkiDroid to use the Anki 23.12 backend. Based on the issue description and the provided diff, the main change required was updating the `exportAnkiPackage` function in `BackendImportExport.kt` to use the new API.

### Changes Made to `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt`:

1. **Added new import**: Added `import anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions` to support the new API

2. **Updated `exportAnkiPackage` function**: Changed from the old API:
   ```kotlin
   backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
   ```
   
   To the new API using options builder pattern:
   ```kotlin
   val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
       this.withScheduling = withScheduling
       this.withMedia = withMedia
       this.legacy = legacy
       this.withDeckConfigs = true
   }
   backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
   ```

### Verified:
- The `build.gradle` already has the correct backend version `0.1.34-anki23.12.1`
- No lint errors were introduced by the changes
- No existing tests directly use the modified function

### Notes from the Issue:
The issue mentions that additional features need to be implemented (filtered deck screen, auto advance feature, import options), but those are larger feature additions. This change implements the immediate workaround provided in the issue discussion to get the codebase compiling with the 23.12 backend, ensuring the FSRS algorithm updates are available to users.

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.178 (commit: f03d541c).

## Issue to Resolve

[BUG]: display order bug for notes
### Checked for duplicates?

- [X] This issue is not a duplicate

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

Go to "card browser" then "options" and under "toggle cards/notes" select "notes". Go to "change display order" and choose another option.

### Expected behaviour

The display order should stay as set, like when "cards" is selected in "toggle cards/notes."

### Actual behaviour

When you select "notes", it automatically sets the display order to "by created time" oldest to newest. If you choose another option it doesn't change except by pressing "by created time" again to flip the direction.  This only works until you exit the app or add a new note, and it then flips to oldest to newest again.

### Debug info

```text

ACRA UUID = cc3ec77b-8b96-40ad-99e3-3187c7799494

New schema = false

Scheduler = std2

Crash Reports Enabled = true

DatabaseV2 Enabled = true
```


### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

_No response_

### Research

- [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app)
- [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue
- [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Hi,  I'd like to work on this issue.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardBrowser.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/model/SortType.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Collection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

17→package com.ichi2.anki.model
18→
19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
20→import androidx.core.content.edit
21→import com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser
22→import com.ichi2.anki.R
23→import com.ichi2.libanki.Config
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.SortOrder
25→
26→/**
27→ * How to sort the rows in the [CardBrowser]
28→ *
29→ * This is an adapter from our [SharedPreferences] based handling of sorting
30→ * to Anki's [Config]
31→ *
32→ * We can likely remove the SharedPreferences and rely entirely on Anki
33→ *
34→ * @param ankiSortType The value to be passed into Anki's "sortType" config
35→ * @param cardBrowserLabelIndex The index into [R.array.card_browser_order_labels]
36→ */
37→@Suppress("unused") // 'unused' entries are iterated over by .entries
38→enum class SortType(val ankiSortType: String?, val cardBrowserLabelIndex: Int) {
39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
49→
50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences) {
51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
53→        config.set("sortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
54→        preferences.edit {
55→            putBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
56→        }
57→    }
58→
59→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
60→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
61→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
62→
63→    companion object {
64→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences): SortType {
65→            val colOrder = config.get<String>("sortType")
66→            val type = entries.firstOrNull { it.ankiSortType == colOrder } ?: NO_SORTING
67→            if (type == SORT_FIELD && preferences.getBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", false)) {
68→                return NO_SORTING
69→            }
70→            return type
71→        }
72→
73→        fun fromCardBrowserLabelIndex(index: Int): SortType {
74→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.cardBrowserLabelIndex == index } ?: NO_SORTING
75→        }
76→    }
77→}

 49→
 50→// Anki maintains a cache of used tags so it can quickly present a list of tags
 51→// for autocomplete and in the browser. For efficiency, deletions are not
 52→// tracked, so unused tags can only be removed from the list with a DB check.
 53→//
 54→// This module manages the tag cache and tags for notes.
 55→@KotlinCleanup("TextUtils -> Kotlin isNotEmpty()")
 56→@KotlinCleanup("inline function in init { } so we don't need to init `crt` etc... at the definition")
 57→@KotlinCleanup("ids.size != 0")
 58→@WorkerThread
 59→open class Collection(
 60→    /**
 61→     *  @param Path The path to the collection.anki2 database. Must be unicode and openable with [File].
 62→     */
 63→    val path: String,
 64→    private var debugLog: Boolean, // Not in libAnki.
 65→    /**
 66→     * Outside of libanki, you should not access the backend directly for collection operations.
 67→     * Operations that work on a closed collection (eg importing), or do not require a collection
 68→     * at all (eg translations) are the exception.
 69→     */
 70→    val backend: Backend
 71→) {
 72→    /** Access backend translations */
 73→    val tr = backend.tr
 74→
 75→    @get:JvmName("isDbClosed")
 76→    val dbClosed: Boolean
 77→        get() {
 78→            return dbInternal == null
 79→        }
 80→
 81→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
 82→    fun debugEnsureNoOpenPointers() {
 83→        val result = backend.getActiveSequenceNumbers()
 84→        if (result.isNotEmpty()) {
 85→            val numbers = result.toString()
 86→            throw IllegalStateException("Contained unclosed sequence numbers: $numbers")
 87→        }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    // a lot of legacy code does not check for nullability
 91→    val db: DB
 92→        get() = dbInternal!!
 93→
 94→    var dbInternal: DB? = null
 95→
 96→    /**
 97→     * Getters/Setters ********************************************************** *************************************
 98→     */
 99→
100→    val media: Media
101→
102→    lateinit var decks: Decks
103→        protected set
104→
105→    val tags: Tags
106→
107→    @KotlinCleanup(
108→        "move accessor methods here, maybe reconsider return type." +
109→            "See variable: conf"
110→    )
111→    lateinit var config: Config
112→
113→    @KotlinCleanup("see if we can inline a function inside init {} and make this `val`")
114→    lateinit var sched: Scheduler
115→        protected set
116→
117→    private var mStartTime: Long
118→    private var mStartReps: Int
119→
120→    val mod: Long
121→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select mod from col")
122→
123→    val crt: Long
124→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select crt from col")
125→
126→    val scm: Long
127→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select scm from col")
128→
129→    private val lastSync: Long
130→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select ls from col")
131→
132→    fun usn(): Int {
133→        return -1
134→    }
135→
136→    var ls: Long = 0
137→    // END: SQL table columns
138→
139→    private var mLogHnd: PrintWriter? = null
140→
141→    init {
142→        media = Media(this)
143→        tags = Tags(this)
144→        val created = reopen()
145→        log(path, VersionUtils.pkgVersionName)
146→        mStartReps = 0
147→        mStartTime = 0
148→        _loadScheduler()
149→        if (created) {
150→            sched.useNewTimezoneCode()
151→            config.set("schedVer", 2)
152→            // we need to reload the scheduler: this was previously loaded as V1
153→            _loadScheduler()
154→        }
155→    }
156→
157→    fun name(): String {
158→        // TODO:
159→        return File(path).name.replace(".anki2", "")
160→    }
161→
162→    /**
163→     * Scheduler
164→     * ***********************************************************
165→     */
166→    fun schedVer(): Int {
167→        // schedVer was not set on legacy v1 collections
168→        val ver = config.get("schedVer") ?: 1
169→        return if (listOf(1, 2).contains(ver)) {
170→            ver
171→        } else {
172→            throw RuntimeException("Unsupported scheduler version")
173→        }
174→    }
175→
176→    fun _loadScheduler() {
177→        val ver = schedVer()
178→        if (ver == 1) {
179→            sched = DummyScheduler(this)
180→        } else {
181→            if (!backend.getConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021)) {
182→                backend.setConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021, true, undoable = false)
183→            }
184→            sched = Scheduler(this)
185→            config.set("localOffset", sched.currentTimezoneOffset())
186→        }
187→    }
188→
189→    /**
190→     * Disconnect from DB.
191→     * Python implementation has a save argument for legacy reasons;
192→     * AnkiDroid always saves as changes are made.
193→     */
194→    @Synchronized
195→    fun close(downgrade: Boolean = false, forFullSync: Boolean = false) {
196→        if (!dbClosed) {
197→            if (!forFullSync) {
198→                backend.closeCollection(downgrade)
199→            }
200→            dbInternal = null
201→            _closeLog()
202→            Timber.i("Collection closed")
203→        }
204→    }
205→
206→    /** True if DB was created */
207→    fun reopen(afterFullSync: Boolean = false): Boolean {
208→        Timber.i("(Re)opening Database: %s", path)
209→        return if (dbClosed) {
210→            val (db_, created) = Storage.openDB(path, backend, afterFullSync)
211→            dbInternal = db_
212→            load()
213→            _openLog()
214→            if (afterFullSync) {
215→                _loadScheduler()
216→            }
217→            created
218→        } else {
219→            false
220→        }
221→    }
222→
223→    fun load() {
224→        notetypes = Notetypes(this)
225→        decks = Decks(this)
226→        config = Config(backend)
227→    }
228→
229→    /** Note: not in libanki.  Mark schema modified to force a full
230→     * sync, but with the confirmation checking function disabled This
231→     * is equivalent to `modSchema(False)` in Anki. A distinct method
232→     * is used so that the type does not states that an exception is
233→     * thrown when in fact it is never thrown.
234→     */
235→    open fun modSchemaNoCheck() {
236→        db.execute(
237→            "update col set scm=?, mod=?",
238→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS(),
239→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS()
240→        )
241→    }
242→
243→    /** Mark schema modified to force a full sync.
244→     * ConfirmModSchemaException will be thrown if the user needs to be prompted to confirm the action.
245→     * If the user chooses to confirm then modSchemaNoCheck should be called, after which the exception can
246→     * be safely ignored, and the outer code called again.
247→     *
248→     * @throws ConfirmModSchemaException
249→     */
250→    @Throws(ConfirmModSchemaException::class)
251→    fun modSchema() {
252→        if (!schemaChanged()) {
253→            /* In Android we can't show a dialog which blocks the main UI thread
254→             Therefore we can't wait for the user to confirm if they want to do
255→             a full sync here, and we instead throw an exception asking the outer
256→             code to handle the user's choice */
257→            throw ConfirmModSchemaException()
258→        }
259→        modSchemaNoCheck()
260→    }
261→
262→    /** True if schema changed since last sync.  */
263→    fun schemaChanged(): Boolean {
264→        return scm > lastSync
265→    }
266→
267→    /**
268→     * Object creation helpers **************************************************
269→     * *********************************************
270→     */
271→    fun getCard(id: Long): Card {
272→        return Card(this, id)
273→    }
274→
275→    fun updateCards(cards: Iterable<Card>, skipUndoEntry: Boolean = false): OpChanges {
276→        return backend.updateCards(cards.map { it.toBackendCard() }, skipUndoEntry)
277→    }
278→
279→    fun updateCard(card: Card, skipUndoEntry: Boolean = false): OpChanges {
280→        return updateCards(listOf(card), skipUndoEntry)
281→    }
282→
283→    fun getNote(id: Long): Note {
284→        return Note(this, id)
285→    }
286→
287→    /**
288→     * Notes ******************************************************************** ***************************
289→     */
290→    fun noteCount(): Int {
291→        return db.queryScalar("SELECT count() FROM notes")
292→    }
293→
294→    /**
295→     * Return a new note with the model derived from the deck or the configuration
296→     * @param forDeck When true it uses the model specified in the deck (mid), otherwise it uses the model specified in
297→     * the configuration (curModel)

448→    fun startTimebox() {
449→        mStartTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
450→        mStartReps = sched.reps
451→    }
452→
453→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
454→
455→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
456→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
457→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
458→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
459→            // timeboxing disabled
460→            return null
461→        }
462→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
463→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
464→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
465→            TimeboxReached(
466→                limit,
467→                sched.reps - mStartReps
468→            ).also {
469→                startTimebox()
470→            }
471→        } else {
472→            null
473→        }
474→    }

532→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
533→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
534→        // cards without notes
535→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
536→            return false
537→        }
538→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
539→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
540→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
541→        ) > 0
542→        // notes without cards or models
543→        if (badNotes) {
544→            return false
545→        }
546→        // invalid ords
547→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
548→            // ignore clozes
549→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
550→                continue
551→            }
552→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
553→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
554→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
555→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
556→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
557→                tmpls.length(),
558→                m.getLong("id")
559→            ) > 0
560→            if (badOrd) {
561→                return false
562→            }
563→        }
564→        return true
565→    }

567→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
568→        if (!debugLog) return
569→
570→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
571→
572→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
573→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
574→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
575→
576→        val objectsString = objects
577→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
578→            .joinToString(", ")
579→
580→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
581→    }
582→
583→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
584→        mLogHnd?.let {
585→            try {
586→                it.println(s)
587→            } catch (e: Exception) {
588→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
589→            }
590→        }
591→        Timber.d(s)
592→    }
593→
594→    private fun _openLog() {
595→        if (!debugLog) {
596→            return
597→        }
598→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
599→        try {
600→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
601→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
602→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
603→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
604→                    lpath2.delete()
605→                }
606→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
607→            }
608→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
609→        } catch (e: IOException) {
610→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
611→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
612→            debugLog = false
613→        }
614→    }
615→
616→    private fun _closeLog() {
617→        if (!debugLog) return
618→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
619→        mLogHnd?.close()
620→        mLogHnd = null
621→    }

[thinking: Now I can clearly see the bug. When `switchCardOrNote` is called, it doesn't update `mOrder` and `mOrderAsc` to reflect the settings for the new mode (cards vs notes). It should:
1. Save the current s...]
Now I understand the root cause of the bug. The issue is that:

1. `SortType.save()` always saves to `sortType` (for cards), but when in notes mode, it should save to `noteSortType`
2. `SortType.fromCol()` always reads from `sortType` (for cards), but when in notes mode, it should read from `noteSortType`
3. `CardBrowser.changeCardOrder()` saves `sortBackwards`, but when in notes mode, it should save `browserNoteSortBackwards`
4. `CardBrowser.onCollectionLoaded()` reads `sortBackwards`, but doesn't load the correct settings when in notes mode
5. `CardBrowser.switchCardOrNote()` doesn't reload `mOrder` and `mOrderAsc` when switching modes

Let me implement the fix. First, let me update `SortType.kt` to support both cards and notes modes:

17→package com.ichi2.anki.model
18→
19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
20→import androidx.core.content.edit
21→import com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser
22→import com.ichi2.anki.R
23→import com.ichi2.libanki.Config
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.SortOrder
25→
26→/**
27→ * How to sort the rows in the [CardBrowser]
28→ *
29→ * This is an adapter from our [SharedPreferences] based handling of sorting
30→ * to Anki's [Config]
31→ *
32→ * We can likely remove the SharedPreferences and rely entirely on Anki
33→ *
34→ * @param ankiSortType The value to be passed into Anki's "sortType" config
35→ * @param cardBrowserLabelIndex The index into [R.array.card_browser_order_labels]
36→ */
37→@Suppress("unused") // 'unused' entries are iterated over by .entries
38→enum class SortType(val ankiSortType: String?, val cardBrowserLabelIndex: Int) {
39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
49→
50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences) {
51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
53→        config.set("sortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
54→        preferences.edit {
55→            putBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
56→        }
57→    }
58→
59→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
60→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
61→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
62→
63→    companion object {
64→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences): SortType {
65→            val colOrder = config.get<String>("sortType")
66→            val type = entries.firstOrNull { it.ankiSortType == colOrder } ?: NO_SORTING
67→            if (type == SORT_FIELD && preferences.getBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", false)) {
68→                return NO_SORTING
69→            }
70→            return type
71→        }
72→
73→        fun fromCardBrowserLabelIndex(index: Int): SortType {
74→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.cardBrowserLabelIndex == index } ?: NO_SORTING
75→        }
76→    }
77→}
