 17→package com.ichi2.anki.model
 18→
 19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 20→import androidx.core.content.edit
 21→import com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 23→import com.ichi2.libanki.Config
 24→import com.ichi2.libanki.SortOrder
 25→
 26→/**
 27→ * How to sort the rows in the [CardBrowser]
 28→ *
 29→ * This is an adapter from our [SharedPreferences] based handling of sorting
 30→ * to Anki's [Config]
 31→ *
 32→ * We can likely remove the SharedPreferences and rely entirely on Anki
 33→ *
 34→ * @param ankiSortType The value to be passed into Anki's "sortType" config
 35→ * @param cardBrowserLabelIndex The index into [R.array.card_browser_order_labels]
 36→ */
 37→@Suppress("unused") // 'unused' entries are iterated over by .entries
 38→enum class SortType(val ankiSortType: String?, val cardBrowserLabelIndex: Int) {
 39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
 40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
 41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
 42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
 43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
 44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
 45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
 46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
 47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
 48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
 49→
 50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences, cardsOrNotes: CardsOrNotes) {
 51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
 52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
 53→        val sortTypeKey = getSortTypeConfigKey(cardsOrNotes)
 54→        val noSortingPrefKey = getNoSortingPrefKey(cardsOrNotes)
 55→        config.set(sortTypeKey, this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
 56→        preferences.edit {
 57→            putBoolean(noSortingPrefKey, this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
 58→        }
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
 62→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
 63→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
 64→
 65→    companion object {
 66→        /** Config key for sort type in cards mode */
 67→        const val SORT_TYPE_CARDS_CONFIG_KEY = "sortType"
 68→        /** Config key for sort type in notes mode */
 69→        const val SORT_TYPE_NOTES_CONFIG_KEY = "noteSortType"
 70→        /** Config key for sort order (ascending/descending) in cards mode */
 71→        const val SORT_BACKWARDS_CARDS_CONFIG_KEY = "sortBackwards"
 72→        /** Config key for sort order (ascending/descending) in notes mode */
 73→        const val SORT_BACKWARDS_NOTES_CONFIG_KEY = "browserNoteSortBackwards"
 74→        /** Preference key for "no sorting" option in cards mode */
 75→        private const val NO_SORTING_CARDS_PREF_KEY = "cardBrowserNoSorting"
 76→        /** Preference key for "no sorting" option in notes mode */
 77→        private const val NO_SORTING_NOTES_PREF_KEY = "cardBrowserNoSortingNotes"
 78→
 79→        fun getSortTypeConfigKey(cardsOrNotes: CardsOrNotes): String =
 80→            when (cardsOrNotes) {
 81→                CardsOrNotes.CARDS -> SORT_TYPE_CARDS_CONFIG_KEY
 82→                CardsOrNotes.NOTES -> SORT_TYPE_NOTES_CONFIG_KEY
 83→            }
 84→
 85→        fun getSortBackwardsConfigKey(cardsOrNotes: CardsOrNotes): String =
 86→            when (cardsOrNotes) {
 87→                CardsOrNotes.CARDS -> SORT_BACKWARDS_CARDS_CONFIG_KEY
 88→                CardsOrNotes.NOTES -> SORT_BACKWARDS_NOTES_CONFIG_KEY
 89→            }
 90→
 91→        private fun getNoSortingPrefKey(cardsOrNotes: CardsOrNotes): String =
 92→            when (cardsOrNotes) {
 93→                CardsOrNotes.CARDS -> NO_SORTING_CARDS_PREF_KEY
 94→                CardsOrNotes.NOTES -> NO_SORTING_NOTES_PREF_KEY
 95→            }
 96→
 97→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences, cardsOrNotes: CardsOrNotes): SortType {
 98→            val sortTypeKey = getSortTypeConfigKey(cardsOrNotes)
 99→            val noSortingPrefKey = getNoSortingPrefKey(cardsOrNotes)
100→            val colOrder = config.get<String>(sortTypeKey)
101→            val type = entries.firstOrNull { it.ankiSortType == colOrder } ?: NO_SORTING
102→            if (type == SORT_FIELD && preferences.getBoolean(noSortingPrefKey, false)) {
103→                return NO_SORTING
104→            }
105→            return type
106→        }
107→
108→        fun fromCardBrowserLabelIndex(index: Int): SortType {
109→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.cardBrowserLabelIndex == index } ?: NO_SORTING
110→        }
111→    }
112→}
113→

 20→package com.ichi2.libanki
 21→
 22→import android.content.ContentValues
 23→import android.content.Context
 24→import android.database.Cursor
 25→import android.database.SQLException
 26→import android.database.sqlite.SQLiteDatabase
 27→import androidx.annotation.WorkerThread
 28→import androidx.sqlite.db.SupportSQLiteDatabase
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionHelper
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DatabaseErrorDialog
 32→import com.ichi2.utils.DatabaseChangeDecorator
 33→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 34→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend
 35→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.database.AnkiSupportSQLiteDatabase
 36→import org.intellij.lang.annotations.Language
 37→import timber.log.Timber
 38→
 39→/**
 40→ * Database layer for AnkiDroid. Wraps an SupportSQLiteDatabase (provided by either the Rust backend
 41→ * or the Android framework), and provides some helpers on top.
 42→ */
 43→@KotlinCleanup("Improve documentation")
 44→@WorkerThread
 45→class DB(db: SupportSQLiteDatabase) {
 46→    /**
 47→     * The collection, which is actually an SQLite database.
 48→     */
 49→    val database: SupportSQLiteDatabase = DatabaseChangeDecorator(db)
 50→    var mod = false
 51→
 52→    /**
 53→     * The default AnkiDroid SQLite database callback.
 54→     *
 55→     * IMPORTANT: this disables the default Android behaviour of removing the file if corruption
 56→     * is encountered.
 57→     *
 58→     * We do not handle versioning or connection config using the framework APIs, so those methods
 59→     * do nothing in our implementation. However, we on corruption events we want to send messages but
 60→     * not delete the database.
 61→     *
 62→     * Note: this does not apply when using the Rust backend (ie for Collection)
 63→     */
 64→    class SupportSQLiteOpenHelperCallback(version: Int) : AnkiSupportSQLiteDatabase.DefaultDbCallback(version) {
 65→        /** Send error message when corruption is encountered. We don't call super() as we don't accidentally
 66→         * want to opt-in to the standard Android behaviour of removing the corrupted file, but as we're
 67→         * inheriting from DefaultDbCallback which does not call super either, it would be technically safe
 68→         * if we did so.  */
 69→        override fun onCorruption(db: SupportSQLiteDatabase) {
 70→            Timber.e("The database has been corrupted: %s", db.path)
 71→            sendExceptionReport(
 72→                RuntimeException("Database corrupted"),
 73→                "DB.MyDbErrorHandler.onCorruption",
 74→                "Db has been corrupted: " + db.path
 75→            )
 76→            CollectionHelper.instance.closeCollection("Database corrupted")
 77→            DatabaseErrorDialog.databaseCorruptFlag = true
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    /**
 82→     * Closes a previously opened database connection.
 83→     */
 84→    fun close() {
 85→        try {
 86→            database.close()
 87→            Timber.d("Database %s closed = %s", database.path, !database.isOpen)
 88→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 89→            // The pre-framework requery API ate this exception, but the framework API exposes it.
 90→            // We may want to propagate it in the future, but for now maintain the old API and log.
 91→            Timber.e(e, "Failed to close database %s", database.path)
 92→        }
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    // Allows to avoid using new Object[]
 96→    fun query(@Language("SQL") query: String, vararg selectionArgs: Any): Cursor {
 97→        return database.query(query, selectionArgs)
 98→    }
 99→
100→    /**
101→     * Convenience method for querying the database for a single integer result.
102→     *
103→     * @param query The raw SQL query to use.
104→     * @return The integer result of the query.
105→     */
106→    fun queryScalar(@Language("SQL") query: String, vararg selectionArgs: Any): Int {
107→        val scalar: Int
108→        database.query(query, selectionArgs).use { cursor ->
109→            if (!cursor.moveToNext()) {
110→                return 0
111→            }
112→            scalar = cursor.getInt(0)
113→        }
114→        return scalar
115→    }
116→
117→    @Throws(SQLException::class)
118→    fun queryString(@Language("SQL") query: String, vararg bindArgs: Any): String {
119→        database.query(query, bindArgs).use { cursor ->
120→            if (!cursor.moveToNext()) {
121→                throw SQLException("No result for query: $query")
122→            }
123→            return cursor.getString(0)
124→        }
125→    }
126→
127→    fun queryLongScalar(@Language("SQL") query: String, vararg bindArgs: Any): Long {
128→        var scalar: Long
129→        database.query(query, bindArgs).use { cursor ->
130→            if (!cursor.moveToNext()) {
131→                return 0
132→            }
133→            scalar = cursor.getLong(0)
134→        }
135→        return scalar
136→    }
137→
138→    /**
139→     * Convenience method for querying the database for an entire column of long.
140→     *
141→     * @param query The SQL query statement.
142→     * @return An ArrayList with the contents of the specified column.
143→     */
144→    fun queryLongList(@Language("SQL") query: String, vararg bindArgs: Any): ArrayList<Long> {
145→        val results = ArrayList<Long>()
146→        database.query(query, bindArgs).use { cursor ->
147→            while (cursor.moveToNext()) {
148→                results.add(cursor.getLong(0))
149→            }
150→        }
151→        return results
152→    }
153→
154→    /**
155→     * Convenience method for querying the database for an entire column of String.
156→     *
157→     * @param query The SQL query statement.
158→     * @return An ArrayList with the contents of the specified column.
159→     */
160→    fun queryStringList(@Language("SQL") query: String, vararg bindArgs: Any): ArrayList<String> {
161→        val results = ArrayList<String>()
162→        database.query(query, bindArgs).use { cursor ->
163→            while (cursor.moveToNext()) {
164→                results.add(cursor.getString(0))
165→            }
166→        }
167→        return results
168→    }
169→
170→    fun execute(@Language("SQL") sql: String, vararg `object`: Any?) {
171→        val s = sql.trim { it <= ' ' }.lowercase()
172→        // mark modified?
173→        for (mo in MOD_SQL_STATEMENTS) {
174→            if (s.startsWith(mo)) {
175→                break
176→            }
177→        }
178→        database.execSQL(sql, `object`)
179→    }
180→
181→    /**
182→     * WARNING: This is a convenience method that splits SQL scripts into separate queries with semicolons (;)
183→     * as the delimiter. Only use this method on internal functions where we can guarantee that the script does
184→     * not contain any non-statement-terminating semicolons.
185→     */
186→    @KotlinCleanup("""Use Kotlin string. Change split so that there is no empty string after last ";".""")
187→    fun executeScript(@Language("SQL") sql: String) {
188→        @Suppress("PLATFORM_CLASS_MAPPED_TO_KOTLIN")
189→        val queries = java.lang.String(sql).split(";")
190→        for (query in queries) {
191→            database.execSQL(query)
192→        }
193→    }
194→
195→    /** update must always be called via DB in order to mark the db as changed  */
196→    fun update(
197→        table: String,
198→        values: ContentValues,
199→        whereClause: String? = null,
200→        whereArgs: Array<String>? = null

Now let me write a summary of the fix and verify all changes are complete:

## Summary of the Bug Fix

The issue was that the display order for "notes" mode in the Card Browser was not persisting correctly. When users switched to notes mode and changed the sort order, the changes would reset.

### Root Cause

The Card Browser was using the same config keys (`sortType` and `sortBackwards`) for both cards and notes modes. However, the `Collection.findNotes()` function uses different config keys (`noteSortType` and `browserNoteSortBackwards`). This mismatch meant that sort order changes in notes mode were saved to the wrong config keys and never took effect.

### Changes Made

1. **`SortType.kt`** - Added support for separate config keys for cards and notes modes:
   - Added constants for the correct config keys (`SORT_TYPE_NOTES_CONFIG_KEY`, `SORT_BACKWARDS_NOTES_CONFIG_KEY`)
   - Updated `save()` method to accept `CardsOrNotes` parameter and save to the correct config key
   - Updated `fromCol()` method to accept `CardsOrNotes` parameter and read from the correct config key
   - Added helper methods `getSortTypeConfigKey()` and `getSortBackwardsConfigKey()`

2. **`CardBrowser.kt`** - Updated to use the correct config keys based on mode:
   - `onCollectionLoaded()`: Load `cardsOrNotes` first, then use it to load correct sort settings
   - `changeCardOrder()`: Save sort settings to the correct config keys for the current mode
   - `switchCardOrNote()`: Reload `mOrder` and `mOrderAsc` when switching between modes

3. **`Collection.kt`** - Updated `findOneCardByNote()` to respect sort order:
   - Added `order` parameter that is passed to `findNotes()`
   - Preserved the sorted order by maintaining a mapping and returning results in the original sorted order

The fix is complete. Let me provide a summary of all the changes:

## Fix Summary

### Bug
The display order for notes in the Card Browser was not persisting. When users selected "notes" in "toggle cards/notes" and changed the display order, it would reset to "by created time" (oldest to newest) and wouldn't persist across app restarts or when adding new notes.

### Root Cause
The Card Browser was using the same config keys (`sortType` and `sortBackwards`) for both cards and notes modes, but the `Collection.findNotes()` function reads from different config keys (`noteSortType` and `browserNoteSortBackwards`). This mismatch meant sort order changes in notes mode were saved to the wrong keys.

### Files Modified

1. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/model/SortType.kt`**:
   - Added separate config key constants for cards and notes modes
   - Updated `save()` and `fromCol()` to accept `CardsOrNotes` parameter
   - Added helper methods `getSortTypeConfigKey()` and `getSortBackwardsConfigKey()`

2. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardBrowser.kt`**:
   - `onCollectionLoaded()`: Load `cardsOrNotes` first, then use it to load correct sort settings
   - `changeCardOrder()`: Save to correct config keys based on `cardsOrNotes`  
   - `switchCardOrNote()`: Reload sort settings when switching between modes

3. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Collection.kt`**:
   - Updated `findOneCardByNote()` to accept and pass `order` parameter to `findNotes()`
   - Preserved sorted order when mapping from note IDs to card IDs

The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: d6dd8b51). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't display images with `#` in filenames ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? 1. Add a card including an image file containing `#` (hashtag) in its filename --- --- --- --- 3. Show the card in reviewer or previewer ### Expected behaviour The image is shown, as on 2.16.5. (2.16.5) ### Actual behaviour The broken-image icon is shown, and the image isn't shown. ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.6 (568538896be943818ca793dd2635822265712def) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 11 (SDK 30) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = OPPO Model = CPH2013 Hardware = qcom Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; CPH2013 Build/RKQ1.200903.002; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.119 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = df169a4d-29ec-46b6-b114-141ca7e01ba3 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? - Originally reported in Anki Forum: https://forums.ankiweb.net/t/ankiweb-and-ankidroid-do-not-display-images-containing-pound-hashtag-sharp-symbol/42444/1 and dae has commented there. - As reported there, this issue occurs on AnkiWeb, too. ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/c588f585-b23c-4be6-b8f1-8a098a127705) So, strictly speaking, this is not a bug specific to AnkiDroid, but some approach specific to AnkiDroid may be needed, I'm not sure though. - This issue doesn't occur on Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum) and on AnkiMobile. - The `#` is shown as `%23` on the editor of Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum). ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/5ba75763-6007-4668-b036-0cb13e54cdc5) > \> Windows changes it back to a # in the sort field, but the HTML text remains unchanged > > This happens because Anki stores filenames without encoding on disk (legacy reasons), but needs to encode them before they’re shown in the editor. (dae) ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) (2.18alpha6) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion It seems to have already been fixed in the current HEAD. I don't think so, it is ongoing discussion in the PR, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/15909#issuecomment-2001940011 Thank you for the feedback. Not for objection (since I don't have enough knowledge about the topic), just for rewording more accurately and for what it is worth, the issue seems to have been fixed in my debug version build from a854e93 (Mar 17, 2024). On the other hand, a card with the image file added in Anki Desktop is not shown properly (i.e. shown as a broken-image icon) even in the debug version of AnkiDroid. In this case, `#` is shown as it is in the editor and the browser. “#” is a illegal char in HTML, so it’s not supposed to work anywhere if not encoded. Ankidroid encodes it when importing, and AFAIK Anki does it as well. > Ankidroid encodes it when importing In case that users import cards that contain image files with unencoded `#`, for example, importing the following shared deck, why images with `#` in filenames are shown in reviewer on 2.16.5, but not on 2.17.6? - https://ankiweb.net/shared/info/789434294 --- Read now damien's comment about "legacy reasons". Since `#` still isn't supposed to be treated as something else that isn't a URL fragment marker in that context, this is a deliberate anki breakage of the URL scheme. I hate breaking things deliberately without a unavoidable reason, but I'll send the fix ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `.idea/dictionaries/davidallison.xml` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

The fix is complete. Here's a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
Images with `#` in filenames were not displaying in AnkiDroid because:
1. The `parseSourcesToFileScheme` function tried to parse image source attributes (e.g., `image#1.png`) as URIs
2. When parsed as a URI, the `#` character is interpreted as a fragment separator, so `image#1.png` becomes `path="image"` and `fragment="1.png"`
3. Only the path ("image") was used to construct the file URI, losing the rest of the filename

### Solution
Changed `parseSourcesToFileScheme` to:
1. **NOT** parse the attribute value as a URI (which would incorrectly interpret `#` as a fragment separator)
2. Instead, detect URI schemes by checking for:
   - `://` pattern (for http://, https://, file://, etc.)
   - `data:` prefix (for data URIs like `data:image/png;base64,...`)
3. Use the raw attribute value directly as the filename when no scheme is detected
4. The existing `getFileUri()` function properly encodes special characters like `#` as `%23`

### Files Modified

1. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt`**:
   - Modified `replaceWithFileScheme` to avoid parsing raw filenames as URIs
   - Added clear comments explaining the rationale
   - Removed unused `URISyntaxException` import

2. **`AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManagerTest.kt`**:
   - Updated the test that incorrectly expected files with `#` to be skipped
   - Added test for filenames with `#` being properly encoded (`image#1.png` → `image%231.png`)
   - Added test for filenames with multiple special characters
   - Added test for data URIs being properly skipped

 92→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""

 95→                var line: String?
 96→                val sb = StringBuilder()

102→            } catch (e: Exception) {

If not, see . 15→ * 16→ * This file incorporates code under the following license 17→ * https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/2.1.34/pylib/anki/sound.py 18→ * 19→ * Copyright: Ankitects Pty Ltd and contributors 20→ * License: GNU AGPL, version 3 or later; http://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl.html 21→ */ 22→ 23→@file:Suppress("unused") 24→ 25→package com.ichi2.libanki 26→ 27→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol 28→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup 29→ 30→@KotlinCleanup("combine file with Sound.kt if done in libAnki") 31→/** 32→ * Records information about a text to speech tag. 33→ */ 34→data class TTSTag( 35→ val fieldText: String, 36→ /** 37→ * Language may be empty if coming from AnkiDroid reading the whole card 38→ */ 39→ val lang: String, 40→ val voices: List, 41→ val speed: Float, 42→ /** each arg should be in the form 'foo=bar' */ 43→ val otherArgs: List 44→) : AvTag() 45→ 46→/** 47→ * Contains the filename inside a [sound:...] tag. 48→ * 49→ * Video files also use [sound:...]. 50→ */ 51→data class SoundOrVideoTag(val filename: String) : AvTag() 52→ 53→/** In python, this is a union of [TTSTag] and [SoundOrVideoTag] */ 54→open class AvTag 55→ 56→/* Methods */ 57→ 58→val AV_REF_RE = Regex("\\[anki:(play:(.):(\\d+))]") 59→val AV_PLAYLINK_RE = Regex("playsound:(.):(\\d+)") 60→ 61→fun stripAvRefs(text: String) = AV_REF_RE.replace("", text) 62→ 63→fun addPlayIcons(content: String): String { 64→ return AV_REF_RE.replace(content) { match -> 65→ val groups = match.groupValues 66→ val side = groups[2] 67→ val index = groups[3] 68→ val playsound = "playsound:$side:$index" 69→ """ 70→ 72→ """ 73→ } 74→} 75→ 76→/** Extract av tag from playsound:q:x link */ 77→suspend fun getAvTag(card: Card, url: String): AvTag? { 78→ return AV_PLAYLINK_RE.matchEntire(url)?.let { 79→ val values = it.groupValues 80→ val questionSide = values[1] == "q" 81→ val index = values[2].toInt() 82→ val tags = withCol { 83→ if (questionSide) { card.questionAvTags() } else { card.answerAvTags() } 84→ } 85→ if (index < tags.size) { 86→ tags[index] 87→ } else { 88→ null 89→ } 90→ } 91→} 92→

 29→import com.ichi2.anki.ReadText

 37→val VIDEO_EXTENSIONS = setOf("mp4", "mov", "mpg", "mpeg", "mkv", "avi")
 38→
 39→private typealias SoundPath = String
 40→// NICE_TO_HAVE: Abstract, then add tests for #6111
 41→/**
 42→ * Parses, loads and plays sound & video files
 43→ * Called `Sound` Anki uses `[sound:]` for both audio and video
 44→ */
 45→class Sound(private val soundPlayer: SoundPlayer, private val soundDir: String) : SoundPlayer() {
 46→    /**
 47→     * @param soundDir base path to the media files
 48→     */
 49→    constructor(soundDir: String) : this(SoundPlayer(), soundDir)
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * The subset of sounds to involve
 53→     * @param int Used for serialisation
 54→     */
 55→    enum class SoundSide(val int: Int) {
 56→        QUESTION(0), ANSWER(1), QUESTION_AND_ANSWER(2);
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    /** Sounds for the question/answer of a card */
 60→    // Stops code paths where QUESTION_AND_ANSWER is invalid
 61→    enum class SingleSoundSide {
 62→        QUESTION, ANSWER;
 63→
 64→        fun toSoundSide(): SoundSide = when (this) {
 65→            QUESTION -> SoundSide.QUESTION
 66→            ANSWER -> SoundSide.ANSWER
 67→        }
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    /**
 71→     * Stores sounds for the current card. Maps from a side to paths paths
 72→     * Should be accessed via [getSounds]
 73→     */
 74→    private val soundPaths: MutableMap<SoundSide, MutableList<SoundPath>> = EnumMap(SoundSide::class.java)
 75→
 76→    /** Returns a non-empty list of sounds, or null if there are no values */
 77→    @VisibleForTesting
 78→    fun getSounds(side: SoundSide) = getSoundList(side).let {
 79→        if (!it.any()) null else it
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    private fun getSoundList(side: SoundSide): List<SoundPath> {
 83→        if (side == QUESTION_AND_ANSWER) {
 84→            return getSoundList(QUESTION) + getSoundList(ANSWER)
 85→        }
 86→        return soundPaths[side] ?: emptyList()
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    /**
 90→     * Clears current sound paths; call before [expandSounds] + [addSounds]
 91→     * is called for the next card
 92→     */
 93→    fun resetSounds() {
 94→        soundPaths.clear()
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    /**
 98→     * Stores entries to the filepaths for sounds, categorized as belonging to the front (question) or back (answer) of cards.
 99→     * Note that all sounds embedded in the content will be given the same base categorization of question or answer.
100→     * Additionally, the result is to be sorted by the order of appearance on the card.
101→     * @param tags the entries expected in display order
102→     * @param side the base categorization of the sounds in the content
103→     */
104→    fun addSounds(tags: List<SoundOrVideoTag>, side: SingleSoundSide) {
105→        val soundPathCollection = soundPaths.getOrPut(side.toSoundSide()) { mutableListOf() }
106→
107→        Timber.d("Adding %d sounds to side: %s", tags.size, side)
108→        val paths = tags.map { getSoundPath(soundDir, it.filename) }
109→        soundPathCollection.addAll(paths)
110→    }
111→
112→    /** Plays all the sounds for the indicated side(s)  */
113→    fun playSounds(side: SoundSide, errorListener: OnErrorListener?) {
114→        // If there are sounds to play for the current card, start with the first one
115→        val soundPaths = getSounds(side) ?: return
116→        Timber.d("playSounds: playing $side")
117→        this.playSound(
118→            soundPaths[0],
119→            PlayAllCompletionListener(side, errorListener),
120→            errorListener
121→        )
122→    }
123→
124→    /** Returns the total length of all sounds for the side in milliseconds */
125→    fun getSoundsLength(side: SoundSide): Long {
126→        val soundPaths = getSounds(side) ?: return 0
127→        val metaRetriever = MediaMetadataRetriever()
128→        val context = AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext
129→        return soundPaths
130→            .map { Uri.parse(it) }
131→            .sumOf { uri ->
132→                try {
133→                    metaRetriever.getDuration(context, uri)
134→                } catch (e: Exception) {
135→                    Timber.w(
136→                        e,
137→                        "metaRetriever - Error setting Data Source for mediaRetriever (media doesn't exist or forbidden?)."
138→                    )
139→                    0
140→                }
141→            }
142→    }
143→
144→    /**
145→     * Class used to play all sounds for a given card side
146→     */
147→    private inner class PlayAllCompletionListener(
148→        private val side: SoundSide,
149→        private val errorListener: OnErrorListener?
150→    ) : OnCompletionListener {
151→        /** Index of next sound to play inside `getSounds(side)` */
152→        // this is a completion listener: [onCompletion] is first called after the first sound
153→        private var nextIndexToPlay = 1
154→        override fun onCompletion(mp: MediaPlayer) {
155→            val paths = getSounds(side) ?: emptyList() // emptyList -> stopSounds()
156→            // If there are still more sounds to play for the current card, play the next one
157→            if (nextIndexToPlay < paths.size) {
158→                Timber.i("Play all: Playing next sound")
159→                playSound(paths[nextIndexToPlay++], this, errorListener)
160→            } else {
161→                Timber.i("Play all: Completed - releasing sound")
162→                soundPlayer.stopSounds()
163→            }
164→        }
165→    }
166→
167→    override fun stopSounds() {
168→        soundPlayer.stopSounds()
169→        ReadText.stopTts() // TODO: Reconsider design
170→    }
171→
172→    fun hasQuestion(): Boolean = getSounds(QUESTION) != null
173→
174→    fun hasAnswer(): Boolean = getSounds(ANSWER) != null
175→
176→    fun interface OnErrorListener {
177→        fun onError(mp: MediaPlayer?, which: Int, extra: Int, path: String?): ErrorHandling
178→
179→        enum class ErrorHandling {
180→            /** Stop playing audio */
181→            STOP_AUDIO,
182→
183→            /** Continue to the next audio (if any) */
184→            CONTINUE_AUDIO,
185→
186→            /** Retry the current audio */
187→            RETRY_AUDIO
188→        }
189→    }
190→
191→    companion object {
192→        /**
193→         * Pattern used to identify the markers for sound files
194→         */
195→        val SOUND_PATTERN: Pattern = Pattern.compile("\\[sound:([^\\[\\]]*)]")
196→
197→        // TODO join with SOUND_PATTERN
198→        val SOUND_RE = SOUND_PATTERN.toRegex()
199→
200→        /**
201→         * Pattern used to parse URI (according to http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#page-50)
202→         */
203→        private val sUriPattern =
204→            Pattern.compile("^(([^:/?#]+):)?(//([^/?#]*))?([^?#]*)(\\?([^#]*))?(#(.*))?$")
205→
206→        /** Extract SoundOrVideoTag instances from content where sound tags are in the form: [sound:filename.mp3]  */
207→        @CheckResult
208→        fun extractTagsFromLegacyContent(content: String): List<SoundOrVideoTag> {
209→            val matcher = SOUND_PATTERN.matcher(content)
210→            // While there is matches of the pattern for sound markers
211→            val ret = mutableListOf<SoundOrVideoTag>()
212→            while (matcher.find()) {
213→                // Get the sound file name
214→                val sound = matcher.group(1)!!
215→                ret.add(SoundOrVideoTag(sound))
216→            }
217→            return ret
218→        }
219→
220→        /**
221→         * expandSounds takes content with embedded sound file placeholders and expands them to reference the actual media
222→         * file
223→         *
224→         * @param content -- card content to be rendered that may contain embedded audio
225→         * @return -- the same content but in a format that will render working play buttons when audio was embedded
226→         */
227→        fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
228→            return addPlayIcons(content)
229→        }
230→
231→        /**
232→         * @param soundDir -- base path to the media files.
233→         * @param sound -- path to the sound file from the card content.
234→         * @return absolute URI to the sound file.
235→         */
236→        fun getSoundPath(soundDir: String, sound: String): String {
237→            val trimmedSound = sound.trim { it <= ' ' }
238→            return if (hasURIScheme(trimmedSound)) {
239→                trimmedSound
240→            } else {
241→                soundDir + Uri.encode(sound.trimEnd())
242→            }
243→        }
244→
245→        /**
246→         * @param path -- path to the sound file from the card content.
247→         * @return true if path is well-formed URI and contains URI scheme.
248→         */
249→        private fun hasURIScheme(path: String): Boolean {
250→            val uriMatcher = sUriPattern.matcher(path.trim { it <= ' ' })
251→            return uriMatcher.matches() && uriMatcher.group(2) != null
252→        }
253→    }
254→}
255→
256→open class SoundPlayer {
257→    val currentAudioUri: String?
258→        get() = mCurrentAudioUri?.toString()
259→
260→    val isCurrentAudioFinished: Boolean
261→        get() = mMediaPlayer == null
262→
263→    /**
264→     * Media player used to play the sounds. It's Nullable and that it is set only if a sound is playing or paused, otherwise it is null.
265→     */
266→    private var mMediaPlayer: MediaPlayer? = null
267→
268→    private var mCurrentAudioUri: Uri? = null
269→
270→    /**
271→     * AudioManager to request/release audio focus
272→     */
273→    private var mAudioManager: AudioManager? = null
274→
275→    private var mAudioFocusRequest: AudioFocusRequest? = null
276→
277→    /**
278→     * Plays the given sound or video and sets playAllListener if available on media player to start next media.
279→     * If videoView is null and the media is a video, then a request is sent to start the VideoPlayer Activity
280→     */
281→    fun playSound(
282→        soundPath: String,
283→        onCompletionListener: OnCompletionListener?,
284→        errorListener: Sound.OnErrorListener?
285→    ) {
286→        Timber.d("Playing single sound")
287→        val completionListener = onCompletionListener ?: SingleSoundCompletionListener()
288→        val errorHandler = errorListener
289→            ?: Sound.OnErrorListener { _: MediaPlayer?, what: Int, extra: Int, _: String? ->
290→                Timber.w("Media Error: (%d, %d). Calling OnCompletionListener", what, extra)
291→                CONTINUE_AUDIO
292→            }
293→        playSoundInternal(soundPath, completionListener, errorHandler)
294→    }
295→
296→    /**
297→     * Plays a sound without ensuring that the playAllListener will release the audio
298→     */
299→    private fun playSoundInternal(
300→        soundPath: String,
301→        completionListener: OnCompletionListener,
302→        errorHandler: Sound.OnErrorListener
303→    ) {
304→        Timber.d("Playing %s", soundPath)
305→        val soundUri = Uri.parse(soundPath)
306→        mCurrentAudioUri = soundUri
307→
308→        val context = AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext
309→
310→        // Play media
311→        fun playMedia() {
312→            try {
313→                // Create media player
314→                if (mMediaPlayer == null) {
315→                    Timber.d("Creating media player for playback")
316→                    mMediaPlayer = MediaPlayer()
317→                } else {
318→                    Timber.d("Resetting media for playback")
319→                    mMediaPlayer!!.reset()
320→                }
321→                val mediaPlayer = mMediaPlayer!!
322→                mAudioManager =
323→                    mAudioManager ?: context.getSystemService(Context.AUDIO_SERVICE) as AudioManager
324→
325→                mediaPlayer.setOnErrorListener { mp: MediaPlayer?, which: Int, extra: Int ->
326→                    val errorHandling = errorHandler.onError(
327→                        mp,
328→                        which,
329→                        extra,
330→                        soundPath
331→                    )
332→                    // returning false calls onComplete()
333→                    return@setOnErrorListener when (errorHandling) {
334→                        CONTINUE_AUDIO -> false
335→                        Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.RETRY_AUDIO -> {
336→                            playMedia()
337→                            true
338→                        }
339→                        Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.STOP_AUDIO -> {
340→                            stopSounds()
341→                            true
342→                        }
343→                    }
344→                }
345→                // Setup the MediaPlayer
346→                mediaPlayer.setDataSource(context, soundUri)
347→                mediaPlayer.setAudioAttributes(
348→                    AudioAttributes.Builder()
349→                        .setContentType(AudioAttributes.CONTENT_TYPE_MUSIC)
350→                        .build()
351→                )
352→                mediaPlayer.setOnPreparedListener {
353→                    Timber.d("Starting media player")
354→                    it.start()
355→                }
356→                mediaPlayer.setOnCompletionListener(completionListener)
357→                mediaPlayer.prepareAsync()
358→                Timber.d("Requesting audio focus")
359→
360→                // Set mAudioFocusRequest for API 26 and above.
361→                if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
362→                    mAudioFocusRequest =
363→                        AudioFocusRequest.Builder(AudioManager.AUDIOFOCUS_GAIN_TRANSIENT_MAY_DUCK)
364→                            .setOnAudioFocusChangeListener(audioFocusChangeListener)
365→                            .build()
366→                }
367→                CompatHelper.compat.requestAudioFocus(
368→                    mAudioManager!!,
369→                    audioFocusChangeListener,
370→                    mAudioFocusRequest
371→                )
372→            } catch (e: Exception) {
373→                Timber.e(e, "playSounds - Error reproducing sound %s", soundPath)
374→                when (
375→                    errorHandler.onError(
376→                        mMediaPlayer,
377→                        MediaPlayer.MEDIA_ERROR_UNSUPPORTED,
378→                        0,
379→                        soundPath
380→                    )
381→                ) {
382→                    CONTINUE_AUDIO -> {
383→                        Timber.d("Force playing next sound.")
384→                        completionListener.onCompletion(mMediaPlayer)
385→                    }
386→                    Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.STOP_AUDIO -> stopSounds()
387→                    Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.RETRY_AUDIO -> playMedia()
388→                }
389→            }
390→        }
391→
392→        playMedia()
393→    }
394→
395→    /**
396→     * Releases the sound.
397→     */
398→    private fun releaseSound() {
399→        Timber.d("Releasing sounds and abandoning audio focus")
400→        mMediaPlayer?.let {
401→            // Required to remove warning: "mediaplayer went away with unhandled events"
402→            // https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9609479/android-mediaplayer-went-away-with-unhandled-events
403→            it.reset()
404→            it.release()
405→            mMediaPlayer = null
406→        }
407→        mAudioManager?.let {
408→            // mAudioFocusRequest was initialised for API 26 and above in playSoundInternal().
409→            CompatHelper.compat.abandonAudioFocus(it, audioFocusChangeListener, mAudioFocusRequest)
410→            mAudioManager = null
411→        }
412→    }
413→
414→    open fun stopSounds() {
415→        mMediaPlayer?.let {
416→            it.stop()
417→            // TODO: Inefficient. Determine whether we want to release or stop, don't do both
418→            // Ensure `currentAudioUri` etc... still work when we do this
419→            releaseSound()
420→        }
421→    }
422→
423→    fun playOrPauseSound() {
424→        mMediaPlayer?.let {
425→            if (it.isPlaying) {
426→                it.pause()
427→            } else {
428→                it.start()
429→            }
430→        }
431→    }
432→
433→    /** #5414 - Ensures playing a single sound performs cleanup  */
434→    private inner class SingleSoundCompletionListener : OnCompletionListener {
435→        override fun onCompletion(mp: MediaPlayer) {
436→            Timber.d("Single Sound completed")
437→            releaseSound()
438→        }
439→    }
440→
441→    companion object {
442→        /**
443→         * Listener to handle audio focus. Currently blank because we're not respecting losing focus from other apps.
444→         */
445→        private val audioFocusChangeListener = OnAudioFocusChangeListener { }
446→    }
447→}

449→@Throws(Exception::class)
450→private fun MediaMetadataRetriever.getDuration(context: Context, uri: Uri): Long {
451→    this.setDataSource(context, uri)
452→    val duration = this.extractMetadata(MediaMetadataRetriever.METADATA_KEY_DURATION)
453→    return duration!!.toLong()
454→}

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.res.Resources
 21→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.ReviewerCustomFonts
 24→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 25→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 26→import timber.log.Timber
 27→import java.io.BufferedReader
 28→import java.io.IOException
 29→import java.io.InputStream
 30→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 31→
 32→class HtmlGenerator(
 33→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 34→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 35→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 36→    val resources: Resources
 37→) {
 38→
 39→    @CheckResult
 40→    fun generateHtml(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side): CardHtml {
 41→        return CardHtml.createInstance(card, reload, side, this)
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: Side): String {
 45→        return when (side) {
 46→            Side.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 47→            Side.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 48→        }
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 52→        return Sound.expandSounds(content)
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    companion object {
 56→        fun createInstance(
 57→            context: Context,
 58→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 59→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 60→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 61→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 62→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 63→
 64→            return HtmlGenerator(
 65→                typeAnswer,
 66→                cardAppearance,
 67→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 68→                context.resources
 69→            )
 70→        }
 71→
 72→        /**
 73→         * Load the template for the card
 74→         */
 75→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 76→            try {
 77→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 78→                return CardTemplate(data)
 79→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 80→                Timber.w(e)
 81→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→
 85→        /**
 86→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 87→         *
 88→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 89→         * @return String version of the InputStream
 90→         */
 91→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
 92→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
 93→            try {
 94→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
 95→                var line: String?
 96→                val sb = StringBuilder()
 97→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
 98→                    sb.append(line)
 99→                }
100→                rd.close()
101→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
102→            } catch (e: Exception) {
103→                Timber.w(e)
104→            }
105→            return contentOfMyInputStream
106→        }
107→    }
108→}
109→

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.res.Resources
 21→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.ReviewerCustomFonts
 25→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 26→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 27→import timber.log.Timber
 28→import java.io.BufferedReader
 29→import java.io.IOException
 30→import java.io.InputStream
 31→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 32→
 33→class HtmlGenerator(
 34→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 35→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 36→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 37→    val resources: Resources,
 38→    private val showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean = true
 39→) {
 40→
 41→    @CheckResult
 42→    fun generateHtml(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side): CardHtml {
 43→        return CardHtml.createInstance(card, reload, side, this)
 44→    }
 45→
 46→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: Side): String {
 47→        return when (side) {
 48→            Side.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 49→            Side.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 50→        }
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 54→        return Sound.expandSounds(content, showAudioPlayButtons)
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    companion object {
 58→        fun createInstance(
 59→            context: Context,
 60→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 61→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 62→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 63→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 64→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 65→            val showAudioPlayButtons = preferences.getBoolean(
 66→                context.getString(R.string.show_audio_play_buttons_key),
 67→                true
 68→            )
 69→
 70→            return HtmlGenerator(
 71→                typeAnswer,
 72→                cardAppearance,
 73→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 74→                context.resources,
 75→                showAudioPlayButtons
 76→            )
 77→        }
 78→
 79→        /**
 80→         * Load the template for the card
 81→         */
 82→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 83→            try {
 84→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 85→                return CardTemplate(data)
 86→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 87→                Timber.w(e)
 88→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 89→            }
 90→        }
 91→
 92→        /**
 93→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 94→         *
 95→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 96→         * @return String version of the InputStream
 97→         */
 98→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
 99→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
100→            try {
101→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
102→                var line: String?
103→                val sb = StringBuilder()
104→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
105→                    sb.append(line)
106→                }
107→                rd.close()
108→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
109→            } catch (e: Exception) {
110→                Timber.w(e)
111→            }
112→            return contentOfMyInputStream
113→        }
114→    }
115→}

If not, see . 15→ * 16→ * This file incorporates code under the following license 17→ * https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/2.1.34/pylib/anki/sound.py 18→ * 19→ * Copyright: Ankitects Pty Ltd and contributors 20→ * License: GNU AGPL, version 3 or later; http://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl.html 21→ */ 22→ 23→@file:Suppress("unused") 24→ 25→package com.ichi2.libanki 26→ 27→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol 28→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup 29→ 30→@KotlinCleanup("combine file with Sound.kt if done in libAnki") 31→/** 32→ * Records information about a text to speech tag. 33→ */ 34→data class TTSTag( 35→ val fieldText: String, 36→ /** 37→ * Language may be empty if coming from AnkiDroid reading the whole card 38→ */ 39→ val lang: String, 40→ val voices: List, 41→ val speed: Float, 42→ /** each arg should be in the form 'foo=bar' */ 43→ val otherArgs: List 44→) : AvTag() 45→ 46→/** 47→ * Contains the filename inside a [sound:...] tag. 48→ * 49→ * Video files also use [sound:...]. 50→ */ 51→data class SoundOrVideoTag(val filename: String) : AvTag() 52→ 53→/** In python, this is a union of [TTSTag] and [SoundOrVideoTag] */ 54→open class AvTag 55→ 56→/* Methods */ 57→ 58→val AV_REF_RE = Regex("\\[anki:(play:(.):(\\d+))]") 59→val AV_PLAYLINK_RE = Regex("playsound:(.):(\\d+)") 60→ 61→fun stripAvRefs(text: String) = AV_REF_RE.replace("", text) 62→ 63→fun addPlayIcons(content: String, showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean = true): String { 64→ if (!showAudioPlayButtons) { 65→ // Strip the av refs without adding play icons 66→ return AV_REF_RE.replace(content, "") 67→ } 68→ return AV_REF_RE.replace(content) { match -> 69→ val groups = match.groupValues 70→ val side = groups[2] 71→ val index = groups[3] 72→ val playsound = "playsound:$side:$index" 73→ """ 74→ 76→ """ 77→ } 78→} 79→ 80→/** Extract av tag from playsound:q:x link */ 81→suspend fun getAvTag(card: Card, url: String): AvTag? { 82→ return AV_PLAYLINK_RE.matchEntire(url)?.let { 83→ val values = it.groupValues 84→ val questionSide = values[1] == "q" 85→ val index = values[2].toInt() 86→ val tags = withCol { 87→ if (questionSide) { card.questionAvTags() } else { card.answerAvTags() } 88→ } 89→ if (index < tags.size) { 90→ tags[index] 91→ } else { 92→ null 93→ } 94→ } 95→} 96→

1. **String resource** (`Anki...]
The implementation is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. Added String Resource (`AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml`)
Added the preference title string:
```xml
<string name="show_audio_play_buttons" maxLength="41">Show play buttons on cards with audio</string>
```

### 2. Updated Preference XML (`AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml`)
Updated the SwitchPreferenceCompat to use the proper title string resource instead of the `tools:title` attribute:
```xml
<SwitchPreferenceCompat
    android:key="@string/show_audio_play_buttons_key"
    android:defaultValue="true"
    android:title="@string/show_audio_play_buttons"
    />
```

### 3. Modified HtmlGenerator.kt (`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/HtmlGenerator.kt`)
- Added `com.ichi2.anki.R` import
- Added `showAudioPlayButtons` parameter to constructor (default `true`)
- Updated `expandSounds()` to pass the preference value to `Sound.expandSounds()`
- Updated `createInstance()` to read the preference from shared preferences

### 4. Modified Sound.kt (`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Sound.kt`)
- Updated `expandSounds()` method signature to accept `showAudioPlayButtons` parameter (default `true`)
- Passes the parameter to `addPlayIcons()`

### 5. Modified SoundKt.kt (`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/SoundKt.kt`)
- Updated `addPlayIcons()` to accept `showAudioPlayButtons` parameter (default `true`)
- When `showAudioPlayButtons` is `false`, the function strips the `[anki:play:...]` tags without adding play buttons (similar to Anki Desktop's `hide_audio_play_buttons` behavior)

## Behavior
- **Default (ON)**: Play buttons are shown on cards with audio (same as current behavior)
- **OFF**: Audio tags are stripped from the card display without showing play buttons, but audio still plays via autoplay or the replay button in the app bar

The preference key `show_audio_play_buttons_key` was already defined in `preferences.xml` at line 45 with value `showAudioPlayButtons`.

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.175 (commit: 05a5e229).

## Issue to Resolve

[BUG]: Edit Note Preview does not show the expected card
### Checked for duplicates?

- [X] This issue is not a duplicate

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

open the browser
Select a card which is not card 1 of the note
long press
in the menu, tap "edit note"
Tap preview

### Expected behaviour

You should see the selected card in the preview mode (or at least, the card should be consistent with the card underlined at the bottom of the editor)

### Actual behaviour

First card of the note is shown (At least I assume. In the case I was interested in, I saw the "this card is empty" warning)

### Debug info

```text
2.17 alpha 3
```


### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

_No response_

### Research

- [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app)
- [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue
- [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This will be slightly easier to fix once https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/14719 goes in:

* `ordinal` is provided to the editor: 
* `ordinal` is `ord`, NOT the index into the array
  * `{{c1::AA}} {{c3::BB}}` returns the second ordinal as 2

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L84

`mOrdinal` is not used to set `templateIndex` in this branch:

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L233-L241

`setCurrentCardFromNoteEditorBundle` uses `templateIndex = 0` and displays the wrong card

I've added two regression tests to help guide a fix

One undefined question: what should the note editor previewer show if one of the cards is empty? Should it be skipped, or displayed

Should this be different between:
* Card Template Preview
* Note Editor Preview
* Card Browser Preview (less likely to want to see empty cards)

For when a few dependencies have been merged:

```kotlin
    private fun indexFromOrdinal(col: Collection, ordinal: Int): Int {
        return when (mEditedNotetype?.isCloze) {
            true -> col.clozeNumbersInNote(col.newNote(mEditedNotetype!!)).indexOf(mOrdinal)
            else -> ordinal
        }
    }
```
Is that correct? with c1 and c3, that will return [1,3], and ord 3 will be an index out of bounds, won't it?

Edit: sorry, ignore me - I mistook this for numbers[ord] instead of searching for the given value.
In the note editor preview, there seems to be no specific card. So I don’t understand what sense it would make to have this behavior on the note editor preview.

For card template preview, I certainly believe you should see the preview for the card you are looking it.

For the browser, if the card selected in the browser is empty, I believe you should show that the card is empty. That’s an information I certainly may want to have if it is the case. Because either:
* there was no recent "clean card"
* there was a clear card, and this is card 1, generated in order to ensure the note is not deleted. 

The first case may be interesting, the second is certainly something that should be noted.

Also, if we touch this part of the code, it may be worth to give more context, by stating either:
* This card is empty. The n cards of this note are empty.
* This card is empty. Jump to the first non-empty card. (with a link/button)

And I guess, potentially, a checkbox to skip the empty card when browsing which cards will be generated.


If you agree it makes sense, I assume it should be many different bugs so they can be easily tackled

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Collection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

462→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
463→
464→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
465→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
466→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
467→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
468→            // timeboxing disabled
469→            return null
470→        }
471→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
472→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
473→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
474→            TimeboxReached(
475→                limit,
476→                sched.reps - mStartReps
477→            ).also {
478→                startTimebox()
479→            }
480→        } else {
481→            null
482→        }
483→    }

546→     * Basic integrity check. Only used by unit tests.

548→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
549→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
550→        // cards without notes
551→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
552→            return false
553→        }
554→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
555→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
556→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
557→        ) > 0
558→        // notes without cards or models
559→        if (badNotes) {
560→            return false
561→        }
562→        // invalid ords
563→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
564→            // ignore clozes
565→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
566→                continue
567→            }
568→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
569→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
570→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
571→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
572→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
573→                tmpls.length(),
574→                m.getLong("id")
575→            ) > 0
576→            if (badOrd) {
577→                return false
578→            }
579→        }
580→        return true
581→    }

583→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
584→        if (!debugLog) return
585→
586→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
587→
588→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
589→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
590→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
591→
592→        val objectsString = objects
593→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
594→            .joinToString(", ")
595→
596→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
597→    }
598→
599→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
600→        mLogHnd?.let {
601→            try {
602→                it.println(s)
603→            } catch (e: Exception) {
604→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
605→            }
606→        }
607→        Timber.d(s)
608→    }
609→
610→    private fun _openLog() {
611→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
612→        if (!debugLog) {
613→            return
614→        }
615→        try {
616→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
617→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
618→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
619→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
620→                    lpath2.delete()
621→                }
622→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
623→            }
624→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
625→        } catch (e: IOException) {
626→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
627→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
628→            debugLog = false
629→        }
630→    }
631→
632→    private fun _closeLog() {
633→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
634→        mLogHnd?.close()
635→        mLogHnd = null
636→    }

The fix is complete. Here's a summary of the changes made:

## Summary

### Problem
When previewing a note from the Note Editor (via Edit Note → Preview), the previewer always displayed the first card (card index 0) instead of the card that was originally selected by the user.

### Root Cause
In `CardTemplatePreviewer.onCollectionLoaded()`, when loading from the Note Editor (`mNoteEditorBundle != null`):
- The `mOrdinal` value (the card's `ord`) was received from the intent but never used
- `cardIndex` was initialized to 0 and used directly without conversion from `mOrdinal`

### Fix
1. **Added `ordinalToIndex()` function** - Converts a card's `ord` value to a card index:
   - For **non-cloze note types**: ordinal equals index, so return as-is
   - For **cloze note types**: Create a temporary note with field values, compute the cloze numbers in the note, and find the position of `ordinal + 1` in that list. For example, with `{{c1::A}} {{c3::B}}`:
     - Cloze numbers are `[1, 3]`
     - Ordinal 0 (c1) → index 0
     - Ordinal 2 (c3) → index 1

2. **Updated `onCollectionLoaded()`** to:
   - Call `ordinalToIndex()` to properly set `cardIndex` from `mOrdinal`
   - Properly initialize the prev/next navigation buttons based on the actual `cardIndex`

3. **Enabled the previously ignored tests** for Issue 14694:
   - `The ordinal provided is used (standard) - Issue 14694`
   - `The ordinal provided is used (cloze)- Issue 14694`

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: a854e93b). ## Issue to Resolve [Bug] AnkiDroidJS API returns incorrect data type ###### Reproduction Steps 1. Add a new basic type note. 2. Add the following code in the front template of the note: ```html api.ankiGetCardQueue() return value type: {{Front}} ``` 3. Study the new card in AnkiDroid. ###### Expected Result According to the `AnkiDroid Javascript API wiki`, the return value type of api.ankiGetCardQueue() is [`int`](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/AnkiDroid-Javascript-API#type-of-return-value-17). Therefore, it should display `api.ankiGetCardQueue() return value type: int` on the front of the card. ###### Actual Result It displays `api.ankiGetCardQueue() return value type: string` in the front of the card. ###### Debug info ``` AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.5 (1c1aa94ec466f37d33fd2f75020d9f9021ac811e) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 10 (SDK 29) ProductFlavor = full Manufacturer = HUAWEI Model = MXW-AN00 Hardware = mt6873 Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 10; MXW-AN00 Build/HONORMXW-AN00; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/92.0.4515.105 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = 82400b65-bd87-4883-a1f6-b911150f725b Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ###### Research *Enter an [x] character to confirm the points below:* - [x] I have read the [support page](https://ankidroid.org/docs/help.html) and am reporting a bug or enhancement request specific to AnkiDroid - [x] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [x] I have searched for similar existing issues here and on the user forum - [x] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Based on the AnkiDroidJS API wiki, almost every API that was expected to return data of type int, long, or boolean, is actually returning data of type string. I will push the fix which return correct data types. ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AnkiDroidJsAPI.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality
