 58→open class AnkiDroidJsAPI(private val activity: AbstractFlashcardViewer) {
 59→    private val currentCard: Card
 60→        get() = activity.currentCard!!
 61→
 62→    private val getColUnsafe: Collection
 63→        get() = activity.getColUnsafe
 64→
 65→    /**
 66→     Javascript Interface class for calling Java function from AnkiDroid WebView
 67→     see js-api.js for available functions
 68→     */
 69→
 70→    private val context: Context = activity
 71→
 72→    // Text to speech
 73→    private val talker = JavaScriptTTS()
 74→
 75→    // Speech to Text
 76→    private val speechRecognizer = JavaScriptSTT(context)
 77→
 78→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, boolean: Boolean): ByteArray {
 79→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, boolean.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, int: Int): ByteArray {
 83→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, int.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, long: Long): ByteArray {
 87→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, long.toString()).toString().toByteArray()
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    open fun convertToByteArray(apiContract: ApiContract, string: String): ByteArray {
 91→        return ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, string).toString().toByteArray()
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    /**
 95→     * The method parse json data and return api contract object
 96→     * @param byteArray
 97→     * @return apiContract or null
 98→     */
 99→    private fun parseJsApiContract(byteArray: ByteArray): ApiContract? {
100→        try {
101→            val data = JSONObject(byteArray.decodeToString())
102→            val cardSuppliedApiVersion = data.optString("version", "")
103→            val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact = data.optString("developer", "")
104→            val cardSuppliedData = data.optString("data", "")
105→            val isValid = requireApiVersion(cardSuppliedApiVersion, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
106→            return ApiContract(isValid, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact, cardSuppliedData)
107→        } catch (j: JSONException) {
108→            Timber.w(j)
109→            activity.runOnUiThread {
110→                activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, j.localizedMessage))
111→            }
112→        }
113→        return null
114→    }
115→
116→    /*
117→     * see 02-strings.xml
118→     * Show Error code when mark card or flag card unsupported
119→     * 1 - mark card
120→     * 2 - flag card
121→     *
122→     * show developer contact if js api used in card is deprecated
123→     */
124→    private fun showDeveloperContact(errorCode: Int, apiDevContact: String) {
125→        val errorMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.anki_js_error_code, errorCode)
126→        val snackbarMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.api_version_developer_contact, apiDevContact, errorMsg)
127→
128→        activity.showSnackbar(snackbarMsg, Snackbar.LENGTH_INDEFINITE) {
129→            setMaxLines(3)
130→            setAction(R.string.reviewer_invalid_api_version_visit_documentation) {
131→                activity.openUrl(Uri.parse("https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki"))
132→            }
133→        }
134→    }
135→
136→    /**
137→     * Supplied api version must be equal to current api version to call mark card, toggle flag functions etc.
138→     */
139→    private fun requireApiVersion(apiVer: String, apiDevContact: String): Boolean {
140→        try {
141→            if (apiDevContact.isEmpty() || apiVer.isEmpty()) {
142→                activity.runOnUiThread {
143→                    activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, ""))
144→                }
145→                return false
146→            }
147→            val versionCurrent = Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.sCurrentJsApiVersion)
148→            val versionSupplied = Version.parse(apiVer)
149→
150→            /*
151→            * if api major version equals to supplied major version then return true and also check for minor version and patch version
152→            * show toast for update and contact developer if need updates
153→            * otherwise return false
154→            */
155→            return when {
156→                versionSupplied == versionCurrent -> {
157→                    true
158→                }
159→                versionSupplied.isLowerThan(versionCurrent) -> {
160→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
161→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.update_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
162→                    }
163→                    versionSupplied.isHigherThanOrEquivalentTo(Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.sMinimumJsApiVersion))
164→                }
165→                else -> {
166→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
167→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.valid_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
168→                    }
169→                    false
170→                }
171→            }
172→        } catch (e: Exception) {
173→            Timber.w(e, "requireApiVersion::exception")
174→        }
175→        return false
176→    }
177→
178→    /**
179→     * Handle js api request,
180→     * some of the methods are overridden in Reviewer.kt and default values are returned.
181→     * @param methodName
182→     * @param bytes
183→     * @param returnDefaultValues `true` if default values should be returned (if non-[Reviewer])
184→     * @return
185→     */
186→    open suspend fun handleJsApiRequest(methodName: String, bytes: ByteArray, returnDefaultValues: Boolean = true) = withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
187→        // the method will call to set the card supplied data and is valid version for each api request
188→        val apiContract = parseJsApiContract(bytes)!!
189→        // if api not init or is api not called from reviewer then return default -1
190→        // also other action will not be modified
191→        if (!apiContract.isValid or returnDefaultValues) {
192→            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, -1)
193→        }
194→
195→        val cardDataForJsAPI = activity.getCardDataForJsApi()
196→        val apiParams = apiContract.cardSuppliedData
197→
198→        return@withContext when (methodName) {
199→            "init" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
200→            "newCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.newCardCount)
201→            "lrnCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.lrnCardCount)
202→            "revCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.revCardCount)
203→            "eta" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.eta)
204→            "nextTime1" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime1)
205→            "nextTime2" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime2)
206→            "nextTime3" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime3)
207→            "nextTime4" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime4)
208→            "toggleFlag" -> {
209→                if (apiParams !in flagCommands) {
210→                    showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeFlagCard, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
211→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
212→                }
213→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.executeCommand(flagCommands[apiParams]!!))
214→            }
215→            "markCard" -> processAction({ activity.executeCommand(ViewerCommand.MARK) }, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeMarkCard, ::convertToByteArray)
216→            "buryCard" -> processAction(activity::buryCard, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeBuryCard, ::convertToByteArray)
217→            "buryNote" -> processAction(activity::buryNote, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeBuryNote, ::convertToByteArray)
218→            "suspendCard" -> processAction(activity::suspendCard, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendCard, ::convertToByteArray)
219→            "suspendNote" -> processAction(activity::suspendNote, apiContract, ankiJsErrorCodeSuspendNote, ::convertToByteArray)
220→            "setCardDue" -> {
221→                try {
222→                    val days = apiParams.toInt()
223→                    if (days < 0 || days > 9999) {
224→                        showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeSetDue, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
225→                        return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
226→                    }
227→                    activity.launchCatchingTask {
228→                        activity.rescheduleCards(listOf(currentCard.id), days)
229→                    }
230→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
231→                } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
232→                    showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeSetDue, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
233→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
234→                }
235→            }
236→            "resetProgress" -> {
237→                val cardIds = listOf(currentCard.id)
238→                activity.launchCatchingTask { activity.resetCards(cardIds) }
239→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
240→            }
241→            "cardMark" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.note(getColUnsafe).hasTag(getColUnsafe, "marked"))
242→            "cardFlag" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.userFlag())
243→            "cardReps" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.reps)
244→            "cardInterval" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.ivl)
245→            "cardFactor" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.factor)
246→            "cardMod" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.mod)
247→            "cardId" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.id)
248→            "cardNid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.nid)
249→            "cardType" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.type)
250→            "cardDid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.did)
251→            "cardLeft" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.left)
252→            "cardODid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.oDid)
253→            "cardODue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.oDue)
254→            "cardQueue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.queue)
255→            "cardLapses" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.lapses)
256→            "cardDue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.due)
257→            "deckName" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, Decks.basename(activity.getColUnsafe.decks.name(currentCard.did)))
258→            "isActiveNetworkMetered" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, NetworkUtils.isActiveNetworkMetered())
259→            "ttsSetLanguage" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setLanguage(apiParams))
260→            "ttsSpeak" -> {
261→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
262→                val text = jsonObject.getString("text")
263→                val queueMode = jsonObject.getInt("queueMode")
264→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.speak(text, queueMode))
265→            }
266→            "ttsIsSpeaking" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.isSpeaking)
267→            "ttsSetPitch" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setPitch(apiParams.toFloat()))
268→            "ttsSetSpeechRate" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setSpeechRate(apiParams.toFloat()))
269→            "ttsFieldModifierIsAvailable" -> {
270→                // Know if {{tts}} is supported - issue #10443
271→                // Return false for now
272→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
273→            }
274→            "ttsStop" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.stop())
275→            "searchCard" -> {
276→                val intent = Intent(context, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
277→                    putExtra("currentCard", currentCard.id)
278→                    putExtra("search_query", apiParams)
279→                }
280→                activity.startActivity(intent)
281→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
282→            }
283→            "searchCardWithCallback" -> ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract)
284→            "isDisplayingAnswer" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isDisplayingAnswer)
285→            "addTagToCard" -> {
286→                activity.runOnUiThread { activity.showTagsDialog() }
287→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
288→            }
289→            "isInFullscreen" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isFullscreen)
290→            "isTopbarShown" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.prefShowTopbar)
291→            "isInNightMode" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isInNightMode)
292→            "enableHorizontalScrollbar" -> {
293→                activity.webView!!.isHorizontalScrollBarEnabled = apiParams.toBoolean()
294→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
295→            }
296→            "enableVerticalScrollbar" -> {
297→                activity.webView!!.isVerticalScrollBarEnabled = apiParams.toBoolean()
298→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
299→            }
300→            "showNavigationDrawer" -> {
301→                activity.onNavigationPressed()
302→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
303→            }
304→            "showOptionsMenu" -> {
305→                activity.openOptionsMenu()
306→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
307→            }
308→            "showToast" -> {
309→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
310→                val text = jsonObject.getString("text")
311→                val shortLength = jsonObject.optBoolean("shortLength", true)
312→                val msgDecode = activity.decodeUrl(text)
313→                UIUtils.showThemedToast(context, msgDecode, shortLength)
314→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
315→            }
316→            "showAnswer" -> {
317→                activity.displayCardAnswer()
318→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
319→            }
320→            "answerEase1" -> {
321→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(AbstractFlashcardViewer.EASE_1)
322→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
323→            }
324→            "answerEase2" -> {
325→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(AbstractFlashcardViewer.EASE_2)
326→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
327→            }
328→            "answerEase3" -> {
329→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(AbstractFlashcardViewer.EASE_3)
330→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
331→            }
332→            "answerEase4" -> {
333→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(AbstractFlashcardViewer.EASE_4)
334→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
335→            }
336→            "sttSetLanguage" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.setLanguage(apiParams))
337→            "sttStart" -> {
338→                val callback = object : JavaScriptSTT.SpeechRecognitionCallback {
339→                    override fun onResult(results: List<String>) {
340→                        activity.lifecycleScope.launch {
341→                            val apiResult = ApiResult(true, Json.encodeToString(ListSerializer(String.serializer()), results))
342→                            val jsonEncodedString = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) { JSONObject.quote(apiResult.toString()) }
343→                            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSttResult($jsonEncodedString)", null)
344→                        }
345→                    }
346→                    override fun onError(errorMessage: String) {
347→                        activity.lifecycleScope.launch {
348→                            val apiResult = ApiResult(false, errorMessage)
349→                            val jsonEncodedString = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) { JSONObject.quote(apiResult.toString()) }
350→                            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSttResult($jsonEncodedString)", null)
351→                        }
352→                    }
353→                }
354→                speechRecognizer.setRecognitionCallback(callback)
355→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.start())
356→            }
357→            "sttStop" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.stop())
358→            else -> {
359→                showDeveloperContact(ankiJsErrorCodeError, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
360→                throw Exception("unhandled request: $methodName")
361→            }
362→        }
363→    }
364→
365→    private fun processAction(
366→        action: () -> Boolean,
367→        apiContract: ApiContract,
368→        errorCode: Int,
369→        conversion: (ApiContract, Boolean) -> ByteArray
370→    ): ByteArray {
371→        val status = action()
372→        if (!status) {
373→            showDeveloperContact(errorCode, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
374→        }
375→        return conversion(apiContract, status)
376→    }
377→
378→    private suspend fun ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract: ApiContract): ByteArray = withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
379→        val cards = try {
380→            searchForCards(apiContract.cardSuppliedData, SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering(), CardsOrNotes.CARDS)
381→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
382→            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript(
383→                "console.log('${context.getString(R.string.search_card_js_api_no_results)}')",
384→                null
385→            )
386→            showDeveloperContact(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ankiJsErrorCodeSearchCard, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
387→            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
388→        }
389→        val searchResult: MutableList<String> = ArrayList()
390→        for (s in cards) {
391→            val jsonObject = JSONObject()
392→            val fieldsData = s.card.note(getColUnsafe).fields
393→            val fieldsName = s.card.model(getColUnsafe).fieldsNames
394→
395→            val noteId = s.card.nid
396→            val cardId = s.card.id
397→            jsonObject.put("cardId", cardId)
398→            jsonObject.put("noteId", noteId)
399→
400→            val jsonFieldObject = JSONObject()
401→            fieldsName.zip(fieldsData).forEach { pair ->
402→                jsonFieldObject.put(pair.component1(), pair.component2())
403→            }
404→            jsonObject.put("fieldsData", jsonFieldObject)
405→
406→            searchResult.add(jsonObject.toString())
407→        }
408→
409→        // quote result to prevent JSON injection attack
410→        val jsonEncodedString = JSONObject.quote(searchResult.toString())
411→        activity.runOnUiThread {
412→            activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSearchCard($jsonEncodedString)", null)
413→        }
414→        convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
415→    }
416→

418→        var newCardCount = ""
419→        var lrnCardCount = ""
420→        var revCardCount = ""
421→        var eta = -1
422→        var nextTime1 = ""
423→        var nextTime2 = ""
424→        var nextTime3 = ""
425→        var nextTime4 = ""
426→    }
427→
428→    class ApiResult(private val status: Boolean, private val value: String) {
429→        override fun toString(): String {
430→            return JSONObject().apply {
431→                put("success", status)
432→                put("value", value)
433→            }.toString()
434→        }
435→    }

438→}

The fix is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

### Root Cause
The issue was in the `AnkiDroidJsAPI.kt` file. The `convertToByteArray` methods were converting all values (Int, Long, Boolean) to strings using `.toString()` before passing them to `ApiResult`. The `ApiResult` class then stored these as strings in the JSON response, causing JavaScript to receive strings instead of the proper types.

### Changes Made

**1. AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AnkiDroidJsAPI.kt**

- Modified `convertToByteArray` methods to pass the actual typed values (Boolean, Int, Long, String) directly to `ApiResult` instead of converting them to strings first:
  - `ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, boolean.toString())` → `ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, boolean)`
  - `ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, int.toString())` → `ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, int)`
  - `ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, long.toString())` → `ApiResult(apiContract.isValid, long)`

- Changed `ApiResult` class to accept `Any` type instead of `String` for the value parameter:
  - `class ApiResult(private val status: Boolean, private val value: String)` → `class ApiResult(private val status: Boolean, private val value: Any)`

**2. AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/AnkiDroidJsAPITest.kt**

- Updated the `formatApiResult` helper function to properly format expected results based on type:
  - For String types: wraps the value in quotes (`"value":"somestring"`)
  - For other types (Int, Long, Boolean): uses the value directly without quotes (`"value":123`, `"value":true`)

### Result

After this fix:
- `api.ankiGetCardQueue()` returns `{"success":true,"value":0}` (number) instead of `{"success":true,"value":"0"}` (string)
- `api.ankiCardMark()` returns `{"success":true,"value":true}` (boolean) instead of `{"success":true,"value":"true"}` (string)
- All other API methods that should return Int, Long, or Boolean types now return the correct data types as documented in the AnkiDroid Javascript API wiki

The fix is complete. Let me summarize what was done:

## Summary of the Fix

**Bug**: When clicking on a card row in the Card Browser's multi-select mode, the card would always be added to the selection, never removed. This caused the card to remain selected even after clicking on it again to unselect it.

**Root Cause**: In `CardBrowser.kt`, the `onItemClickListener` was calling `viewModel.selectRowAtPosition(position)` which only adds cards to the selection set - it never removes them. Meanwhile, the checkbox was being visually toggled with `cb.toggle()`, creating a mismatch between the visual state and the actual selection state.

**Fix**: Changed line 547 from:
```kotlin
viewModel.selectRowAtPosition(position)
```
to:
```kotlin
viewModel.toggleRowSelectionAtPosition(position)
```

**Why this works**: 
- `selectRowAtPosition()` only calls `_selectedRows.add(cards[pos])` - it can only add, never remove
- `toggleRowSelectionAtPosition()` checks if the card is already selected and either removes or adds it accordingly

This ensures consistent behavior between:
1. Clicking on the row (now correctly toggles selection)
2. Clicking directly on the checkbox (already used `toggleRowSelectionAtPosition`)

The `selectRowAtPosition` method is kept in the ViewModel as it's still used in test helpers for programmatically selecting rows without toggling.

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.174 (commit: b6ed71fd).

## Issue to Resolve

Feature Request: Implement 'User Action' Functionality Similar to AnkiMobile
In AnkiMobile (starting from version 2.0.88+), there is a feature that allows users to trigger JavaScript in the review screen. This advanced functionality is especially beneficial for those using shared decks that provide keyboard shortcuts to perform actions, such as revealing a hint or the next cloze deletion. By defining a user action, it's possible to trigger these actions with external devices, like a gamepad, or even by using gestures on the screen.

For instance, in AnkiMobile, if a card template provides a revealNext() function, users can instruct AnkiMobile to call it with the following code:

```
var userJs1 = revealNext;
```

This feature is especially handy for those who have written their own actions or are using certain shared decks/templates that provide these actions.

For more details, please refer to the [AnkiMobile documentation](https://docs.ankimobile.net/study-tools.html). Once on the page, scroll down to the "User Action 1-8" section for specifics.

 87→    val preferenceKey: String
 88→        get() = "binding_$name"

 90→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 91→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 92→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 93→            collection.remove(element)
 94→            collection.add(0, element)
 95→            true
 96→        }
 97→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 98→    }
 99→
100→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
101→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
102→            // do not reorder the elements
103→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
104→                return@BiFunction false
105→            }
106→            collection.add(element)
107→            return@BiFunction true
108→        }
109→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
113→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
114→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
115→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
116→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
117→    }
118→
119→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
120→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
121→        bindings.remove(binding)
122→        prefs.edit {
123→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
124→        }
125→    }

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 17→
 18→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 19→import android.view.KeyEvent
 20→import androidx.core.content.edit
 21→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.keyCode
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.unicode
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys.Companion.ctrl
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys.Companion.shift
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.CardSide
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.*
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.fromPreference
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
 32→import java.util.*
 33→import java.util.function.BiFunction
 34→import java.util.stream.Collectors
 35→
 36→/** Abstraction: Discuss moving many of these to 'Reviewer'  */
 37→enum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 38→    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),
 39→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.answer_again),
 40→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2(R.string.answer_hard),
 41→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3(R.string.answer_good),
 42→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4(R.string.answer_easy),
 43→    UNDO(R.string.undo),
 44→    REDO(R.string.redo),
 45→    EDIT(R.string.cardeditor_title_edit_card),
 46→    MARK(R.string.menu_mark_note),
 47→    BURY_CARD(R.string.menu_bury_card),
 48→    SUSPEND_CARD(R.string.menu_suspend_card),
 49→    DELETE(R.string.menu_delete_note),
 50→    PLAY_MEDIA(R.string.gesture_play),
 51→    EXIT(R.string.gesture_abort_learning),
 52→    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note),
 53→    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note),
 54→    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red),
 55→    TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE(R.string.gesture_flag_orange),
 56→    TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN(R.string.gesture_flag_green),
 57→    TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE(R.string.gesture_flag_blue),
 58→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK(R.string.gesture_flag_pink),
 59→    TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE(R.string.gesture_flag_turquoise),
 60→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE(R.string.gesture_flag_purple),
 61→    UNSET_FLAG(R.string.gesture_flag_remove),
 62→    PAGE_UP(R.string.gesture_page_up),
 63→    PAGE_DOWN(R.string.gesture_page_down),
 64→    TAG(R.string.add_tag),
 65→    CARD_INFO(R.string.card_info_title),
 66→    ABORT_AND_SYNC(R.string.gesture_abort_sync),
 67→    RECORD_VOICE(R.string.record_voice),
 68→    SAVE_VOICE(R.string.save_voice),
 69→    REPLAY_VOICE(R.string.replay_voice),
 70→    TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD(R.string.gesture_toggle_whiteboard),
 71→    CLEAR_WHITEBOARD(R.string.clear_whiteboard),
 72→    CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR(R.string.title_whiteboard_editor),
 73→    SHOW_HINT(R.string.gesture_show_hint),
 74→    SHOW_ALL_HINTS(R.string.gesture_show_all_hints),
 75→    ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note),
 76→    RESCHEDULE_NOTE(R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card);
 77→
 78→    companion object {
 79→        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 80→            get() = Arrays.stream(entries.toTypedArray())
 81→                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 82→                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 83→
 84→        fun fromPreferenceKey(key: String) = ViewerCommand.entries.first { it.preferenceKey == key }
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    val preferenceKey: String
 88→        get() = "binding_$name"
 89→
 90→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 91→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 92→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 93→            collection.remove(element)
 94→            collection.add(0, element)
 95→            true
 96→        }
 97→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 98→    }
 99→
100→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
101→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
102→            // do not reorder the elements
103→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
104→                return@BiFunction false
105→            }
106→            collection.add(element)
107→            return@BiFunction true
108→        }
109→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
113→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
114→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
115→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
116→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
117→    }
118→
119→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
120→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
121→        bindings.remove(binding)
122→        prefs.edit {
123→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
124→        }
125→    }
126→
127→    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
128→    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
129→        get() {
130→            // all of the default commands are currently for the Reviewer
131→            fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, modifierKeys: ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys.none()) =
132→                keyCode(keycode, Screen.Reviewer(side), modifierKeys)
133→            fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide) = unicode(c, Screen.Reviewer(side))
134→            return when (this) {

164→    private fun createViewer(): CommandTestCardViewer {

168→    private fun cardWith(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") flag: Int): Card {
169→        val c = mock(Card::class.java)
170→        val flags = intArrayOf(flag)
171→        whenever(c.userFlag()).then { flags[0] }
172→        doAnswer { invocation: InvocationOnMock ->
173→            flags[0] = invocation.getArgument(0)
174→            null
175→        }.whenever(c).setUserFlag(anyInt())
176→        return c
177→    }
178→
179→    private class CommandTestCardViewer(private var currentCardOverride: Card?) : Reviewer() {
180→        var lastFlag = 0
181→            private set
182→
183→        override var currentCard: Card?
184→            get() = currentCardOverride
185→            set(card) {
186→                // we don't have getCol() here and we don't need the additional sound processing.
187→                currentCardOverride = card
188→            }
189→
190→        override fun performReload() {
191→            // intentionally blank
192→        }
193→
194→        override fun onFlag(card: Card?, flag: Flag) {
195→            lastFlag = flag.code
196→            currentCard!!.setUserFlag(flag.code)
197→        }
198→    }
199→}

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 20→import android.view.Menu
 21→import android.view.MenuItem
 22→import androidx.annotation.IdRes
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 24→import com.ichi2.utils.HashUtil.hashMapInit
 25→
 26→// loads of unboxing issues, which are safe
 27→class ActionButtonStatus {
 28→    /**
 29→     * Custom button allocation
 30→     */
 31→    private val customButtons: MutableMap<Int, Int> = hashMapInit(25) // setup's size
 32→
 33→    fun setup(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
 34→        // NOTE: the default values below should be in sync with preferences_custom_buttons.xml and reviewer.xml
 35→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_undo, "customButtonUndo", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 36→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_redo, "customButtonRedo", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 37→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_schedule, "customButtonScheduleCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 38→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_flag, "customButtonFlag", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 39→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_tag, "customButtonTags", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 40→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_edit, "customButtonEditCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 41→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_add_note_reviewer, "customButtonAddCard", MENU_DISABLED)
 42→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_replay, "customButtonReplay", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 43→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_card_info, "customButtonCardInfo", MENU_DISABLED)
 44→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_clear_whiteboard, "customButtonClearWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 45→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_hide_whiteboard, "customButtonShowHideWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 46→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_select_tts, "customButtonSelectTts", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 47→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_open_deck_options, "customButtonDeckOptions", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 48→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_bury, "customButtonBury", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 49→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_bury_card, "customButtonBury", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 50→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_suspend, "customButtonSuspend", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 51→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_suspend_card, "customButtonSuspend", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 52→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_mark_card, "customButtonMarkCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 53→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_delete, "customButtonDelete", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 54→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_mic_tool_bar, "customButtonToggleMicToolBar", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 55→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_whiteboard, "customButtonEnableWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 56→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_stylus, "customButtonToggleStylus", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 57→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_save_whiteboard, "customButtonSaveWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 58→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color, "customButtonWhiteboardPenColor", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    private fun setupButton(preferences: SharedPreferences, @IdRes resourceId: Int, preferenceName: String, showAsActionType: Int) {
 62→        customButtons[resourceId] = preferences.getString(
 63→            preferenceName,
 64→            showAsActionType.toString()
 65→        )!!.toInt()
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    fun setCustomButtons(menu: Menu) {
 69→        for ((itemId, value) in customButtons) {
 70→            if (value != MENU_DISABLED) {
 71→                val item = menu.findItem(itemId)
 72→                item.setShowAsAction(value)
 73→            } else {
 74→                menu.findItem(itemId).isVisible = false
 75→            }
 76→        }
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    fun hideWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 80→        return customButtons[R.id.action_hide_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    fun toggleStylusIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 84→        return customButtons[R.id.action_toggle_stylus] == MENU_DISABLED
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    fun clearWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 88→        return customButtons[R.id.action_clear_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    fun selectTtsIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 92→        return customButtons[R.id.action_select_tts] == MENU_DISABLED
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun saveWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 96→        return customButtons[R.id.action_save_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 97→    }
 98→
 99→    fun whiteboardPenColorIsDisabled(): Boolean {
100→        return customButtons[R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color] == MENU_DISABLED
101→    }
102→
103→    fun suspendIsDisabled(): Boolean = customButtons[R.id.action_suspend] == MENU_DISABLED
104→
105→    fun buryIsDisabled(): Boolean = customButtons[R.id.action_bury] == MENU_DISABLED
106→
107→    companion object {
108→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER
109→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM
110→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS
111→        const val MENU_DISABLED = 3
112→    }
113→}

[thinking: All the changes are complete. Let me create a quick summary of what was implemented:## Summary of Changes1. **ViewerCommand.kt**: Added 9 new enum entries for USER_ACTION_1 through USER_ACTION_9, each...]
I have successfully implemented the User Action functionality. Let me provide a summary of all the changes made:

## Summary of Implementation

### Files Modified:

1. **ViewerCommand.kt** - Added 9 new enum values for USER_ACTION_1 through USER_ACTION_9

2. **AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt** - Added command handlers that execute JavaScript: `userAction(1)` through `userAction(9)`

3. **card.js** - Added the `userAction(actionNumber)` JavaScript function that:
   - Looks for user-defined variables like `userJs1`, `userJs2`, etc. in the card template
   - If found and it's a function, calls it

4. **UsageAnalytics.kt** - Added tracking for the new binding keys and custom button keys

5. **preferences_controls.xml** - Simplified to use regular `PreferenceCategory` (the referenced `ExtendedPreferenceCategory` didn't exist)

6. **reviewer.xml** - Updated user action menu items with proper icon (`ic_code`) and visibility settings

7. **ActionButtonStatus.kt** - Added setup for user action app bar buttons (disabled by default)

8. **Reviewer.kt** - Added menu item click handlers for user actions

9. **ViewerCommandTest.kt** - Updated test expectations to include the new binding keys

### How Users Can Use This Feature:

1. **In card templates**, define JavaScript variables:
   ```javascript
   var userJs1 = revealNext;  // reference to existing function
   var userJs2 = function() { /* custom code */ };
   ```

2. **In AnkiDroid Settings → Controls → User Actions**, bind gestures, keyboard shortcuts, or gamepad buttons to User Action 1-9

3. **Optionally**, enable User Action buttons in the app bar via Settings → Appearance → App bar buttons

When triggered, AnkiDroid will call the corresponding user-defined JavaScript function, exactly like AnkiMobile's User Action 1-8 feature (but with 9 actions available).

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.16 (commit: a8117a0a).

## Issue to Resolve

Request to make "Reschedule" as one of the command mapping controller button options
     Is it possible to make "Reschedule" as one of the command mapping controller button options? I would like to be able to reschedule a card to a different date as needed with a touch of a gesture or button on a game controller without having to tap multiple times to reach the "Reschedule for review in x days"  option for a specific card. 
     Simply put, I would like to be able to do a certain gesture or click a single button to reach the "Reschedule" option like we can do for "Bury card", "Undo", etc. Please let me know if there's already a way to do this! Thank you all so much!!

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Yes, it is possible. Pretty valid feature request
Oh, that's awesome! Thank you so much!
Hi, I would like to work on this feature request! And if I can work, is the way to solve it is just segregate the 2 options which are in reschedule tab to the main tab?
Hello @parthn2. We appreciate the work.

For now, there is no need to do a command for `Reset progress`. Just the `Reschedule` command asked by the OP is enough
> Hello @parthn2. We appreciate the work.
> 
> For now, there is no need to do a command for `Reset progress`. Just the `Reschedule` command asked by the OP is enough

Okay!! But wouldn't it just make it useless to go 2 clicks for 'Reset' because no one will be using 2 clicks for 'Reschedule' because it is just one click away. So why not break that into 2 if it is the correct thing?
> because no one will be using 2 clicks for 'Reschedule' because it is just one click away.

Not everybody is going to add a gesture for Reschedule, so it may be still be a 2 taps action.

- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommand.kt`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:

 76→    companion object {

 78→            get() = Arrays.stream(values())

 80→                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    val preferenceKey: String
 84→        get() = "binding_$name"
 85→
 86→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 87→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 88→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 89→            collection.remove(element)
 90→            collection.add(0, element)
 91→            true
 92→        }
 93→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 97→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 98→            // do not reorder the elements
 99→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
100→                return@BiFunction false
101→            }
102→            collection.add(element)
103→            return@BiFunction true
104→        }
105→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
106→    }
107→
108→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
109→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
110→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
111→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
112→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
113→    }
114→
115→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
116→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
117→        bindings.remove(binding)
118→        prefs.edit {
119→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
120→        }
121→    }

126→            when (this) {

132→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> from(

134→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
135→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
136→                )
137→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> from(

139→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
140→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER)
141→                )
142→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> from(

144→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
145→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
146→                )
147→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_RECOMMENDED -> from(
148→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
149→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
150→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
151→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
152→                )
153→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
154→                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
155→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
156→                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
157→                SUSPEND_CARD -> from(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
158→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> from(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
159→                PLAY_MEDIA -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
160→                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
161→                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
162→                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
163→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
164→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
165→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
166→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
167→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
168→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
169→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
170→                SHOW_HINT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
171→                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
172→                ADD_NOTE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
173→                else -> ArrayList()

176→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
177→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
178→    }

180→    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {

182→    }
183→
184→    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {

 146→    protected fun <T : Computation<NextCard<Array<Card>>>?> scheduleCollectionTaskHandler(@PluralsRes toastResourceId: Int): TaskListenerBuilder<Unit, T> {
 147→        return nextCardHandler<Computation<NextCard<*>>?>().alsoExecuteAfter { result: T ->
 148→            // BUG: If the method crashes, this will crash
 149→            invalidateOptionsMenu()
 150→            val cardCount: Int = result!!.value.result.size
 151→            showSnackbar(
 152→                resources.getQuantityString(toastResourceId, cardCount, cardCount),
 153→                Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT
 154→            )
 155→        }
 156→    }

 244→    protected open fun onFlag(card: Card?, flag: Int) {
 245→        if (card == null) {
 246→            return
 247→        }
 248→        launchCatchingTask {
 249→            card.setUserFlag(flag)
 250→            if (BackendFactory.defaultLegacySchema) {
 251→                card.flush()
 252→                /* Following code would allow to update value of {{cardFlag}}.
 253→               Anki does not update this value when a flag is changed, so
 254→               currently this code would do something that anki itself
 255→               does not do. I hope in the future Anki will correct that
 256→               and this code may becomes useful.
 257→
 258→            card._getQA(true); //force reload. Useful iff {{cardFlag}} occurs in the template
 259→            if (sDisplayAnswer) {
 260→                displayCardAnswer();
 261→            } else {
 262→                displayCardQuestion();
 263→                } */
 264→            } else {
 265→                withCol {
 266→                    newBackend.setUserFlagForCards(listOf(card.id), flag)
 267→                }
 268→            }
 269→            refreshActionBar()
 270→            onFlagChanged()
 271→        }
 272→    }

 281→    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
 282→        val extras = intent.extras
 283→        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
 284→            // deckId is not set, load default
 285→            return
 286→        }
 287→        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
 288→        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
 289→
 290→        // deckId does not exist, load default
 291→        if (col.decks.get(did, _default = false) == null) {
 292→            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
 293→            return
 294→        }
 295→
 296→        // Clear the undo history when selecting a new deck
 297→        if (col.decks.selected() != did) {
 298→            col.clearUndo()
 299→        }
 300→        // Select the deck
 301→        col.decks.select(did)
 302→        // Reset the schedule so that we get the counts for the currently selected deck
 303→        col.sched.deferReset()
 304→    }

 318→    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
 319→        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
 320→        if (Intent.ACTION_VIEW == intent.action) {
 321→            Timber.d("onCreate() :: received Intent with action = %s", intent.action)
 322→            selectDeckFromExtra()
 323→        }
 324→        // Load the first card and start reviewing. Uses the answer card
 325→        // task to load a card, but since we send null
 326→        // as the card to answer, no card will be answered.
 327→        prefWhiteboard = MetaDB.getWhiteboardState(this, parentDid)
 328→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 329→            // DEFECT: Slight inefficiency here, as we set the database using these methods
 330→            val whiteboardVisibility = MetaDB.getWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid)
 331→            setWhiteboardEnabledState(true)
 332→            setWhiteboardVisibility(whiteboardVisibility)
 333→            toggleStylus = MetaDB.getWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid)
 334→            whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus
 335→        }
 336→        col.sched.deferReset() // Reset schedule in case card was previously loaded
 337→        col.startTimebox()
 338→        GetCard().runWithHandler(answerCardHandler(false))
 339→        disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts()
 340→        // Add a weak reference to current activity so that scheduler can talk to to Activity
 341→        sched!!.setContext(WeakReference(this))
 342→
 343→        // Set full screen/immersive mode if needed
 344→        if (mPrefFullscreenReview) {
 345→            setFullScreen(this)
 346→        }
 347→        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 348→    }

 601→    private fun openOrToggleMicToolbar() {
 602→        if (!canRecordAudio(this)) {
 603→            ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(
 604→                this,
 605→                arrayOf(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO),
 606→                REQUEST_AUDIO_PERMISSION
 607→            )
 608→        } else {
 609→            toggleMicToolBar()
 610→        }
 611→    }

 613→    private fun toggleMicToolBar() {
 614→        audioView?.let {
 615→            it.visibility = if (it.visibility != View.VISIBLE) View.VISIBLE else View.GONE
 616→            return
 617→        }
 618→        // Record mic tool bar does not exist yet
 619→        tempAudioPath = generateTempAudioFile(this)
 620→        if (tempAudioPath == null) {
 621→            return
 622→        }
 623→        audioView = createRecorderInstance(
 624→            this,
 625→            R.drawable.ic_play_arrow_white_24dp,
 626→            R.drawable.ic_pause_white_24dp,
 627→            R.drawable.ic_stop_white_24dp,
 628→            R.drawable.ic_rec,
 629→            R.drawable.ic_rec_stop,
 630→            tempAudioPath!!
 631→        )
 632→        if (audioView == null) {
 633→            tempAudioPath = null
 634→            return
 635→        }
 636→        val lp2 = FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
 637→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
 638→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
 639→        )
 640→        audioView!!.layoutParams = lp2
 641→        val micToolBarLayer = findViewById<LinearLayout>(R.id.mic_tool_bar_layer)
 642→        micToolBarLayer.addView(audioView)
 643→    }

 708→    // Related to https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/11061#issuecomment-1107868455
 709→    @NeedsTest("Order of operations needs Testing around Menu (Overflow) Icons and their colors.")
 710→    override fun onCreateOptionsMenu(menu: Menu): Boolean {
 711→        Timber.d("onCreateOptionsMenu()")
 712→        // NOTE: This is called every time a new question is shown via invalidate options menu
 713→        menuInflater.inflate(R.menu.reviewer, menu)
 714→        displayIcons(menu)
 715→        mActionButtons.setCustomButtonsStatus(menu)
 716→        var alpha = if (super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 717→        val markCardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_mark_card)
 718→        if (currentCard != null && isMarked(currentCard!!.note())) {
 719→            markCardIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_unmark_note).setIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_white)
 720→        } else {
 721→            markCardIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_mark_note).setIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_border_white)
 722→        }
 723→        markCardIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 724→
 725→        val flagIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_flag)
 726→        if (flagIcon != null) {
 727→            if (currentCard != null) {
 728→                when (currentCard!!.userFlag()) {
 729→                    1 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_red)
 730→                    2 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_orange)
 731→                    3 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_green)
 732→                    4 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_blue)
 733→                    5 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_pink)
 734→                    6 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_turquoise)
 735→                    7 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_purple)
 736→                    else -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_transparent)
 737→                }

 742→        // Undo button
 743→        @DrawableRes val undoIconId: Int

 747→            undoIconId = R.drawable.eraser

 749→        } else {

 753→        val alphaUndo = if (undoEnabled && super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 754→        val undoIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_undo)
 755→        undoIcon.setIcon(undoIconId)
 756→        undoIcon.setEnabled(undoEnabled).iconAlpha = alphaUndo
 757→        undoIcon.actionView!!.isEnabled = undoEnabled
 758→        if (colIsOpen()) { // Required mostly because there are tests where `col` is null
 759→            if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {

 763→                //  e.g. Undo Bury, Undo Change Deck, Undo Update Note
 764→            } else {
 765→                // In this case, there is no object word for the verb, "Undo",
 766→                // so in some languages such as Japanese, which have pre/post-positional particle with the object,

 917→    override fun displayAnswerBottomBar() {
 918→        super.displayAnswerBottomBar()
 919→        mOnboarding.onAnswerShown()
 920→        val buttonCount: Int = try {
 921→            this.buttonCount
 922→        } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
 923→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport(e, "AbstractReviewer-showEaseButtons")
 924→            closeReviewer(DeckPicker.RESULT_DB_ERROR, true)
 925→            return
 926→        }
 927→
 928→        // Set correct label and background resource for each button
 929→        // Note that it's necessary to set the resource dynamically as the ease2 / ease3 buttons
 930→        // (which libanki expects ease to be 2 and 3) can either be hard, good, or easy - depending on num buttons shown
 931→        val background = getBackgroundColors(this)
 932→        val textColor = getTextColors(this)
 933→        easeButton1!!.setVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
 934→        easeButton1!!.setColor(background[0])
 935→        easeButton4!!.setColor(background[3])
 936→        when (buttonCount) {
 937→            2 -> {
 938→                // Ease 2 is "good"
 939→                easeButton2!!.setup(background[2], textColor[2], R.string.ease_button_good)
 940→                easeButton2!!.requestFocus()
 941→            }
 942→            3 -> {
 943→                // Ease 2 is good
 944→                easeButton2!!.setup(background[2], textColor[2], R.string.ease_button_good)
 945→                // Ease 3 is easy
 946→                easeButton3!!.setup(background[3], textColor[3], R.string.ease_button_easy)
 947→                easeButton2!!.requestFocus()
 948→            }
 949→            else -> {
 950→                // Ease 2 is "hard"
 951→                easeButton2!!.setup(background[1], textColor[1], R.string.ease_button_hard)
 952→                easeButton2!!.requestFocus()
 953→                // Ease 3 is good
 954→                easeButton3!!.setup(background[2], textColor[2], R.string.ease_button_good)
 955→                easeButton4!!.setVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
 956→                easeButton3!!.requestFocus()
 957→            }
 958→        }
 959→
 960→        // Show next review time
 961→        if (shouldShowNextReviewTime()) {
 962→            fun nextIvlStr(button: Int) = sched!!.nextIvlStr(this, currentCard!!, button)
 963→
 964→            easeButton1!!.nextTime = nextIvlStr(Consts.BUTTON_ONE)
 965→            easeButton2!!.nextTime = nextIvlStr(Consts.BUTTON_TWO)
 966→            if (buttonCount > 2) {
 967→                easeButton3!!.nextTime = nextIvlStr(Consts.BUTTON_THREE)
 968→            }
 969→            if (buttonCount > 3) {
 970→                easeButton4!!.nextTime = nextIvlStr(Consts.BUTTON_FOUR)
 971→            }
 972→        }
 973→    }

1021→    private fun updateScreenCounts() {
1022→        if (currentCard == null) return
1023→        super.updateActionBar()
1024→        val actionBar = supportActionBar
1025→        val counts = sched!!.counts(currentCard!!)
1026→        if (actionBar != null) {
1027→            if (mPrefShowETA) {
1028→                mEta = sched!!.eta(counts, false)
1029→                actionBar.subtitle = Utils.remainingTime(this, (mEta * 60).toLong())
1030→            }
1031→        }
1032→        mNewCount = SpannableString(counts.new.toString())
1033→        mLrnCount = SpannableString(counts.lrn.toString())
1034→        mRevCount = SpannableString(counts.rev.toString())
1035→        if (mPrefHideDueCount) {
1036→            mRevCount = SpannableString("???")
1037→        }
1038→        // if this code is run as a card is being answered, currentCard may be non-null but
1039→        // the queues may be empty - we can't call countIdx() in such a case
1040→        if (counts.count() != 0) {
1041→            when (sched!!.countIdx(currentCard!!)) {
1042→                Counts.Queue.NEW -> mNewCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mNewCount!!.length, 0)
1043→                Counts.Queue.LRN -> mLrnCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mLrnCount!!.length, 0)
1044→                Counts.Queue.REV -> mRevCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mRevCount!!.length, 0)
1045→            }
1046→        }
1047→        mTextBarNew.text = mNewCount
1048→        mTextBarLearn.text = mLrnCount
1049→        mTextBarReview.text = mRevCount
1050→    }

1122→    override fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean {
1123→        if (isControlBlocked && which !== ViewerCommand.EXIT) {
1124→            return false
1125→        }
1126→        when (which) {
1127→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> {
1128→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_RED)
1129→                return true
1130→            }
1131→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> {
1132→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_ORANGE)
1133→                return true
1134→            }
1135→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> {
1136→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_GREEN)
1137→                return true
1138→            }
1139→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> {
1140→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_BLUE)
1141→                return true
1142→            }
1143→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> {
1144→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_PINK)
1145→                return true
1146→            }
1147→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> {
1148→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_TURQUOISE)
1149→                return true
1150→            }
1151→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> {
1152→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_PURPLE)
1153→                return true
1154→            }
1155→            ViewerCommand.UNSET_FLAG -> {
1156→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_NONE)
1157→                return true
1158→            }
1159→            ViewerCommand.MARK -> {
1160→                onMark(currentCard)
1161→                return true
1162→            }
1163→            ViewerCommand.ADD_NOTE -> {
1164→                addNote(fromGesture)
1165→                return true
1166→            }
1167→            ViewerCommand.CARD_INFO -> {
1168→                openCardInfo(fromGesture)
1169→                return true
1170→            }
1171→            else -> return super.executeCommand(which, fromGesture)
1172→        }
1173→    }

1239→    @Suppress("deprecation") // #9332: UI Visibility -> Insets
1240→    private fun setFullScreen(a: AbstractFlashcardViewer) {
1241→        // Set appropriate flags to enable Sticky Immersive mode.
1242→        a.window.decorView.systemUiVisibility = (
1243→            View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_STABLE // | View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_HIDE_NAVIGATION // temporarily disabled due to #5245
1244→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_FULLSCREEN
1245→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION
1246→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_FULLSCREEN
1247→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LOW_PROFILE
1248→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_IMMERSIVE
1249→            )
1250→        // Show / hide the Action bar together with the status bar
1251→        val prefs = a.sharedPrefs()
1252→        val fullscreenMode = fromPreference(prefs)
1253→        a.window.statusBarColor = getColorFromAttr(a, android.R.attr.colorPrimary)
1254→        val decorView = a.window.decorView
1255→        decorView.setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener { flags: Int ->
1256→            val toolbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.toolbar)
1257→            val answerButtons = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.answer_options_layout)
1258→            val topbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.top_bar)
1259→            if (toolbar == null || topbar == null || answerButtons == null) {
1260→                return@setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener
1261→            }
1262→            // Note that system bars will only be "visible" if none of the
1263→            // LOW_PROFILE, HIDE_NAVIGATION, or FULLSCREEN flags are set.
1264→            val visible = flags and View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION == 0
1265→            Timber.d("System UI visibility change. Visible: %b", visible)
1266→            if (visible) {
1267→                showViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1268→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1269→                    showViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1270→                    showViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1271→                }
1272→            } else {
1273→                hideViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1274→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1275→                    hideViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1276→                    hideViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1277→                }
1278→            }
1279→        }
1280→    }
1281→
1282→    private fun showViewWithAnimation(view: View) {
1283→        view.alpha = 0.0f
1284→        view.visibility = View.VISIBLE
1285→        view.animate().alpha(TRANSPARENCY).setDuration(ANIMATION_DURATION.toLong()).setListener(null)
1286→    }
1287→
1288→    private fun hideViewWithAnimation(view: View) {
1289→        view.animate()
1290→            .alpha(0f)
1291→            .setDuration(ANIMATION_DURATION.toLong())
1292→            .setListener(object : AnimatorListenerAdapter() {
1293→                override fun onAnimationEnd(animation: Animator) {
1294→                    view.visibility = View.GONE
1295→                }
1296→            })
1297→    }

1299→    @Suppress("deprecation") // #9332: UI Visibility -> Insets
1300→    private fun isImmersiveSystemUiVisible(activity: AnkiActivity): Boolean {
1301→        return activity.window.decorView.systemUiVisibility and View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION == 0
1302→    }
1303→
1304→    private fun createWhiteboard() {
1305→        whiteboard = createInstance(this, true, this)
1306→
1307→        // We use the pen color of the selected deck at the time the whiteboard is enabled.
1308→        // This is how all other whiteboard settings are
1309→        val whiteboardPenColor = MetaDB.getWhiteboardPenColor(this, parentDid).fromPreferences()
1310→        if (whiteboardPenColor != null) {
1311→            whiteboard!!.penColor = whiteboardPenColor
1312→        }
1313→        whiteboard!!.setOnPaintColorChangeListener(object : OnPaintColorChangeListener {
1314→            override fun onPaintColorChange(color: Int?) {
1315→                MetaDB.storeWhiteboardPenColor(this@Reviewer, parentDid, !currentTheme.isNightMode, color)
1316→            }
1317→        })
1318→        whiteboard!!.setOnTouchListener { v: View, event: MotionEvent? ->
1319→            if (event == null) return@setOnTouchListener false
1320→            // If the whiteboard is currently drawing, and triggers the system UI to show, we want to continue drawing.
1321→            if (!whiteboard!!.isCurrentlyDrawing && (
1322→                !mShowWhiteboard || (
1323→                    mPrefFullscreenReview &&
1324→                        isImmersiveSystemUiVisible(this@Reviewer)
1325→                    )
1326→                )
1327→            ) {
1328→                // Bypass whiteboard listener when it's hidden or fullscreen immersive mode is temporarily suspended
1329→                v.performClick()
1330→                return@setOnTouchListener gestureDetector!!.onTouchEvent(event)
1331→            }
1332→            whiteboard!!.handleTouchEvent(event)
1333→        }
1334→    }
1335→
1336→    // Show or hide the whiteboard
1337→    private fun setWhiteboardVisibility(state: Boolean) {
1338→        mShowWhiteboard = state
1339→        MetaDB.storeWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid, state)
1340→        if (state) {
1341→            whiteboard!!.visibility = View.VISIBLE
1342→            disableDrawerSwipe()
1343→        } else {
1344→            whiteboard!!.visibility = View.GONE
1345→            if (!mHasDrawerSwipeConflicts) {
1346→                enableDrawerSwipe()
1347→            }
1348→        }
1349→    }
1350→
1351→    private fun disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts() {
1352→        if (mGestureProcessor.isBound(Gesture.SWIPE_UP, Gesture.SWIPE_DOWN, Gesture.SWIPE_RIGHT)) {
1353→            mHasDrawerSwipeConflicts = true
1354→            super.disableDrawerSwipe()
1355→        }
1356→    }

1373→     * @return true if there is another card of same note that could be dismissed
1374→     */
1375→    @KotlinCleanup("mCurrentCard handling")
1376→    private fun suspendNoteAvailable(): Boolean {
1377→        return if (currentCard == null || isControlBlocked) {
1378→            false
1379→        } else {
1380→            col.db.queryScalar(
1381→                "select 1 from cards where nid = ? and id != ? and queue != " + Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_SUSPENDED + " limit 1",
1382→                currentCard!!.nid,
1383→                currentCard!!.id
1384→            ) == 1
1385→        }
1386→        // whether there exists a sibling not buried.
1387→    }

1389→    @KotlinCleanup("mCurrentCard handling")
1390→    private fun buryNoteAvailable(): Boolean {

1392→            false
1393→        } else {
1394→            col.db.queryScalar(
1395→                "select 1 from cards where nid = ? and id != ? and queue >=  " + Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_NEW + " limit 1",
1396→                currentCard!!.nid,
1397→                currentCard!!.id
1398→            ) == 1
1399→        }
1400→        // Whether there exists a sibling which is neither suspended nor buried
1401→    }

1482→    private fun createActionViewWith(
1483→        context: Context,
1484→        menuItem: MenuItem,
1485→        @MenuRes subMenuRes: Int,
1486→        onMenuItemSelection: (MenuItem) -> Boolean,
1487→        showsSubMenu: () -> Boolean
1488→    ): View = ImageButton(context, null, android.R.attr.actionButtonStyle).apply {
1489→        TooltipCompat.setTooltipText(this, menuItem.title)
1490→        menuItem.icon?.isAutoMirrored = true
1491→        setImageDrawable(menuItem.icon)
1492→        id = menuItem.itemId
1493→        setOnClickListener {
1494→            if (!menuItem.isEnabled) {
1495→                return@setOnClickListener
1496→            }
1497→            if (showsSubMenu()) {
1498→                PopupMenu(context, this).apply {
1499→                    inflate(subMenuRes)
1500→                    setOnMenuItemClickListener(onMenuItemSelection)
1501→                    show()
1502→                }
1503→            } else {
1504→                onOptionsItemSelected(menuItem)
1505→            }
1506→        }
1507→    }

1602→                return false

1620→                return false

16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences

18→import androidx.preference.PreferenceCategory
19→import com.ichi2.anki.R

23→
24→class ControlsSettingsFragment : SettingsFragment() {
25→    override val preferenceResource: Int
26→        get() = R.xml.preferences_controls
27→    override val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
28→        get() = "prefs.controls"
29→
30→    override fun initSubscreen() {
31→        val commandMappingCategory = requirePreference<PreferenceCategory>(R.string.controls_command_mapping_cat_key)
32→        addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(commandMappingCategory)
33→    }
34→
35→    /** Attaches all possible [ControlPreference] elements to a given [PreferenceCategory] */
36→    fun addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(category: PreferenceCategory) {
37→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
38→            val preference = ControlPreference(category.context).apply {
39→                setTitle(command.resourceId)
40→                key = command.preferenceKey
41→                setDefaultValue(command.defaultValue.toPreferenceString())
42→            }
43→            category.addPreference(preference)
44→        }
45→    }
46→}

The implementation is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

I've implemented the feature request to add "Reschedule" as a command mapping controller button option. Here's what was changed:

### 1. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommand.kt`
Added a new enum value `RESCHEDULE` with the existing string resource `R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card` (which displays as "Reschedule"):

```kotlin
ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note),
RESCHEDULE(R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card);  // NEW
```

### 2. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt`
Added handling for the new `RESCHEDULE` command in the `executeCommand()` method:

```kotlin
ViewerCommand.RESCHEDULE -> {
    showRescheduleCardDialog()
    return true
}
```

This calls the existing `showRescheduleCardDialog()` method which opens the reschedule dialog for the current card.

### 3. `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommandTest.kt`
Updated the test to include the new `binding_RESCHEDULE` preference key in the expected list of preference keys.

## How It Works

With these changes:
1. Users can now navigate to **Settings → Controls → Command mapping** and see "Reschedule" as one of the available actions
2. They can map any gesture (swipe up/down/left/right, tap, double tap, long press) or controller button to the "Reschedule" action
3. When the mapped gesture/button is triggered during card review, the "Reschedule for review in x days" dialog opens directly - no more need to tap through multiple menus

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.171 (commit: a4cbca04).

## Issue to Resolve

Remove `Answer recommended (green)` and `Answer better than recomended` commands
I asked myself what those did, so I peeked at the code to discover that they are the exact same thing of calling `Answer button 3` and `Answer button 4`, respectively. So they are actually useless.

For reference, AnkiMobile doesn't have those options and instead of `Answer button [x]`, it is phrased as `Answer [again/easy/good/hard]` which IMO is clearer

---
so the task here is:
1. Remove the `Answer recommended (green)` and `Answer better than recomended` commands
2. Move their default commands to `Answer button 3` and `Answer button 4` respectively
3. Upgrade their preference assigned commands to `Answer button 3` and `Answer button 4` respectively
